control 17 18 eco driving familiarisation eco driving eco driving is a range of everyday practice that allow the motorist to optimise their fuel consumption and co emission optimise the use of your gearbox with a manual gearbox move off gently and change up without waiting during acceleration change up early the gear shift indicator invite you engage the most suitable gear as soon as the indication is displayed in the instrument panel follow it straight away with an electronic gearbox do not press the accelerator pedal heavily or suddenly drive smoothly maintain a safe distance between vehicle use engine braking rather than the brake pedal and press the accelerator progressively these practice contribute toward a reduction in fuel consumption and co emission and also help reduce the background traffic noise if your vehicle has cruise control make use of the system at speed above 25 mph 40 km h when the traffic is flowing well control the use of your electrical equipment before moving off if the passenger compartment is too warm ventilate it by opening the window and air vent before using the air conditioning above 30 mph 50 km h close the window and leave the air vent open remember to make use of equipment that can help keep the temperature in the passenger compartment down sunroof and window blind to save energy do not set the air conditioning at too low a temperature as soon as the desired temperature has been reached limit the use of the air conditioning except if regulation is automatic switch off the demisting and defrosting control as soon as you consider possible switch off the heated seat as soon as possible switch off the headlamp and foglamp when the level of light doe not require their use avoid running the engine before moving off particularly in winter your vehicle will warm up much faster while driving as a passenger if you avoid connecting your multimedia device film music video game you will contribute toward limiting the consumption of electrical energy and so of fuel disconnect your portable device before leaving the vehicle limit the cause of excess consumption spread load throughout the vehicle place the heaviest item in the bottom of the boot as close as possible to the rear seat limit the load carried in the vehicle and reduce wind resistance roof bar roof rack bicycle carrier trailer use a roof box in preference remove roof bar and roof rack after use at the end of winter remove snow tyre and refit your summer tyre familiarisation a observe the recommendation on maintenance check the tyre pressure regularly when cold referring to the label in the door aperture driver s side carry out this check in particular before a long journey at each change of season after along period out of use don t forget the spare wheel and the tyre on any trailer or caravan have your vehicle serviced regularly engine oil oil filter air filter passenger compartment filter and observe the schedule of operation recommended in the maintenance and warranty guide when refuelling do not continue after the third cut off of the nozzle to avoid any overflow at the wheel of your new vehicle it is only after the first 1 800 mile 3 000 kilometre that you will see the fuel consumption settle down to a consistent average 19 monitoring instrument panel ses e o m w woe ar use on pa sa eo ss a a a oe nigh noc 7 conditioning in order for these system to be fully effective follow the operation and maintenance guideline below i se to obtain an even air distribution take care not to obstruct the exterior air intake grille located at the base of the windscreen the nozzle the vent and the air outlet as well as the air extractor located in the boot do not cover the sunshine sensor located on the dashboard this is used for regulation of the air conditioning system operate the air conditioning system for at least 5 to 10 minute once or twice a month to keep it in perfect working order ensure that the passenger compartment filter is in good condition and have the filter element replaced regularly we recommend the use of a combined passenger compartment filter thank to its special active additive it contribute to the purification of the air breathed by the occupant and the cleanliness of the passenger compartment reduction of allergic symptom bad odour and greasy deposit to ensure correct operation of the air conditioning system you are also advised to have it checked regularly as recommended in the maintenance and warranty guide if the system doe not produce cold air switch it off and contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop when towing the maximum load on a steep gradient in high temperature switching off the air conditioning increase the available engine power and so improve the towing ability ee e if after an extended stop in sunshine the interior temperature is very high first ventilate the passenger compartment for a few moment put the air flow control at a setting high enough to quickly change the air in the passenger compartment the air conditioning system doe not contain chlorine and doe not present any danger to the ozone layer the condensation created by the air conditioning result in a discharge of water under the vehicle which is perfectly normal stop start the heating and air conditioning system only work when the engine is running to parking sensor the system is switched on by engaging reverse gear this is accompanied by an audible signal the system is switched off when you come out of reverse gear audible assistance the proximity information is given by an intermittent audible signal the frequency of which increase as the vehicle approach the obstacle the sound emitted by the speaker right or left indicate the side on which the obstacle is located when the distance between the vehicle and the obstacle become less than approximately thirty centimetre the audible signal become continuous driving visual assistance this supplement the audible signal by displaying bar in the screen or the instrument panel which move progressively nearer to the vehicle when the obstacle is near the danger symbol is displayed 105 driving front parking sensor in addition to the rear parking sensor the front parking sensor are triggered when an obstacle is detected in front and the speed of the vehicle is still below 6 mph 10 km h the front parking sensor are interrupted if the vehicle stop for more than three second in forward gear if no further obstacle are detected or when the speed of the vehicle exceed 6 mph 10 km h the or assistance call to the emergency service or audible warning system to alert other road to the dedicated citroen service user to an imminent danger ze press the central part of the steering wheel pedestrian approaching a location lacking visibility 130 tyre under inflation detection system which automatically check the pressure of the tyre while driving the system monitor the pressure on the four tyre once the vehicle is moving it compare the information given by the four wheel speed sensor with reference value which must be reinitialised every time the tyre pressure are adjusted or a wheel changed the system trigger an alert as soon as it detect a drop in the inflation pressure of one or more tyre the under inflation detection system doe not replace the vigilance or the responsibility of the driver this system doe not avoid the need to check the tyre pressure including the spare wheel every month as well as before a long journey driving with under inflated tyre impair road holding extend braking distance and cause premature tyre wear particularly under arduous condition high loading high speed long journey driving with under inflated tyre increase fuel consumption safety the inflation pressure defined for your vehicle can be found on the tyre pressure label see the identification marking section tyre pressure should be checked when the tyre are cold vehicle stopped for 1 hour or after a journey of less than 6 mile 10 km at moderate speed otherwise when hot add 0 3 bar to the pressure shown on the label 7 131 safety under inflation alert this is given by fixed illumination of ifyou have a compressor such as the one this warning lamp accompanied by in the temporary puncture repair kit check an audible signal and the display of a the pressure of the four tyre when cold message if it is not possible to carry out this check straight away drive carefully at reduced ze reduce speed immediately avoid speed excessive steering movement and sudden or braking inthe event of a puncture use the stop the vehicle as soon as it is safe to do temporary puncture repair kit or the spare so wheel depending on equipment f the loss of pressure detected doe not alway cause visible bulging of the tyre do not satisfy yourself with just a visual the alert is maintained until the system check is reinitialised 132 reinitialisation it is necessary to reinitialise the system every time one or more tyre pressure is adjusted and after changing one or more wheel a label attached to the middle pillar driver s side give a reminder of this before reinitialising the system ensure that the pressure of the four tyre are correct for the use of the vehicle and in line with the recommendation on the tyre pressure label the under inflation detection system doe not give a warning if a pressure is incorrect at the time of reinitialisation reinitialisation of the system is done in the the touch screen tablet with the ignition on and the vehicle stationary e inthe secondary page press l under inflation initialisation select the driving assistance menu confirm the instruction by pressing yes an audible signal and a message confirm the reinitialisation the new pressure setting registered are considered to be reference value by the system snow chain the system doe not have to be reinitialised after fitting or removing snow chain the under inflation alert can only be considered reliable if reinitialisation of the system is done with the the four tyre pressure correctly set safety operating fault 1 service the flashing and then fixed illumination of the under inflation warning lamp accompanied by illumination of the service warning lamp indicate a fault with the system in this case under inflation monitoring of the tyre is no longer assured have the system checked by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop before doing anything to the system the pressure of the four tyre must be checked and the system reinitialised 133 134 safety electronic stability control esc electronic stability control esc incorporating the following system the anti lock braking system abs and the electronic brake force distribution ebfd the emergency braking assistance the anti slip regulation asr or traction control the dynamic stability control dsc definition anti lock braking system abs and electronic brake force distribution ebfd this system improve the stability and manoeuvrability of your vehicle when braking and provide improved control in corner in particular on poor or slippery road surface the abs prevent wheel lock in the event of emergency braking the electronic brake force distribution system manage the braking pressure wheel by wheel emergency braking assistance in an emergency this system enable you to reach the optimum braking pressure more quickly and therefore reduce the stopping distance it is triggered according to the speed at which the brake pedal is pressed this is felt by a reduction in the resistance of the pedal and an increase in the effectiveness of the braking anti slip regulation asr the asr system also known as traction control optimise traction in order to limit wheel slip by acting on the brake of the driving wheel and on the engine it also improve the directional stability of the vehicle on acceleration dynamic stability control dsc if there is a difference between the path followed by the vehicle and that required by the driver the dsc monitor each wheel and automatically act on the brake of one or more wheel and on the engine to return the vehicle to the required path within the limit of the law of physic operation anti lock braking system abs and electronic brake force distribution ebfd when this warning lamp come on accompanied by an audible signal and a message it indicate that there is a fault with the abs which could cause loss of control of the vehicle when braking when this warning lamp come on o coupled with the stop warning lamp accompanied by an audible signal and a message it indicate that there is a fault with the electronic brake force distribution ebfd which could cause loss of control of the vehicle when braking you must stop as soon as it is safe to do so in both case contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop be o when changing wheel tyre and rim make sure that these are approved for your vehicle normal operation of the abs may make itself felt by slight vibration of the brake pedal in emergency braking press very firmly without releasing the pressure safety 135 136 safety md ay dynamic stability control dsc and anti slip regulation asr activation these system are activated automatically each time the vehicle is started as soon as they detect a problem of grip or trajectory these system act on the operation if the engine and brake e ee this is indicated by flashing of this warning lamp in the instrument panel deactivation in exceptional condition starting a vehicle which is bogged down stuck in snow on soft ground it may be advisable to deactivate the dsc system so that the wheel can move freely and regain grip but it is recommended that the system be reactivated as soon as possible e press the button s ee off this warning lamp and the indicator ch lamp in the button come on the ee dsc system no longer act on the operation of the engine reactivation the system is reactivated automatically each time the ignition is switched back on or from 30 mph 50 km h e press the button again to reactivate it manually operating fault if this warning lamp come on ch accompanied by an audible signal ce anda message this indicate a fault with the system contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop to have the system checked the dsc system offer exceptional safety in normal driving but this should not encourage the driver to take extra risk or drive at high speed the correct operation of the system depend on observation of the manufacturer s recommendation regarding the wheel tyre and rim the braking component the electronic component and the assembly and repair procedure used by citroen dealer after an impact have the system checked by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop seat belt front seat belt 4 7 the front seat belt are fitted with a pretensioning and force limiting system this system improve safety in the front seat in the event of a front or side impact depending on the severity of the impact the pretensioning system instantly tighten the seat belt against the body of the occupant the pretensioning seat belt are active when the ignition is on the force limiter reduce the pressure of the seat belt on the chest of the occupant so improving their protection rear seat belt lia f pan 1 wa if st at fe a 7 se e the rear seat are each fitted with a three point seat belt warning lamp s a 4 to 6 come on in red in the flash for two minute accompanied seat belt and front airbag warning by an audible signal once these lamp display if one or more rear two minute have elapsed the passenger unfasten their seat belt warning lamp s remain on until the driver or front passenger fasten their seat belt the driver must ensure that passenger use the seat belt correctly and that they are all restrained securely before setting off wherever you are seated in the vehicle alway fasten your seat belt even for short journey do not interchange the seat belt buckle as they will not fulfil their role fully the seat belt are fitted with an inertia reel permitting automatic adjustment of the length of the strap to your size the seat belt is stowed automatically when not in use before and after use ensure that the seat belt is reeled in correctly the lower part of the strap must be positioned as low as possible on the pelvis the upper part must be positioned in the hollow of the shoulder the inertia reel are fitted with an automatic locking device which come into operation in the event of a collision emergency braking or if the vehicle roll over you can release the device by pulling firmly on the strap and releasing it so that it reel in slightly in order to be effective a seat belt must be tightened as close to the body as possible be pulled in front of you with a smooth movement checking that it doe not twist be used to restrain only one person not bear any trace of cut or fraying not be converted or modified to avoid affecting its performance in accordance with current safety regulation for all repair on your vehicle go to a qualified workshop with the skill and equipment needed which a citroen dealer is able to provide have your seat belt checked regularly by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop particularly if the strap show sign of damage clean the seat belt strap with soapy water or a textile cleaning product sold by citroen dealer after folding or moving a seat or rear bench seat ensure that the seat belt is positioned and reeled in correctly safety recommendation for child use a suitable child seat if the passenger is less than 12 year old or shorter than one and a half metre never use the same seat belt to secure more than one person never allow a child to travel on your lap in the event of an impact depending on the nature and seriousness of the impact the pretensioning device may be deployed before and independently of the airbag deployment of the pretensioner is accompanied by a slight discharge of harmless smoke and a noise due to the activation of the pyrotechnic cartridge incorporated in the system in all case the airbag warning lamp come on following an impact have the seat belt system checked and if necessary replaced by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop 7 139 safety ee airbag system designed to maximise the safety of the occupant with the exception of the rear centre passenger in the the airbag will not be deployed the seat belt alone contribute toward ensuring your protection in these situation activation of the pyrotechnic cartridge incorporated in the system this smoke is not harmful but sensitive individual may experience slight irritation the noise of the detonation may result in a slight loss of hearing for a short time the airbag do not operate when the ignition is switched off this equipment will only deploy once if a second impact occur during the same or a subsequent accident the airbag will not be deployed again 140 front airbag system which protect the area deployment the curtain airbag is deployed at the same time as the corresponding lateral airbag in the event of a serious side impact applied to all or part of the side impact zone b perpendicular to the longitudinal centreline of the vehicle on a horizontal plane and directed from the outside toward the inside of the vehicle the curtain airbag inflate between the front or rear occupant of the vehicle and the window operating fault 2e if this warning lamp come on in the instrument panel accompanied by an audible signal and a message contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop to have the system checked the airbag may no longer be deployed in the event of a serious impact in the event of a minor impact or bump on the side of the vehicle or if the vehicle roll over the airbag may not be deployed in the event of a rear or front collision none of the lateral airbag is deployed safety for the airbag to be fully effective observe the following safety rule sit in a normal upright position wear a correctly adjusted seat belt do not leave anything between the occupant and the airbag a child pet object this could hamper the operation of the airbag or injure the occupant after an accident or if the vehicle has been stolen or broken into have the airbag system checked all work on the airbag system must be carried out by a citroen dealer ora qualified workshop even if all of the precaution mentioned are observed a risk of injury or of minor burn to the head chest or arm when an airbag is deployed cannot be ruled out the bag inflate almost instantly within a few millisecond then deflate within the same time discharging the hot gas via opening provided for this purpose front airbag do not drive holding the steering wheel by its spoke or resting your hand on the centre part of the wheel passenger must not place their foot on the dashboard do not smoke as deployment of the airbag can cause burn or the risk of injury from a cigarette or pipe never remove or pierce the steering wheel or hit it violently do not fit or attach anything to the steering wheel or dashboard this could cause injury with deployment of the airbag lateral airbag use only approved cover on the seat compatible with the deployment the lateral airbag for information on the range of seat cover suitable for your vehicle you can contact a citroen dealer refer to the accessory section do not fix or attach anything to the seat back clothing this could cause injury to the chest or arm if the lateral airbag is deployed do not sit with the upper part of the body any nearer to the door than necessary 7 curtain airbag do not fix or attach anything to the roof this could cause injury to the head if the curtain airbag is deployed if fitted on your vehicle do not remove the grab handle installed on the roof they play a part in securing the curtain airbag 145 146 child safety general point relating to child seat although one of citroen main criteria when designing your vehicle the safety of your child also depend on you for maximum safety please observe the following recommendation in accordance with european regulation all child under the age of 12 or less than one and a half metre tall must travel in approved child seat suited to their weight on seat fitted with a seat belt or isofix mounting statistically the safest seat in your vehicle for carrying child are the rear seat achild weighing less than 9 kg must travel in the rearward facing position both in the front and in the rear citroen recommend that child should travel on the rear seat of your vehicle rearward facing up to the age of 3 forward facing over the age of 3 k e the rule for carrying child are specific to each country refer to the legislation in force in your country child seat at the rear rearward facing when a rearward facing child seat is installed on a rear passenger seat move the vehicle s front seat forward and straighten the backrest so that the rearward facing child seat doe not touch the vehicle s front seat forward facing when a forward facing child seat is installed on a rear passenger seat move the vehicle s front seat forward and straighten the backrest so that the leg of the child in the forward facing child seat do not touch the vehicle s front seat ensure that the seat belt is correctly tensioned for child seat with a support leg ensure that this is in firm contact with the floor if necessary adjust the front seat of the vehicle child safety centre rear seat a child seat with a support leg must never be installed on the centre rear passenger seat 147 protection in the event of an accident check that there is no seat belt or seat belt buckle under the child seat as this could destabilise it remember to fasten the seat belt or the child seat harness that it is holding the child seat firmly against the seat of your vehicle if your front passenger seat is adjustable move it forward if necessary at the rear seat alway leave sufficient space between the front seat and arearward facing child seat the foot of a child seated in a forward facing child seat for this move the front seat forward and if necessary straighten its backrest for optimum installation of the forward facing child seat ensure that the back of the child seat is as close as possible to the backrest of the vehicle s seat or in contact if possible the head restraint must be removed before installing a child seat with a backrest on the passenger seat ensure that the head restraint is stowed or attached securely so that it is not thrown around the vehicle in the event of sharp braking refit the head restraint as soon as the child seat is removed child at the front the legislation on carrying a child on the front passenger seat is specific to each country refer to the legislation in force in your country deactivate the passenger s airbag when a rearward facing child seat is installed on the front seat otherwise the child would risk being seriously injured or killed if the airbag were deployed child safety installing a booster cushion the chest part of the seat belt must be positioned on the child s shoulder without touching the neck ensure that the lap part of the seat belt pass correctly over the child s thigh citroen recommend the use of a booster seat which has a back fitted with a seat belt guide at shoulder level as a safety precaution do not leave one and prevent filling do not persist but introduce a diesel type filler nozzle depending on country of sale e it remain possible to use a fuel can to fill the tank in order to ensure a good flow of fuel your vehicle is suitable for the fuel pump in the country in which you want to travel 9 163 164 practical information quality of the fuel used for petrol engine the petrol engine are perfectly compatible with e10 type petrol biofuel containing 10 ethanol conforming to european standard en 228 and en 15376 e85 type fuel containing up to 85 ethanol are reserved exclusively for vehicle marketed for the use of this type of fuel bioflex vehicle the quality of the ethanol must comply with european standard en 15293 for brazil only special vehicle are marketed to run on fuel containing up to 100 ethanol e100 type quality of the fuel used for diesel engine the diesel engine are perfectly compatible with biofuel which conform to current and future european standard diesel fuel which comply with standard en 590 mixed with a biofuel which comply with standard en 14214 available at the pump containing up to 7 fatty acid methyl ester the b30 biofuel can be used in certain diesel engine however this use is subject to strict application of the special servicing condition contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop the use of any other type of bio fuel vegetable or animal oil pure or diluted domestic fuel is strictly prohibited risk of damage to the engine and fuel system running out of fuel diesel on vehicle fitted with hdi engine the fuel system must be primed if you run out of fuel f1 if the engine doe not start first time don t keep trying but start the procedure again from the beginning ne 92 engine d d fill the fuel tank with at least five litre of diesel open the bonnet if necessary unclip the styling cover for access to the priming pump squeeze and release the priming pump repeatedly until resistance is felt there may be resistance at the first press operate the starter until the engine start if the engine doe not start at the first attempt wait around 15 second before trying again if the engine doe not start after a few attempt operate the priming pump again then start the engine clip the styling cover back in place close the bonnet practical information blue hdi 100 engine fill the fuel tank with at least five litre of diesel switch on the ignition without starting the engine e wait around 6 second and switch off the ignition ze repeat the operation 10 time operate the starter to run the engine 165 166 practical information adblue additive and scr system for blue hdi diesel engine to assure respect for the environment and conformity with the new euro 6 emission standard without adversely affecting the performance or fuel consumption of diesel engine citroen has taken the decision to equip its vehicle with an effective system that associate scr selective catalytic reduction with a particle emission filter pef for the treatment of exhaust gase presentation of the scr system using an additive called adblue containing urea a catalytic converter turn up to 85 of nitrous oxide nox into nitrogen and water which are harmless to health and the environment the adblue additive is held in a special tank located under the boot at the rear of the vehicle it has a capacity of 17 litre this provide a driving range of about 12 500 mile 20 000 km after which an alert is triggered warning you when the reserve remaining is enough for just 1 500 mile 2 400 km during each scheduled service of your vehicle by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop the adblue additive tank is refilled in order to allow normal operation of the scr system if the estimated mileage between two service is greater than 12 500 mile 20 000 km we recommend that you go to a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop to have the necessary top up carried out once the adblue tank is empty a system required by regulation prevent starting of the engine if the scr is faulty the level of emission from your vehicle will no longer meet the euro 6 standard you vehicle become polluting in the event of a confirmed fault with the scr system you must go to a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop as soon as possible after a running distance of 650 mile 1 100 km a system will be triggered automatically to prevent engine starting range indicator when switching on the ignition an indicator give an estimate of the distance that can be travelled with your vehicle before engine starting is automatically prevented once the vehicle start using the reserve of adblue or after a fault is detected with the scr system in the event of simultaneous system fault and low adblue level the shortest range is the one displayed practical information in the event of the risk of non starting related to a lack of adblue the engine start prevention system required by regulation is activated automatically once the adblue tank is empty range greater than 1 500 mile 2 400 km when switching on the ignition no information on range is displayed automatically in the instrument panel you can access this information in the touch screen tablet select the heater for the adblue tank in exceptional circumstance vehicle kept for a long period at temperature that are constantly below 15 c an emission control system fault alert may be related to freezing of the adblue in your vehicle park your vehicle in a warmer area for a few hour until the additive become liquid again the emission alert doe not go off immediately it will occur after several mile of running topping up the adblue additive filling the adblue tank is an operation included in every routine service on your vehicle by a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop nevertheless given the capacity of the tank it may be necessary to top up the additive between service more particularly if an alert warning lamp and a message signal the requirement you can got to a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop if you envisage topping up yourself please read the following warning carefully precaution in use the adblue additive is an urea based solution this liquid is non flammable colourless and odourless kept in a cool area in the event of contact with the skin wash the affected area with soap and water in the event of contact with the eye wash irrigate the eye with large amount of water or with an eye wash solution for at least 15 minute if a burning sensation or irritation persist get medical attention if swallowed immediately wash out the mouth with clean water and then drink plenty of water in certain condition high ambient temperature for example the risk of release of ammonia cannot be excluded do not inhale the product ammonia vapour has en irritant effect on mucous membrane eye nose and throat keep adblue out of the reach of child in its original bottle never transfer adblue to another container it would loose its purity practical information use only adblue additive that meet the iso 22241 standard never dilute the additive with water never pour the additive into the diesel fuel tank i adblue the supply in non drip bottle simplify toping up you can obtain 1 89 litre half us gallon bottle from a citro n dealer or a qualified workshop never top up from an adblue dispenser reserved for heavy good vehicle 171 172 practical information recommendation on storage adblue freez at about 11 c and deteriorate above 25 c it is recommended that bottle be stored in a cool area and protected from direct sunlight under these condition the using a slave battery external or on another vehicle and jump lead cable first check that the slave battery has a nominal voltage of 12 v and a capacity at least equal to that of the discharged battery do not try to start the engine by connecting a battery charger do not disconnect the terminal when the engine is running e remove the plastic cover from the terminal if your vehicle has one ze connect the red cable to the positive terminal of the flat battery a then to the positive terminal of the slave battery b connect one end of the green or black cable to the negative terminal of the slave battery b or earth point on the other vehicle connect the other end of the green or black cable to the earth point c on the broken down vehicle or on the engine mounting e start the engine of the vehicle with the good battery and leave it running for a few minute operate the starter on the broken down vehicle and let the engine run if the engine doe not start straight away switch off the ignition and wait a few moment before trying again wait until the engine return to idle then disconnect the jump lead cable in the reverse order refit the plastic cover to the terminal if your vehicle has one practical information before disconnecting the battery wait 2 minute after switching off the ignition before disconnecting the battery close the window and the door before disconnecting the battery following reconnection of the battery following reconnection of the battery switch on the ignition and wait 1 minute before starting to permit initialisation of the electronic system however if problem remain following this operation contact a citroen dealer or a qualified workshop 9 referring to the corresponding section you must yourself reinitialise depending on version the remote control key 205 206 practical information charging the battery using a battery charger with stop start the battery doe not have to be disconnected for charging disconnect the battery from the vehicle follow the instruction for use provided by the manufacturer of the charger connect the battery starting with the negative terminal check that the terminal and connector are clean if they are covered with sulphate whitish or greenish deposit remove them and clean them p o the battery contain harmful substance such as sulphuric acid and lead they must be disposed of in accordance with regulation and must not in any circumstance be discarded with household waste take used remote control battery and vehicle battery to a special collection point it is advisable to disconnect the battery if the vehicle is to be left unused for more than one month protect your eye and face before handling the battery all operation on the battery must be carried out in a well ventilated area and away from naked flame and source of spark so as to avoid the risk of explosion or fire do not try to charge a frozen battery the battery must first be thawed out to avoid the risk of explosion if the battery has been frozen before charging have it checked by a citro n dealer or a qualified workshop who will check that the internal component have not been damaged and the casing is not cracked which could cause a leak of toxic and corrosive acid do not reverse the polarity and use only a 12 v charger do not disconnect the terminal while the engine is running do not charge the battery without disconnecting the terminal first wash your hand afterward do not push the vehicle to start the engine if the vehicle is fitted with the electronic gearbox load reduction mode system which manage the use of certain function according to the level of charge remaining in the battery when the vehicle is being driven the load reduction function temporarily deactivate certain function such as the air conditioning the heated rear screen the deactivated function are reactivated automatically as soon as condition permit practical information energy economy mode system which manage the duration of use of certain function to conserve a sufficient level of charge in the battery after the engine has stopped you can still use function such as the audio and telematic system windscreen wiper dipped beam headlamp courtesy lamp etc for a maximum combined duration of about forty minute switching to economy mode a message then appear in the instrument panel screen indicating that the vehicle has switched to economy mode and the active function are put on standby if a telephone call is being made at this time it will be maintained for around 10 minute with the bluetooth hand free system of your audio system exiting economy mode these function are reactivated automatically next time the vehicle is driven in order to restore the use of these function immediately start the engine and let it run for less than ten minute to use the equipment for approximately five minute for more than ten minute to use the equipment for up to approximately thirty minute let the engine run for the duration specified to ensure that the battery charge is sufficient do not repeatedly and continuously restart the engine in order to charge the battery a flat battery prevent the engine from starting refer to the battery section 207 practical information o oo changing a wiper blade before removing a front fitting wiper blade e put the corresponding new wiper blade in place and clip it connect the in your country ensure that the weight of the towing vehicle is higher than that of the towed vehicle the driver must remain at the wheel of the towed vehicle and must have a valid driving licence there is no longer any power assistance for braking or steering in the following case you must alway call on a professional recovery service vehicle broken down on a motorway or fast road four wheel drive vehicle when it is not possible to put the gearbox into neutral unlock the steering or release the parking brake towing with only two wheel on the ground where there is no approved towing arm are as close as possible to the axle and the nose weight approach the maximum permitted without exceeding it air density decrease with altitude thus reducing engine performance above 1 000 metre the maximum towed load must be reduced by 10 for every 1 000 metre of altitude refer to the technical data section for detail of the weight and towed load which apply to your vehicle side wind ze take into account the increased sensitivity to side wind cooling towing a trailer on a slope increase the temperature of the coolant as the fan is electrically controlled its cooling capacity is not dependent on the engine speed to lower the engine speed reduce your speed the maximum towed load on a long incline depend on the gradient and the ambient temperature in all case keep a check on the coolant temperature ifthe warning lamp and the stop warning lamp come on stop the vehicle and switch off the engine as soon as possible e wps when not towing a trailer remove the quickly detachable tow ball before driving braking towing a trailer increase the braking distance to avoid overheating of the brake on a long mountain type of descent the use of engine braking is recommended tyre check x o the steering mounted src source button can be used to go to the next media source available if the source is active press ok to confirm the selection d cd cd mp3 y a radio src usb ipod r d auxiliary bluetooth streaming 04 music cd mp3 cd usb player information and advice the audio equipment will only play audio file with wma aac flac ogg and mp3 file extension and with a bit rate of between 32 kbp and 320 kbp it also support vbr variable bit rate mode no other type of file mp4 can be played wma file must be of the standard wma 9 type the sampling rate supported are 11 22 44 and 48 khz it is advisable to restrict file name to 20 character without using of special character e g u to avoid any playing and displaying problem in order to be able to play a recorded cdr or cdrw when recording it is preferable to select the iso 9660 level 1 2 or joliet standard if the disc is recorded in another format it may not be played correctly it is recommended that the same recording standard is alway used for an individual disc with as low a speed as possible 4x maximum for optimum sound quality in the particular case of a multi session cd the joliet standard is recommended the system support usb mass storage device blackberry device or apple player via usb port the adaptor cable not supplied control of the peripheral device is with the audio system control other peripheral not recognised on connection must be connected to the auxiliary socket using a jack cable not supplied in order to be read a usb memory stick must be formatted fat 16 or 32 the system doe not support two identical device connected at the same time two memory stick two apple player but it is possible to connect one memory stick and one apple player at the same time it is recommended that official apple usb cable are used to ensure correct operation 255 04 music selecting a track to play previous track next track previous folder next folder pause 256 b b u list of usb or cd track and folder system question the route calculation is not successful am unable to enter my post code the pol do not appear the risk area audible warning doe not work the system doe not suggest a detour around an incident on the route receive a risk area alert which is not on my route 302 answer navigation guidance the guidance criteria may conflict with the current location exclusion of toll road on a toll motorway the system only accept post code of up to 5 character the pol have not been selected the audible warning is not active the guidance criteria do not take account of tmc message other than guidance the system announce all risk area positioned in a cone located in front of the vehicle it may provide an alert for a risk area located on nearby or parallel road solution check the guidance criteria on the navigation menu guidance option define calculation criteria select the pol in the list of pol activate audible warning in the guidance option menu navigation guidance set parameter for risk area select the traffic info function in the list of guidance criteria zoom in on the map to view the exact position of the risk area select on the route to no longer receive alert other than guidance or to reduce the time for the announcement frequently asked question question certain traffic jam along the route are not indicated in real time the altitude is not displayed cannot connect my bluetooth telephone the volume of the telephone connected in bluetooth mode is inaudible answer on starting it is several minute before the system begin to receive the traffic information the filter are too restrictive in certain country only major route motorway are listed for the traffic information on starting the initialisation of the gps may take up to 3 minute to receive more than 4 satellite correctly depending on the geographical environment tunnel or the weather the condition of reception of the gps signal may vary telephone bluetooth the telephone s bluetooth function may be switched off or the telephone may not be visible the bluetooth telephone is not compatible with the system the volume depend both on the system and on the telephone the ambient noise level has an influence on the quality of telephone communication solution wait until the traffic information is being received correctly display of the traffic information icon on the map modify the geographic filter setting this phenomenon is normal the system is dependent on the traffic information available wait until the system has started up completely check that there is a gps coverage of at least 4 satellite long press on the setup button then select gps coverage this phenomenon is normal the system is dependent on the gps signal reception condition check that your telephone s bluetooth function is switched on check in the telephone setting that it is visible to all you can check the compatibility of your telephone at www citroen co uk service increase the volume of the audio system to maximum if required and increase the volume of the telephone if necessary reduce the ambient noise level close the window reduce the booster fan speed slow down 303 frequently asked question question some contact are duplicated in the list contact are not shown in alphabetical order the system doe not receive sms text message the cd is ejected automatically or is not played by the player there is a long waiting period following the insertion of a cd or connection of a usb memory stick the cd player sound is poor 304 answer the option for synchronizing contact are synchronizing the contact on the sim card the contact on the telephone or both when both synchronization are selected some contact may be duplicated some telephone offer display option depending on the setting chosen contact can be transferred in a specific order the bluetooth mode doe not permit sending sms text message to the system cd mp3 the cd is inserted upside down is unplayable doe not contain any audio data or contain an audio format which the player cannot play the cd has been recorded in a format that is not compatible with the player ud the cd is protected by an anti pirating protection system which is not recognised by the audio system when a new medium is inserted the system read a certain amount of data directory title artist etc this may take from a few second to a few minute the cd used is scratched or of poor quality the audio equipment setting bass treble ambience are unsuitable solution select display sim card contact or display telephone contact modify the display setting in the telephone directory check that the cd is inserted in the player the right way up check the condition of the cd the cd cannot be played if it is too damaged check the content in the case of a recorded cd refer to the advice in the audio section the audio system s cd player doe not play dvd some recorded cds will not be played by the audio system because they are not of the correct quality this phenomenon is normal insert good quality cds and store them in suitable condition set the treble or bass level to 0 without selecting an ambience frequently asked question question some character in the media information are not displayed correctly while playing playing of streaming file doe not start the name of track and the track length are not displayed on the screen when streaming audio the quality of reception of the radio station listened to gradually deteriorate or the stored station do not function no sound 87 5 mhz is displayed cannot find some radio station in the list of station received the name of the radio station change answer the audio system doe not display some type of character the peripheral device connected doe not support automatic play the bluetooth profile doe not allow the transfer of this information radio the vehicle is too far from the transmitter used by the station listened to or there is no transmitter in the geographical area through which the vehicle is travelling the environment hill building tunnel basement car park block reception including in rds mode the aerial is absent or has been damaged for example when going through a car wash or into an underground car park the station is not received or its name has changed in the list some radio station send other information in place of their name the title of the song for example the system interpret this information as the name of the station solution use standard character to name track and folder start the playback from the device activate the rds function by mean of the short cut menu to enable the system to check whether there is a more powerful transmitter in the geographical area this phenomenon is normal and doe not indicate a fault with the audio system have the aerial checked by a citroen dealer 305 frequently asked question question in changing the setting of treble and bass the equalizer setting is deselected in changing the equalizer setting treble and bass return to zero when changing the balance setting the distribution setting is deselected when changing an distribution setting the balance setting is deselected there is a difference in sound quality between the different audio source radio cd 306 answer setting configuration the selection of an equalizer setting impose the balance setting modifying one without the other is not possible the selection of a distribution setting impose the balance setting modifying one without the other is not possible for optimum sound quality the audio setting for volume bass treble equalizer and loudness can be adapted to the different sound source which may result in audible difference when changing source radio cd solution modify the balance or equalizer setting to obtain the desired musical ambience modify the balance or distribution setting to obtain the desired musical ambience check that the audio setting for volume bass treble equalizer loudness are adapted to the source listened to it is advisable to set the audio function bass treble fr re balance le ri balance to the middle position select the none musical ambience and set the loudness correction to the active position in cd mode or to the inactive position in radio mode frequently asked question question with the engine off the system switch off after a few minute of use playback of my usb memory stick manual dc 24 electrical safety testing ensure that at all time during the repair and testing of product that customer pet child and you are not exposed to any live electrical supply insulation test the following test must be performed prior to and upon completion of all repair to dyson floorcare product and before any functional check you must ensure that a full visual inspection of the product is completed prior to repair these test are vital to avoid any possibility of personal injury to the end user the seaward primetest 200 or equivalent should be used to test the electrical insulation of a class 1 1 appliance it indicate any electrical leakage insulation test point test the two screw highlighted in the upright switch cover test result an insulation test reading of 2 mq is acceptable a reading of below 2 mq is not considered safe and further investigation rectification and testing must be completed before the product is used the following component must be visually inspected the wiring behind the upright switch cover all wiring behind the switch housing test the two screw highlighted in the rear of the duct assembly if you cannot repair a product with an insulation test reading of below 2mq you must inform the customer that the product is unsafe to use please inform the customer of the required action to repair the product including the charge structure if the product is left un repaired please indicate on your paperwork hand held device that the product is electrically unsafe and attach a warning product electrically unsafe sticker in a cleaner head assembly jockey wheel japanese model only normally open bb motor n pcb assembly 03 service manual dc 24 electrical fault diagnosis when dc24 is switched on in the upright position the brushbar motor is off tilting the machine into normal vacuuming mode will automatically operate the upright switch turning the brushbar motor on the brushbar motor can be switched off using the brushbar switch the vacuum motor of dc24 is fitted with a heat sensitive thermal cut out tco this will shut the motor down for up to 60 minute if it reach a temperature 96 degree celcius excessive temperature within the motor are usually caused by machine filter blockage the cleaner head is fitted with a tco reset switch that will shut the brushbar motor off if it is put under an excessive load blocked seized brushbar etc the cause of the shut down should be cleared and the tco reset by pressing the switch if the cause of the brushbar motor cut out is not rectified the tco will repeatedly reactivate preventing the brushbar from turning electrical fault diaignosis note all connection number and continuity check refer to the illustration and table on page 3 no machine power vac motor and and machine identity 1 4 technical data biodrill 500 machine weight kg 260 noise level fan sound pressure max 75 db a 4 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 general safety regulation 2 general safety regulation 2 1 obligation and responsibility these instruction are to be regarded as for guidance only and entail no responsibility whatsoever on the part of v derstad ab and or its representative full responsi bility for the use transportation maintenance and servicing of the machine lie with the owner driver these instruction cover a large number of machine configuration local condition affecting crop rotation soil type climate etc may require procedure different to those mentioned in these instruction it is the full responsibility of the owner driver to ensure that the machine is used correctly in all respect the owner is also responsible for ensuring that all individual who use the machine have read and understood this instruction manual and that they work in accordance with all valid provision and regulation if an individual working with the machine detect any safety defect these must be addressed immediately all v derstad machine have passed quality assurance inspection and operational test prior to delivery however the user owner shall bear full responsibility for the machine functioning correctly when in use in the field in the event of a complaint please refer to the general delivery provision for the v derstad group design modification form a part of the continual improvement of our machine description of the machine therefore refer to the form and design of the machine at the time of writing the user manual may contain image which show a machine that doe not look exactly like the one you have received depending on which optional addition have been fitted the model or whether any update have been carried out some description in these instruction may not be relevant to your machine the operating instruction are designed according to the guideline of the iso 3600 standard 2 2 before using the machine a carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content b learn to use the machine correctly and carefully in the wrong hand or if carelessly operated the machine can become a dangerous implement c the machine will form a part of your workplace and the workplace of your colleague it is therefore important to ensure that everyone is protected and that functioning safeguard are in place 2 3 how to read these instruction letter within bracket refer to corresponding letter in the image and are used as a reference in a text a reference a b reference b information for which the order is important is indicated using numbered action instruction when referring to image figure are also used in the same way as the alphabetical list if the reference outnumber the letter in the alphabet 1 start by 2 then 2 4 description of safety symbol alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them a signal word used to indicate a potentially hazardous situation which if not avoided could result in minor or moderate injury however such distinction can be too subtle to affect behaviour or to be translated in certain circumstance signal phrase such as danger of death risk of blinding or beware of fume can be more effective at drawing greater attention to some instruction or safety information than signal word this indicate a special situation or an activity required for proper machine handling failure to follow these instruction may cause disruption to the machine or to its surrounding information marked with this symbol may be worth noting as it relate to a useful tip or a particularly useful piece of information that will help you handle the machine properly used to clarify information 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 5 general safety regulation used to present information in the form of bullet point the order in which the information is set out is not indicative of any particular order of precedence which must be followed 2 5 safety instruction make sure that the machine is set to the correct height correct height of the machine will ensure that the loading height is correct the loading height vary according to the machine type risk of eye injury never look into the radar optic while in operation alway follow the instruction and safety precaution for the host machine alway make sure that the coupling are clean and free from dirt to maintain the machine s high level of quality and operational safety use only genuine v derstad spare part the warranty and any claim under will become null and void if non genuine part are used 6 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 general safety regulation 2 6 scrapping and recycling when the machine or any of its part are worn out v derstad ab recommend that scrapping be carried out according to applicable regulation in order to avoid toxic and environmentally hazardous material ending up in the environment during its lifespan replaced wear part in steel should be sent to metal scrapping company replaced wear part in rubber plastic or the like should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility oil oil filter battery and other electrical component may have a negative impact on the environment and should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility in the case of scrapping the machine mostly consist of steel that is suitably scrapped by an authorised metal scrapping company other component should be taken care of according to local recycling regulation reach art 33 reach is a regulation adopted to improve the protection of human health and the environment from the risk that can be posed by chemical for more information on how v derstad ab follow reach article 33 visit www vaderstad com reach alway follow current local recycling regulation 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 control system 5 control system for isobus e control please refer to the separate user guide 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 11 filling and emptying 6 filling and emptying 6 1 before filling the hopper check before filling that the machine is empty clean and dry that the discharge hatch is closed that the correct rotor is fitted that the level guard is at the correct height 6 2 fill the hopper safety first never walk or stand under a suspended load make sure nobody is on the machine or in the hopper during delivery of the product to the machine do not approach the hopper unless absolutely necessary avoid contact with and inhalation of seed dressing 1 to fill the hopper use a loader with a pallet place the bag on the pallet 2 set the machine to the correct loading height when at the correct loading height the ladder will be at a good angle and the correct distance from the ground so that the operator can easily access the platform 3 use the provided access way when approaching the platform 6 2 1 adjust the level guard location of the level guard figure 6 1 the height of the level guard can be adjusted so that the sensor can be moved between the different bracket when drilling e g higher rate or a large size product the level guard can be placed higher up in the hopper when drilling low rate or a small size product the level guard can be placed at a lower level on the hopper disassembly and assembly 1 turn the level guard counter clockwise and loosen from the bracket 2 turn the level guard clockwise to assemble on to the other bracket 6 2 2 level scale for seed a scale with interval of 50l is installed inside the hopper to measure the level of the product the scale has a limit of 500l 12 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 filling and emptying 6 3 opening the discharge door figure 6 2 before opening the discharge door check that the fan is turned off the seed feed housing is emptied in a single operation figure 6 3 fan lever 1 turn off the fan by switching off the hydraulic remote on the tractor if the agitator is required for efficient emptying use the ball valve instead figure 6 4 2 turn the knob to release both the discharge door 206115 work using the hopper and when performing service and maintenance the machine must be supported safely on stand for all work underneath the machine or when there is a risk of crush injury ensure also that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them alway lower the machine to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system depressurise the hydraulic system before service and maintenance the machine should be in the lowered position and standing on a flat surface when carrying out service and maintenance alway switch off the ipad e control when performing maintenance and service work on the machine 8 1 regular maintenance to preserve the high quality of the drill alway use genuine v derstad spare part perform maintenance check at regular interval and at the end of the season check that there is no seed or any bit of packaging remaining in the biodrill hopper seed feed housing rotor or feed system check that there has been no seed or any bit of packaging in the seed hose or in the outlet of the distributor head check that no seed has run down into the ejector tube and associated air hose check that the seed hose and coupling are not trapped or damaged 8 1 1 cleaning cleanliness is of vital importance when undertaking any kind of work with the machine s hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on a clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with a degreasing agent never rinse the bearing using a high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean electrical electronic component directly with a high pressure water jet clean electrical electronic component by blow ing them with air or by wiping them with a slightly dampened cloth exercise extreme caution when washing with high pressure water in the area around the seed feed housing after cleaning air and dry all component carefully before reassembly keep the fan running for a while after cleaning so that the whole system is blown dry any remaining seed that sprout can block the air and seed hose seed can also attract small rodent which can damage the seed drill clean biodrill hopper rotor seed feed housing seed hose the tramlining motor and outlet in the distributor head clean the seed feed housing open and disassemble according to 8 3 seed meter allow all the part to dry thoroughly prior to reassembly clean the radar 904006 en gb 06 07 2023 15 maintenance and service 8 2 seed transport 8 2 1 2 repair and replace the seed hose figure 8 3 8 2 2 1 repair if a seed hose is damaged due to abrasion or folding it can be jointed with a sleeve 1 cut the hose at the centre of the fold or damage safety provision 2 general safety provision 2 1 obligation and responsibility these instruction cover various machine configuration and should be regarded as a guide entailing no responsibility whatsoever on the part of v derstad ab and or its representative full responsibility for the use transportation maintenance and servicing of the machine lie with the owner driver local condition affecting crop rotation soil type climate etc may require procedure different to those mentioned in these instruction it is the full responsibility of the owner driver to ensure that the machine is used correctly in all respect the owner is also responsible for ensuring that all individual who use the machine have read and understood this instruction manual and that they work in accordance with all valid provision and regulation if an individual working with the machine detect any safety defect these must be addressed immediately all v derstad seed drill have passed quality assurance inspection and operational test prior to delivery the owner driver shall however bear full responsibility for the machine functioning correctly when in use in the field in the case of a complaint please refer to the general delivery provision for the v derstad group design modification form a part of the continual improvement of our machine description of the machine therefore refer to the form and design of the machine at the time of writing the user manual may contain image which show a machine that doe not look exactly like the one you have received depending on which optional addition have been fitted the model or whether any update have been carried out some description in these instruction may not be relevant to your machine the operating instruction are designed according to the guideline of the iso 3600 standard 2 2 before using the machine a carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content b learn to use the machine correctly and carefully in the wrong hand or if carelessly operated the machine can become a dangerous implement c the machine will form a part of your workplace and the workplace of your colleague it is therefore important to ensure that everyone is protected and that functioning safeguard are in place 2 3 how to read these instruction letter within bracket refer to corresponding letter in the image and are used as a reference in a text a reference a b reference b information for which the order is important is indicated using numbered action instruction when referring to image figure are also used in the same way as the alphabetical list if the reference outnumber the letter in the alphabet 1 start by 2 then 2 4 description of safety symbol alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them alway pay extra attention to text or figure indicated by this symbol the symbol indicate danger that may result in death serious bodily harm or considerable material damage if action are not taken to avoid them this indicate a special situation or an activity required for proper machine handling failure to follow these instruction may cause disruption to the machine or to its surrounding information marked with this symbol may be worth noting as it relate to a useful tip or a particularly useful piece of information that will help you handle the machine properly used to clarify information used to present information in the form of bullet point the order in which the information is set out is not indicative of any particular order of precedence which must be followed 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 7 general safety provision 2 5 warning decal 2 5 1 location of warning decal figure 2 1 2 5 2 content of warning decal a carefully read through these instruction and ensure that you have understood their content refer to these instruction and safety pro vision as needed when working b alway ensure that the implement s entire working and extension area is alway kept clear never walk under a suspended section make sure that the wing section are locked when folded up for road transport parking and or maintenance check that the functionality of the locking hook is not impeded by soil and plant residue c warning tape beware of a crush or shock hazard also used on safety component d never work under the machine unless it has been properly secured with jack stand or other strong support on a firm surface lock the lifting cylinder using the appropri ate yellow locking device e crushing risk warning b l g d cfe a d h i j k n m o 8 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 general safety provision f alway make sure that the working area of the bout marker are free from obstruction bear in mind that there is a risk of injury when the bout marker are being unfolded and a risk of crushing between the seed drill and the bout marker when they are being retracted note the bout marker are alway retracted when the machine is lifted no matter what is shown on the control unit the indicated bout marker are alway extended when the machine is lowered for this reason alway switch off the control unit when the machine is not in the field setting and machine data are saved when the control unit is switched off g do not climb on the machine s wheel or packer as these may rotate even when the machine is stationary h make sure nobody is on the drill during operation i make sure nobody is on the seed drill when front loading seed and or fertiliser j the machine s ladder step and platform are not intended to be used for manual loading from small seed bag k extra transport height warning beware of overhead cable via duct gate and tree etc alway check the maximum permitted navigable height l warning against cutting oil jet as the hydraulic system contain accumulator exercise extreme caution when disconnecting hy draulic coupling from the machine never point hydraulic coupling at any part of the body empty the accumulator of oil before service and repair m do not stand on the machine grating n do not stand between the tractor and the machine when the tractor is being reversed for hitching o jack point236607 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 9 general safety provision 2 6 safety instruction 2 6 1 safety instruction during installation and maintenance alway park the implement on a level and stable surface depressurise the machine until it rest on its wheel disc and the support stand keep in mind that the ground pressure under the support stand is considerable especially when the hopper is full never stand under the intermediate packer or seed drill if the intermediate packer is raised and only secured with the tractor s hydraulic lifting arm prior to any service on the intermediate packer be sure to secure it properly with trestle etc on a stable surface any welding work on the machine must be carried out to a professional standard note that poor welding may result in serious personal injury or fatality if you are unsure contact a professional welding technician for instruction on machine that are delivered packaged you will find two packing box of machine part in the seed hopper the packaging for these packing box can get damp in transit check the packaging in the seed hopper when unloading if the packaging is damp or wet the content must be unloaded by hand regularly inspect the tractor s hitch device and the machine s towing eye for wear replace the towing eye when it has been worn down to the wear limit the machine is hydraulically pressurised when the tractor is connected and in operation alway shut down the tractor and disconnect the feed s electrical system when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill prior to connecting the hydraulic hose alway make sure the seed drill s and the tractor s coupling are clean and free of dirt to maintain the machine s high level of quality and operational safety use only v derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used 2 6 2 safety instruction during transport the owner operator has sole responsibility when transporting the seed drill on a public road ensure that at least 20 of the tractor s weight is still being carried by its front wheel when the seed drill has been connected loaded this is to ensure that the steering ability of the whole vehicle is maintained good judgement and extreme caution should be exercised when transporting the seed drill on public road we recommend the use of a tractor with a gross weight that is at least equal to the gross weight of the seed drill if the seed drill is not equipped with brake alway comply with national legislation concerning brake equipment when transporting the machine on public road show good judgement and drive carefully pay close attention to the machine s width and turning circle of the implement during transport the rear view is very restricted check the positioning of the tractor s rear view mirror please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is a risk that the machine may overturn use the light on the seed drill in accordance with local traffic regulation 10 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 general safety provision in order to avoid any risk arising from mistake during road transport all electronic control equipment inside and outside of the tractor cab must be switched off before commencing transport on the road during transportation on public road the pre implement must be raised retracted so that the transport width and transport height of the implement comply with national regulation before driving ensure that all nut and bolt are tight after a couple of hour driving re tighten the wheel nut regularly check that they are tight note that nut must be tightened to a specific torque do not stand on the platform during operation keep the platform clean to prevent any risk of slipping use the yellow locking device to block the lifting cylinder when transporting the drill on the road over longer distance please be aware that if the machine is running on very steep and or rough terrain there is a risk that the machine may overturn never fill the tyre to a pressure higher than tyre manufacturer s recommendation this may result in great danger on the road this machine and its tyre are designed for a maximum speed of 30 km h during transportation on the public highway pay attention to national speed limit this speed should be reduced on uneven road surface the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when being transported on road this will avoid unnecessary tyre wear fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it 2 6 3 safety instruction during work ensure that people who are near the seed drill when the tractor s engine is in operation maintain an adequate safety distance from a suspended load and from raised or moving machine part alway make sure that the following harrow s working area is free of obstruction bear in mind that there is a risk of injury when the following harrow is being lowered to the ground alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper as well as when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on a flat surface the ladder on the inside of the seed hopper is only intended for use in conjunction with service work the machine is equipped with a platform do not stand on the platform during operation the machine s platform is not intended to be used for manual loading from small bag if small bag are to be used the best way to fill the seed drill is to use a loader with the bag on a pallet watch out for hanging power line when the machine is being transported do not work with the machine during thunder storm and when there is a risk of lightning strike do not stand on or next to the machine 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 11 general safety provision the intermediate packer should alway be in the raised position when reversing the seed drill if the packer is not in the raised position the wheel carrier and the wheel themselve could be damaged fold up the lower step before transport to avoid the tractor damaging it 2 6 4 design change the user owner is liable for design change e g addition or modification that have not been approved by v derstad ab design change can affect wear and may therefore cause the machine to no longer comply with the legislative requirement in accordance with which it has been approved 2 6 5 configuration change the user owner is solely responsible for any configuration change that have not been approved by v derstad ab conversion of the machine invalidate the type approval for the machine 12 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 general safety provision 2 7 moving the machine when not hitched to a tractor if the machine must be moved when not hitched to a tractor it must be transported on a machine trailer or lorry flatbed the machine must be rolled onto and off the transport vehicle using a tractor lifting with a crane is prohibited for data pertaining to the dimension and weight of the machine see 1 5 technical data alway make sure that you comply with applicable national regulation concerning transport dimension requirement for escort vehicle or similar figure 2 2 1 switch the machine to its transport position see 6 2 2 switching to transport mode 2 activate the wheel retraction function see 6 2 2 1 wheel retraction function 3 raise the front tool to highlift height 4 reverse the machine lengthwise onto the trailer or flatbed if using a flatbed a ramp loading platform or similar will be required exercise extreme care check that no machine part are damaged during loading 5 lower the machine adjust the support stand so that the machine is resting on the wheel disc and support stand or the intermediate packer in the lowered position ensure that the machine s hydraulic system is depressurised 6 prevent the machine s transport wheel from rolling using chock or similar 7 secure the tarpaulin using tie down strap or similar 8 unhitch the tractor from the machine 9 secure the machine using suitable lashing equipment in accordance with applicable regulation the lashing equipment must be attached to the machine at the location indicated by the decal see figure 2 2 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 13 general safety provision 2 8 scrapping and recycling when the machine or any of its part are worn out v derstad ab recommend that scrapping be carried out according to applicable regulation in order to avoid toxic and environmentally hazardous material ending up in the environment during its lifespan replaced wear part in steel should sent to metal scrapping company replaced wear part in rubber plastic or the like should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility oil oil filter battery and other electrical component may have a negative impact on the environment and should be sent to the appropriate recycling facility in the case of scrapping the machine mostly consist of steel that is suitably scrapped by an authorised metal scrapping company other component should be taken care of according to local recycling regulation reach art 33 reach is a regulation adopted to improve the protection of human health and the environment from the risk that can be posed by chemical for more information on how v derstad ab follow reach article 33 visit www vaderstad com reach alway follow current local recycling regulation and applicable
