emergency brake system that is applied if the machine become unhitched from the tractor 6 1 1 1 parking brake figure 6 1 1 alway park the implement on a stable and level surface 2 activate the parking brake by undoing the pin b and rotating the lever handle a 3 tighten the lever a 4 release the parking brake by moving the lever slightly forward and then to the upright position if the implement has to be parked on or close to a public road the machine must also be secured by putting chock under the transport wheel if the machine is to be parked for a longer period of time the parking brake should be disengaged the machine should instead be secured with chock 6 1 1 2 emergency brake in order for the emergency brake to function the wire c in the parking brake lever must be secured to the tractor see figure 6 1 the emergency brake engage if the machine become unhitched from the tractor 6 1 2 pneumatic brake the machine can be fitted with pneumatic brake the pneumatic brake are linked to the tractor s compressed air coupling and are controlled by the pressure applied to the tractor s brake pedal the pneumatic brake have compressed air in the brake pipe but the last section out toward the brake drum consist of a closed oil system when the tractor s brake are applied the braking effect is proportionally transferred to the machine and ensure effective braking 6 1 2 1 connecting the brake connect the brake system s compressed air line and steering link to the tractor s brake fitting 1 the compressed air line has a red mounting and must be connected to the tractor s red coupling 2 the control line has a yellow mounting and must be connected to the tractor s yellow coupling 6 1 2 2 disconnecting the brake unhitching and parking must alway take place on a flat firm surface 1 disconnect the brake hose and electric cable from the tractor and hang them on the hose holder figure 6 2 2 release the brake by pressing in the retarder valve 3 lower and secure the support stand 4 disconnect the hitching hook agricultural drawbar device 6 1 2 3 parking pneumatic brake alway park the implement on a level and stable surface the brake are automatically applied when the loaded wheel retraction function alway use an appropriate tool when operating the locking device doe not apply to rda 400 without idc the seed drill is equipped with a function that allow the wing section wheel to be pulled in once the machine has been folded up into its transport position this is to minimise the machine s transport width figure 6 11 to activate the function the locking device a one on each wing section must be lifted up to position b once the machine has been folded up figure 6 12 use the enclosed key c or other suitable tool when the machine is folded out to working position the wheel retraction mechanism is reset automatically and the wheel return to normal position the seed drill should not be fully raised when the wing section are unfolded lower it approx 5 cm from top position before starting the folding process if the wheel retraction mechanism s automatic reset has not worked this is shown by the machine hanging at an angle in the extended position to rectify this retract the folding cylinder slightly lower the machine until its wheel retraction mechanism engage the machine can be folded up and folded out even without this function being activated with the only consequence being that the wing section wheel then stay in normal position and the transport width is therefore slightly greater to avoid serious damage to the machine if the machine is on soft ground and folded up do not lower it to the drilling position if the machine is folded up the wheel must not be drawn up from the ground using the hydraulic system b ac 28 904105 en gb 16 11 s drilling depth is controlled by three hydraulic cylinder connected in sery in a so called master slave system before the seed drill is brought into use the cylinder must be bled and reset in relation to each other 7 3 1 bleeding 1 raise the machine to its top position so that all hydraulic cylinder are fully extended 2 keep the hydraulic lever in this position and the tractor idling for approx 15 20 second the cylinder permit seepage when in the top position that allow oil to overflow through the system and expel any air 3 repeat this procedure for a few second once the tractor has been connected before adjusting the seed drill after folding out and a few time during the working day after bleeding and resetting the master slave system the height level for the three section should then be checked and adjusted in relation to each other if necessary this is important in order to ensure that the drilling depth is identical in all section 7 3 2 adjusting the height of the wing section against the centre section rda 600 800 adjustment should be made with the seed drill on a flat and solid surface the setting should then be checked with the machine in working mode in the 7 23 2 if the setting a is insufficient the holder b can be adjusted lengthwise 3 the distance c between the scraper blade and the tyre must not be less than 5 mm 4 rotate the tyre by hand to verify that this distance is not less than 5 mm at any point 5 depending on the circumstance a larger distance c may be required for optimum result if the scraper doe not operate satisfactorily then try with different scraper setting example if there is a lot of plant residue in the soil and it is humid the scraper will usually provide better result when the distance c is increased to approx 20 mm if the soil is humid without plant residue the scraper will provide better result when mounted in the default position c 5 mm 7 13 track eradicator for road transport the track eradicator must be mounted at consumption in kg ha 4 adjust the setting if required and repeat the test drive 5 reset the machine after the calibration test 10 3 scale the entire scale must alway be in a vertical position when used to ensure it give a correct result when performing the calibration test to prevent the precision of the scale from affecting the calibration it is recommended to weigh at least 2 kg of the selected seed or fertiliser it is recommend to replace the scale battery once a year figure 10 3 weighing of a calibration sample should be carried out as follow 1 press the start reset button 2 hang the empty calibration bag on the scale s hook 3 the weight of the bag is displayed wait until hold is shown on the display the scale automatically shut off after approximately 5 minute it is therefore advisable to note the empty bag s weight 4 press the start reset button 2 24kg hold 42 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 calibration 5 remove the bag and fill it with the calibration sample 6 weigh the now filled bag the scale now show the net weight of the sample whilst driving the scale should be stored safely in the calibration box regularly check the scale against a known weight and alway prior to the start of the season replace the battery 9v 6lr61 type when the battery indicator show one bar or less 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 43 seed drilling 11 seed drilling the drilling depth used must be adjusted to suit the current crop the soil type and drilling condition at the time of drilling continuously and carefully monitor the drilling depth the drilling depth is set mechanically using the lifting cylinder s stop device or from the tractor cab using the control unit in field with varying soil type the depth setting should be checked and adjusted to ensure the correct drilling depth across the entire field 11 1 feed system operation at too high a speed can lead to the seed bouncing around at the bottom of the seed furrow this result in an uneven drilling depth operation at too low a speed in relation to the application rate speed can lead to the seed hose getting blocked 11 1 1 setting the amount of air the fan rotation speed must not exceed 3600 rpm the amount of air is regulated by mean of the fan rotation speed the fan rotation speed is regulated by the tractor s flow valve there is a flow valve on most tractor model with constant pressure hydraulic system if the tractor has a priority hydraulic coupling use that one controlling the flow from the tractor s hydraulic system provide for optimum function of both the hydraulically powered fan and the tractor set the fan rotation speed in accordance with the table check the fan rotation speed in the display on ipad normal mode fan rotation speed 2500 3200 rpm the recommended speed are guideline at low application rate it may be a good idea to reduce the speed slightly at extremely high application rate high speed the fan speed may need to be increased the alarm warning of too low a rotation speed is best set for 300 rpm below the preset rotation speed and the alarm warning of too high a rotation speed to 300 rpm above the preset rotation speed the alarm level are set in the general setting menu table 11 1 recommended fan rotation speed rda 400 rda 600 rda 800 small size seed 2300 rpm 2500 rpm 2500 rpm grain 2800 rpm 3000 rpm 3200 rpm if full fan speed is not required the fan s rotation speed should be lowered using the tractor s flow valve 11 2 setting the drilling depth reset and bleed the system for approx 5 second several time during the working day raise the machine to its top position so that all hydraulic cylinder are fully extended keep the hydraulic lever in this position and the tractor idling 11 2 1 master cylinder stop bolt figure 11 1 when the interactive depth control function is used it is a good idea to set the stop bolt a to a safety depth i e a maximum desired drilling depth should problem arise with the interactive depth control function then the manual drilling depth setting can be used and the drilling can continue a 44 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 seed drilling 11 2 2 mechanical adjustment of drilling depth rda 400 800 with master slave system position see figure 11 2 the scale indication doe not give an absolute value of the drilling depth in centimetre but serve as a reference if there are wide variation in drilling depth the horizontal alignment may need to be adjusted see 7 1 horizontal alignment 11 2 3 mechanical adjustment of drilling depth rda 400 without master slave system figure 11 3 the drilling depth of the centre section is set using the stop bolt a the drilling depth of the wing section is set with the relevant crank b the scale indication do not constitute absolute value of the drilling depth in centimetre but serve drill finish drilling or making a normal turn on headland in the bout this mean the auto advance is switched off before the seed drill is raised to drill around a post well or other obstacle in the bout when it is time to fill the seed hopper or take a break 1 raise the seed drill out of the most recent bout and wait approx 10 second 2 then switch off the auto advance however the auto advance need not be switched off if no additional lifting will take place during the pause 3 engage the auto advance before the seed drill is raised again for the first time when you continue drilling ad 46 the ground keep the hydraulic lever in the push position i e the lowered position until the bout marker has been folded out completely check that the tramlining indication is progressing and that the indication for right left bout marker arm switch when lifting take place while driving lowlift and auto advance should normally be engaged 11 6 turning in lowlift mode the bout marker retract on arrival at the headland when the seed drill is lifted if lowlift is engaged the machine is lifted to lowlift height when the seed drill is lifted at the headland the bout marker and tramlining automatically advance by one step if the seed drill doe not lift up lift stop is probably engaged and if the bout marker and tramlining do not advance auto advance is probably disengaged when turning in lowlift mode the following harrow work throughout the turn before reversing the tractor train lowlift must be switched off and the seed drill raised to highlift position 11 7 drilling the headland in dry and fine condition the headland can be drilled first in wet condition or when drilling soil sensitive to compaction higher yield can be obtained if the headland is drilled last figure 11 8 the best way to mark out the headland is by completing the last tillage operation before drilling e g the autumn harrowing by harrowing the headland last so that the outermost headland pass has the right distance from the edge of the field if a seed coulter become blocked do not go under the machine unless it has first been properly secured with the yellow locking device 12 2 securing the machine for service 11 8 drilling around obstacle switch off the auto advance before raising the seed drill to sow around a post well or other obstacle in the bout 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 49 seed drilling the bout marker are retracted to get past an obstacle by activating the lift stop and moving the hydraulic lever to the lifting position the bout marker will retract while the seed drill will remain in its working position fold down the bout marker again in this case the function selector for the auto advance or the marker do not need to be touched 11 9 mechanical following harrow do not reverse the tractor unless the drill has been fully raised and the following harrow has sufficient clearance height if the machine has been set to the maximum drilling depth and the following harrow is subject to a high load the clearance in the transport position may be limited figure 11 9 1 select a suitable working angle from hole sery b figure 11 10 2 the work pressure of the following harrow is adjusted using the adjusting screw a use the ratchet supplied adjust the following harrow to remain in the work position when turning on the headland in the low lift position this will eradicate out the track of the drill when turning figure 11 11 3 check that the following harrow tine trail between the wheel and in the wheel track this indicate that the following harrow tine are going between the furrow when this is happening high pressure can be applied on the following harrow without disrupting the seed this allow a vaporisation layer to be harrowed while sowing ba c d 50 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 maintenance and service 12 maintenance and service the machine must alway be secured before any type of service or maintenance work is commenced never stand near hydraulic hose under pressure drain the system s accumulator before servicing the hydraulic system 12 1 securing prior to servicing alway shut down the tractor and take out the ignition key in conjunction with work in the seed hopper and when carrying out service and maintenance on the seed drill never work under the drill during maintenance or service work unless it has been secured with trestle and the lifting cylinder has been blocked machine should be placed in the topmost position before the catch can be mounted be sure to alway lower the drill to the ground prior to performing any service on the hydraulic system also ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is firm enough to support them if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable when carrying out service and maintenance the drill should be in the lowered position and standing on a flat surface exercise extreme caution when undertaking any kind of work with the machine s hydraulic system wipe with clean paper or cloth place part on a clean surface not directly on the work bench rinse before assembly for example with a decreasing agent never rinse the bearing with a high pressure water supply it is important to grease the bearing after washing in order to remove any water that has remained behind never clean bearing directly with a high pressure water jet clean electrical component by blowing them with air or by wiping them with a slightly dampened cloth to maintain the machine s high level of quality and operational safety use only v derstad genuine spare part the warranty and any claim under it will become void if part other than genuine part are used figure 12 1 1 lubricate the machine according to the interval specified in the lubrication schedule and alway before and after winter storage and after high pressure water cleaning 2 before operation ensure that all nut and bolt have been tightened doe not apply to bolt in flexible joint 3 during the season check periodically that the nut and bolt have not worked loose and inspect joint and hydraulic ram mounting for wear 12 2 securing the machine for service when work is to be performed on the implement the machine must alway be parked on a stable and level surface 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 51 maintenance and service when working on the hydraulic system the wing must be folded down and the implement lowered to the ground if the hydraulic system include accumulator these must be drained of oil before carrying out the service or repair work be very careful when working with the hydraulic system even if the tractor has been shut down and is depressurised there may still be some pressure left in the hydraulic hose check that the wing section locking hook are engaged before carrying out work underneath the folded up section for all work underneath the seed drill or when there is a risk of crush injury the machine must be supported safely on stand secure the seed drill using trestle or similar and lock all the lifting cylinder using the appropriate yellow locking device ensure that the surface supporting the trestle is sufficiently firm if the hopper is full which it ought not to be during maintenance work the weight of the seed drill is considerable figure 12 2 secure the machine by placing trestle in the appropriate position which are marked by x in figure 12 position figure 12 14100 410450 0 0 100 100 410410450450 0 0 0 0 a b c d e 60 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 maintenance and service to ensure a satisfactory sowing result it is important that the seed coulter are correctly installed on the drill select the installation height based on the condition and disc wear when the drill has been lowered with the disc resting on firm ground the seed coulter should not touch the surface c 0 mm please note that as the c measurement is reduced the seed coulter become more susceptible to impact with stone upon delivery the seed coulter are installed in position a this installation height suit most operating condition figure 12 15 drilling under very moist condition with a lot of plant residue in the soil surface or shallow drilling in light or humus soil may cause the disc to stop occasionally this can be avoided by moving the coulter up to position b position b however can result in impaired seed placement the illustration below show an example of drilling when there is a lot of plant residue on the soil surface in this case the seed coulter should be installed in position b figure 12 16 12 8 2 tightening nut figure 12 17 the seed coulter are spring mounted on two bolt and the soft washer press the seed coulter closer to the disc as the nut are tightened the nut should not be tightened more than allow for the coulter to be rocked with the hand the coulter must not have too close contact with the disc since this will cause increased wear and rotational friction for very loose soil and or shallow drilling depth it may be necessary to slightly loosen the nut when new seed coulter are mounted it is necessary to check the width of the slot between the disc and the coulter the spring should be wider at the top otherwise straw and plant residue may collect here if the seed coulter do not make contact at the correct place this can be adjusted by moving the metal plate at position d and e inside or outside the fixing bracket the point of contact can also be moved somewhat by tightening the front nut more than the rear one position reference measurement between the disc and the seed coulter 1 0 mm 2 0 mm 3 0 mm 12 8 3 changing the seed coulter mounting bolt the disc arm has two recessed bolt for coulter mounting if these cannot be driven out they must be cut and ground level with the arm it may be necessary to remove the whole disc arm to facilitate this figure 12 18b1 23 d e 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 61 maintenance and service the bolt can then be driven out using a 13 5 mm diameter punch burr may be left in the hole after the bolt have been removed file them away to facilitate driving in the new bolt drive or press in the new bolt or pull them into place with the aid of a few washer and a nut not a locknut oil the thread the bottom of the washer and the nut thread liberally use the nut to pull the bolt into place be sure to use a sufficient number of washer to prevent the nut from reaching the bottom of the threaded section of the bolt 12 9 locking device figure 12 19 do not lubricate the locking device check its function regularly the locking hook a on the back of the seed hopper must go into the lug b can move without any excessive effort and that there is no longer any play if the play still exist two additional spacer may be required 12 11 4 wheel bearing adjustment it is important that the wheel procedure the highest possible level of cleanliness should be observed when replacing sealing kit be careful not to damage any surface of the hydraulic component while working and that all gasket are 17 troubleshooting 17 1 general information on troubleshooting many of the function of the seed drill are controlled by a sery of electrical hydraulic and mechanical component a good way to immediately exclude many of the source of fault is to first determine whether or not the fault is electrical because of this first check whether the electrical circuit is intact right up to the last electrical component in the chain then continue troubleshooting by first performing the easiest inspection in order to quickly exclude other source of fault 17 2 electrical fault general check in the event of electrical fault check that all the connector pin and socket are clean free from damage and are not pushed inward spray the switch with contact spray that is intended for use with electronic equipment check that all cable are correctly connected and that no cable is trapped or otherwise damaged check that the power cable fuse is intact 17 3 hydraulic fault general check in the event of hydraulic fault check that the hydraulic hose are connected to the correct socket on the tractor hose with the same colour coding constitute a pair make sure the hydraulic quick coupler are designed for and suited to the tractor s coupling there is a wide variety of connector on the market all standardised but still there are problem problem can manifest themselve so that female and male coupling operate as non return valve that is that a machine can be raised but not lowered or vice versa the problem may be aggravated by a large flow or worn coupling 17 4 hydraulic electric valve an electric valve has a coil which act as an electromagnet when an electrical current is connected to the valve it is easy to determine whether or not the power is on the connecting contact s diode light up and the coil become warm after a few minute the top nut will also become magnetic figure 17 1 use a small chisel or the side of a blade to determine whether or not the top nut has become magnetic the nut is alway weakly magnetic so try both with and without power figure 17 2 the bout marker valve a and b receive current when the control unit indicate active bout marker while the machine is below lowlift height the lift stop valve i receive current when the lift stop is engaged or during lift in the lowlift mode the pre emergence bout marker s solenoid valve d receive current when the control unit indicate active tramlining while the machine is below lowlift height the bout marker stop valve j receive current when the control system is activated to finely adjust the drilling depth the sink stop valve h receive current when the machine reach the set drilling depth outgoing signal to bout marker and the pre emergence bout marker are not activated until the machine is below lowlift height d b a j i h 94 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 troubleshooting 17 5 reed switch figure 17 3 a magnetic switch known also as a reed switch is a switch or a sensor that react to a magnetic field the magnetic switch is a glass tube containing two metal reed that attract each other when subjected to a magnetic field from a magnet see the illustration reed switch operation can easily be tested using a multimeter and a magnet 17 6 inductive sensor figure 17 4 react to metal object passing within a distance of 1 6 mm function testing can be carried out easily since a diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time an object is detected 17 7 capacitive sensor figure 17 5 react to object that contain moisture for example grain or a hand and so on function testing is easily carried out since a diode at the rear of the sensor light up each time such an object is detected the sensitivity of the sensor can be adjusted by use of the screw next to the led various type of grain and fertiliser contain different amount of moisture for this reason adjustment may be necessary under certain condition 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 95 troubleshooting 17 8 troubleshooting list the control unit doe not operate when the main switch is switched on see 17 2 electrical fault the bout marker s cannot be unfolded check in accordance with 17 2 electrical fault check in accordance with 17 3 hydraulic fault check whether the control unit is indicating that a bout marker is active check whether the machine is at or above the lowlift height for safety reason the bout marker cannot be unfolded in the lowlift mode or at a higher height check the lowlift switch remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the wing lowering switch this switch block the bout marker valve current signal when the seed drill is folded up to prevent a bout marker from unintentionally being lowered in this mode remove the switch connector to workstation and check the bout marker function check the power supply to the two centre solenoid valve a and b on the top of the valve block see 14 1 8 hydraulic electric valve the bout marker valve s must receive current when the respective indication lamp are lit the bout marker s fail to retract probably due to dirt in the solenoid valve note that dirt causing fault is often invisible it is recommended that solenoid valve are changed it should alway be possible to retract both bout marker regardless of the setting and indication on the control unit and even if the control unit is switched off the folded up bout marker s slowly extend inadvertently check whether the indication for this bout marker show that it is inactive on the control unit check that there is no current being supplied to the solenoid valve see 14 1 8 hydraulic electric valve swap the right and left bout marker solenoid valve if the fault shift to the other side that solenoid valve must be faulty if the fault remain the bout marker hydraulic cylinder is probably defective remove the solenoid valve and check that it is free from dirt and that the outer seal of the valve are intact and undamaged note first lower the seed drilling unit to the ground and release the hydraulic pressure check that the bout marker cylinder is not leaking externally or internally if the seal need to be changed see 12 13 1 service of hydraulic component if the bout marker unfold when the machine is parked set the stop device so that the machine is resting on the wheel disc and support stand use the cotter pin 96 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 troubleshooting automatic switching of the bout or tramline marker doe not take place is auto advance selected on the control unit has the bout marker function selector been set to the alternating position the indicator lamp in the button should light has the correct tramlining program been selected is the lowlift switch correctly adjusted see 7 10 setting the lowlift height when lowlift position is reached the signal for auto advance is given check whether the lowlift switch holder is crooked the holder is stuck in the wrong position or the magnetic sensor is damaged has the magnet come off or become loose check for a possible break in the wiring or a short circuit the seed drill can be raised even though the lift stop is engaged check that the solenoid valve c on the valve block is receiving current see 14 1 8 hydraulic electric valve check the lowlift switch as described above if the lift stop valve has no current see 17 2 electrical fault if the lift stop valve has voltage see 17 3 hydraulic fault the seed drill cannot be raised or lowered check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see 17 3 hydraulic fault if the seed drill cannot be raised check that the lift stop or lowlift mode has not been engaged see 17 3 hydraulic fault check whether the lift stop solenoid valve c is receiving current either via a short circuit or because of another fault see 14 1 8 hydraulic electric valve switch off the control unit and check whether the seed drill can be raised if the unit still cannot be raised the fault must be hydraulic check whether the short circuit is in the cable braiding or intermediate cable and which function is affected the control unit is giving a warning that the fan speed is too low or too high note no warning occur if a fan hose has come loose or the ejector hook are not in place fan check that the hydraulic hose are connected properly and that the hydraulic outlet is activated check the control unit s programming are the pressure and flow as they should be on the tractor 180 bar and at least 40 l min is the tractor s flow valve correctly adjusted is the fan s speed regulator correctly adjusted not supplied as standard used when the tractor doe not have flow control 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 97 troubleshooting the shaft seal in the fan hydraulic motor is leaking leakage from the hydraulic motor shaft seal may be due to damage during assembly wear because of dirt wear because of high pressure in the drain line destroyed seal because of extremely high pressure in the drain line the pressure in the drain line can be measured on the block use a 0 10 bar pressure gauge the maximum pressure during operation should be 10 bar high pressure may be due to a large oil flow in the drain line back pressure in the tractor s hydraulic valve detach the drain line from the tractor and remove the quick release coupling start the fan collect the drained oil in a container measure the pressure measure the amount of oil that arrive over a period of one minute a considerable decrease in pressure indicate a problem with the tractor s hydraulic valve a slight decrease in pressure indicate a large oil flow the drain flow should normally be less than 3 litre per minute a large oil flow may be due to a worn motor a leaking non return valve in the connection block oil from the return line leaking into the drain line the control unit warn there is a problem with the metering unit operation if there is a sensor fault check the operation of the sensor see 17 6 inductive sensor check that the toothed washer in the metering unit is not damaged check that the inductive sensor is sufficiently close to the rotating toothed washer adjust if necessary the area meter speedometer show no value or an incorrect value has the correct number of pulse per metre been programmed in if too low a speed or too small an area is displayed then reduce the number of pulse per metre if too high a speed or too large an area is displayed then increase the number of pulse per metre isobus e control change the number of pulse by pressing the field which display the radar pulse metre see the section on calibration in the instruction manual for e service the tramlining hatch get stuck check whether the long piston rod of the folding cylinder is completely extended and that the rod doe not slowly retract during driving check the setting of the wing section see 7 3 3 adjusting the wing section on rda 400 for the weight transfer setting see 11 3 1 setting the weight transfer check the setting of the master slave system see 7 3 adjusting the master slave system 98 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 troubleshooting disc do not rotate properly are the seed coulter exposed to undue stress the seed coulter should sit very lightly is the soil very loose it may be necessary to compact the soil using a roller is the drilling depth too shallow have the seed coulter been installed too low the disc provide better traction if the seed coulter are raised one notch are the disc excessively worn doe the soil surface contain a lot of plant residue are the front tool tine being used too roughly making the soil very loose the seed drill doe not place seed at the bottom of the furrow are the seed coulter excessively worn are the seed coulter set one notch too high the drill will place the seed more precisely if the coulter are set low this doe not normally need to be changed for different soil type the fan s speed is irregular replace the hydraulic oil filter the seed application rate is irregular replace the proportional valve in the fan block the left metering unit doe not rotate even though the drive unit is rotating check the locking pin between the metering unit and the drive unit and replace if necessary 904105 en gb 16 11 2023 99 seeding table 18 seeding table alway perform a seed calibration test the content of the seeding table must be viewed as guideline only new feed sample should be taken regularly in the case of small feed quantity check the area driven and the amount of seed fed every time you fill up 18 1 wheat rye corn oat wheat rye corn oat kg l 0 77 0 72 0 67 0 50 scale professional dual fuel range with self clean thermal convection oven job aid 4317282 forward this job aid professional dual fuel range with self clean thermal convection oven part no 4317282 provide the technician with information on the operation and service of the pro fessional dual fuel range it is to be used as a training job aid and service manual the wiring diagram and strip circuit used in this job aid are typical and should be used for training purpose only alway use the wiring diagram supplied with the product when servicing the unit goal and objective the goal of this job aid is to provide detailed information that will enable the service technician to properly diagnose malfunction and repair the professional dual fuel range the objective of this job aid are to understand and follow proper safety precaution successfully troubleshoot and diagnose malfunction successfully perform necessary repair successfully return the professional dual fuel range to the proper operational status whirlpool corporation assume no responsibility for any repair made on our product by anyone other than authorized factory service technician copyright 2001 whirlpool corporation benton harbor the safety alert symbol this symbol alert you to hazard that can kill or hurt you and other all safety message will be preceded by the safety alert symbol and the word warning all safety message will identify the hazard tell you how to reduce the chance of injury and tell you what can happen if the instruction are not followed important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem service manual provide information about the specification system introduction service procedure and adjustment of the new mg 3 vehicle how to use this manual general to assist in the use of this manual it is divided into section and sub section the relevant sub section title is given at the header of each page there is a single content section at the front of the manual which is divided by section and sub section the book is numbered from page 1 the page number is given at the bottom of each page the individual action of the repair operation must be followed in the sequence in which they appear item number in the illustration refer to the relevant numbered text under the illustration adjustment and repair operation include reference to service tool number and the associated illustration depict the tool in use adjustment and repair operation also include reference to wear limit relevant data torque figure and specialist information and useful assembly detail each adjustment or repair operation is given a unique repair operation number warning caution note and tip have the following meaning when encountering a warning you will be asked to take a necessary action or not to take a prohibited action if a warning is not heeded the following consequence may occur serious bodily injury or death to the technician serious bodily injury or death to other technician in the workplace area serious bodily injury to the driver and or passenger s of the vehicle if the vehicle has been improperly repaired caution call special attention to a necessary action or to a prohibited action if a caution is not heeded the following consequence may occur damage to the vehicle unnecessary vehicle repair unnecessary component replacement improper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation of the system or component under repair improper operation or performance of any system or component which are dependent upon the proper operation or performance of the system or component under repair damage to fastener basic tool or special tool the leakage of coolant lubricant or other vital fluid note statement emphasize a necessary characteristic of a diagnostic or repair procedure note statement are designed to do the following clarify a procedure present additional information for accomplishing a procedure give insight into the reason or reason for performing a procedure in the manner recommended present information that will help to accomplish a procedure in a more effective manner present information that give the technician the benefit of past experience in accomplishing a procedure with greater ease tip give helpful information reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this manual are made when viewing the vehicle from the rear with the engine and gearbox assembly removed the crankshaft pulley end of the engine is referred to as the front operation covered in this manual do not include reference to testing the vehicle after repair it is essential that work is inspected and tested after completion and if necessary a road test of the vehicle is carried out this is of particular importance where safety related item are concerned dimension the dimension quoted are to design engineering specification service limit are included where applicable our company are constantly seeking to improve the specification design and production of their vehicle and alteration take place accordingly while every effort has been made to ensure the accuracy of this manual it should not be regarded as an infallible guide to current specification of any particular vehicle 1 0 1 repair and replacement part when replacement part are required it is essential that only our company s recommended part are used attention is particularly drawn to the following point concerning repair and the fitting of replacement part and accessory safety feature and corrosion prevention treatment embodied in the vehicle may be impaired if other than smc recommended part are fitted torque wrench setting figure given in this manual must be used locking device where specified must be fitted if the efficiency of a locking device is impaired during removal it must be renewed the term of the vehicle warranty may be invalidated by the fitting of part other than smc recommended part allsmcrecommendedpartshavethefullbackingofthevehicle warranty smc dealer are obliged to supply only smc recommended part special tool general information general precaution dangerous substance modernvehiclescontainmanymaterialsandliquidswhichifnot handled with care can be hazardous to both personal health and the environment warning many liquid and other substance used in vehicle are poisonous and should under no circumstance be consumed and should as far as possible be kept from contact with the skin these liquid and substance include acid antifreeze brake fluid fuel windscreen washer additive lubricant refrigerant and various adhesive warning alway read carefully the instruction printed on label or stamped on component and obey them implicitly such instruction are included for reason of your health and personal safety never disregard them synthetic rubber many o ring seal hose flexible pipe and other similar item which appear to be natural rubber are in fact made of synthetic material called fluoroelastomer under normal operating condition this material is safe and doe not present a health hazard however if the material is damaged by fire or excessive heating it can break down and produce highly corrosive hydrofluoric acid contact with hydrofluoric acid can cause serious burn on contact with skin if skin contact doe occur remove any contaminated clothing immediately irrigate affected area of skin with a copious amount of cold water or limewater for 15 to 60 minute obtain medical assistance immediately should any material be in a burnt or overheated condition handle with extreme caution and wear protective clothing seamless industrial glove protective apron etc decontaminate and dispose of glove immediately after use lubricating oil avoid excessive skin contact with used lubricating oil and alway adhere to the health protection precaution warning avoid excessive skin contact with used engine oil used engine oil contain potentially harmful contaminant which may cause skin cancer or other serious skin disorder warning avoid excessive skin contact with mineral oil mineral oil remove the natural fat from the skin leading to dryness irritation and dermatitis health protection precaution the following precaution should be observed at all time wear protective clothing including impervious glove where practicable avoid prolonged and repeated contact with oil particularly used engine oil do not put oily rag in pocket avoid contaminating clothe particularly those next to the skin with oil overall must be cleaned regularly discard heavily soiled clothing and oil impregnated footwear first aid treatment should be obtained immediately for open cut and wound apply barrier cream before each work period to help prevent lubricating oil from contaminating the skin wash with soap and water to ensure all oil is removed proprietary skin cleanser and nail brush will help use moisturiser after cleaning preparation containing lanolinhelpreplacetheskin snaturaloilswhichhavebeen removed do not use petrol gasoline kerosene diesel fuel oil thinner or solvent for cleaning skin where practicable degrease component prior to handling if skin disorder develop obtain medical advice without delay wear eye protection e g goggle or face shield if there is a risk of eye contamination eye wash facility should be provided in close vicinity to the work area 1 0 1 general information safety instruction jacking caution it is best to use the lift when working under the vehicle be sure to hold the wheel with chock and apply parking brake alway use the recommended jacking point alway ensure that any lifting apparatus has sufficient load capacity for the weight to be lifted ensure the vehicle is standing on level ground prior to lifting or jacking apply the parking brake and chock the wheel warning do not work on or under a vehicle supported only by a jack alway support the vehicle on safety stand do not leave tool lifting equipment spilt oil etc around or on the work bench area alway keep a clean and tidy work area brake shoe and pad alway fit the correct grade and specification of brake lining when renewing brake pad and brake shoe alway replace as complete axle set brake hydraulic observe the following recommendation when working on the brake system ensure that hose run in a natural curve and are not kinked or twisted ensure that hose run in a natural curve and are not kinked or twisted fit brake pipe securely in their retaining clip and ensure that the pipe cannot contact a potential chafing point container used for brake fluid must be kept absolutely clean do not store brake fluid in an unsealed container it will absorb water and in this condition would be dangerous to use due to a lowering of its boiling point do not allow brake fluid to be contaminated with mineral oil or put new brake fluid in a container which has previously contained mineral oil do not re use brake fluid removed from the system alway use clean brake fluid or a recommended alternative to clean hydraulic component after disconnection of brake pipe and hose immediately fit suitable blanking cap or plug to prevent the ingress of dirt only use the correct brake fitting with compatible thread observe absolute cleanliness when working with hydraulic component cooling system cap and plug expansion tank cap and coolant drain or bleed screw when the engine is hot especially if it is overheated to avoid the possibility of scalding allow the engine to cool before attempting removal 1 0 2 general information environmental precaution general this section provide general information which can help to reduce the environmental impact from the activity carried out in workshop emission to air many of the activity that are carried out in workshop emit gase and fume which contribute to global warming depletion of the ozone layer and or the formation of photochemical smog at ground level by considering how the workshop activity are carried out these gase and fume can be minimised thus reducing the impact on the environment exhaust fume running car engine is an essential part of workshop activity and exhaust fume need to be ventilated to atmosphere however the amount of time engine are running and the position of the vehicle should be carefully considered at all time to reduce the release of poisonous gase and minimise the inconvenience to people living nearby solvent some of the cleaning agent used are solvent based and will evaporate to atmosphere if used carelessly or if can are left unsealed all solvent container should be firmly closed when not needed and solvent should be used sparingly suitable alternative material may be available to replace some of the commonly used solvent similarly many paint are solvent based and the spray should be minimised to reduce solvent emission refrigerant discharge and replacement of these material from air conditioning unit should only be carried out using the correct equipment engine alway adhere to the following don t leave engine running unnecessarily minimise testing time and check where the exhaust fume are being blown material keep lid on container of solvent only use the minimum quantity consider alternative material minimise over spray when painting gase use the correct equipment for collecting refrigerant don t burn rubbish on site discharge to water oil petrol solvent acid hydraulic oil antifreeze and other such substance should never be poured down the drain and every precaution must be taken to prevent spillage reaching the drain handling of such material must take place well away from the drain and preferably in an area with a kerb or wall around it to prevent discharge into the drain if a spillage occur it should be soaked up immediately having a spill kit available will make this easier checklist alway adhere to the following disposal and spillage prevention instruction never pour anything down a drain without first checking that it is environmentally safe to do so and that it doe not contravene any local regulation store liquid in a walled area protect bulk storage tank from vandalism by locking the valve transfer liquid from one container to another in an area away from open drain ensure lid are replaced securely on container make sure that tap on liquid container are secure and cannot be accidentally turned on have spill kit available near to point of storage and handling of liquid spill kit special material are available to absorb a number of different substance they can be in granular form ready to use and bought in convenient container for storage disposal of used spill absorbing material is dealt with in waste management section land contamination oil fuel and solvent etc can contaminate any soil that they are allowed to contact such material should never be disposed of by pouring onto soil and every precaution must be taken to prevent spillage reaching soil waste material stored on open ground could also leak or have polluting substance washed off them that would contaminate the land alway store these material in suitable robust container checklist alway adhere to the following don t pour or spill anything onto the soil or bare ground don t store waste material on bare ground see spillage prevention list 1 0 3 general information local issue a number of environmental issue will be of particular concern to resident and other neighbour close to the site the sensitivity of these issue will depend on the proximity of the site and the layout and amount of activity carried on at the site car alarm testing panel beating hammering and other such noisy activity should whenever possible be carried out indoor with door and window shut or as far away from house as possible be sensitive to the time of day when these activity are carried out and minimise the time of the noisy operation particularly in the early morning and late evening another local concern will be the smell from the various material used using less solvent paint and petrol could help prevent this annoyance localresidentsandotherbusinessuserswillalsobeconcerned about traffic congestion noise and exhaust fume be sensitive to these concern and try to minimise inconvenience from delivery customer and servicing operation checklist alway adhere to the following identify where the neighbour who are likely to be affected are situated minimise noise smell and traffic nuisance prevent litter by disposing of waste in the correct manner have waste container emptied regularly waste management one of the major way that pollution can be reduced is by the careful handling storage and disposal of all waste material that occuronsite thismeansthatitisnecessarytonotonlyknow what the waste material are but also to have the necessary documentation and to know local regulation that apply handling and storage of waste they should be stored in such a way as to prevent the escape of the material to land water or air they must also be segregated into different type of waste e g oil metal battery used vehicle component this will prevent any reaction between different material and assist in disposal disposal of waste disposalofwastematerialsmustonlybetowastecarrierswho are authorised to carry those particular waste material and have all the necessary documentation the waste carrier is responsible for ensuring that the waste is taken to the correct disposal site dispose of waste in accordance with the following guideline fuel hydraulic fluid anti freeze and oil keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor refrigerant collect in specialist equipment and reuse detergent safe to pour down the foul drain if diluted paint thinner keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor component send back to supplier for refurbishment or disassemble and reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste small part reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste metal can be sold if kept separate from general waste tyre keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor packaging compact as much as possible and dispose of in ordinary waste asbesto material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor oily and fuel waste e g rag used spill kit material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor air filter keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor rubber plastic dispose of in ordinary waste hose dispose of in ordinary waste battery keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor airbag explosive keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor electrical component send back to supplier for refurbishment or disassemble and reuse any suitable part dispose of the remainder in ordinary waste catalyst can be sold if kept separate from general waste used spill absorbing material keep separate and dispose of to specialist contractor 1 0 4 general information general fitting instruction component removal whenever possible clean component and surrounding area before removal blank off opening exposed by component removal immediately seal fuel oil or hydraulic line when aperture are exposed use plastic cap or plug to prevent loss of fluid and ingress of dirt close the open end of oilway exposed by component removal with tapered hardwood plug or conspicuous plastic plug immediately a component is removed place it in a suitable container use a separate container for each component and its associated part clean bench and provide marking material label and container before dismantling a component dismantling observescrupulouscleanlinesswhendismantlingcomponent particularly when brake fuel or hydraulic system part are being worked on a particle of dirt or a cloth fragment could cause a serious malfunction if trapped in these system blow out all tapped hole crevice oilway and fluid passage with an air line ensure that any o ring used forsealingarecorrectlyreplacedorrenewed ifdisturbed during the process use marking ink to identify mating part and ensure correct reassembly do not use a centre punch or scriber to mark part they could initiate crack or distortion in marked component wire together mating part where necessary to prevent accidental interchange e g roller bearing component attach label to all part which are to be renewed and to part requiring further inspection before being passed for reassembly place these part in separate container from those containing part for rebuild do not discard a part due for renewal until after comparing it with a new part to ensure that its correct replacement has been obtained cleaning component alway use the recommended cleaning agent or equivalent ensure that adequate ventilation is provided when volatile degreasing agent are being used do not use degreasing equipment for component containing item which could be damaged by the use of this process general inspection all component should be inspected for wear or damage before being reassembled never inspect a component for wear or dimensional check unless it is absolutely clean a slight smear of grease can conceal an incipient failure when a component is to be checked dimensionally against recommended value use the appropriate measuring equipment surface plate micrometer dial gauge etc ensure the measuring equipment is calibrated and in good serviceable condition reject a component if its dimension are outside the specified tolerance or if it appear to be damaged a part may be refitted if its critical dimension is exactly to its tolerance limit and it appear to be in satisfactory condition use plastigauge for checking bearing surface clearance 1 0 5 general information ball and roller bearing general when removing and installing bearing ensure that the following practice are observed to ensure component serviceability remove all trace of lubricant from bearing under inspection by cleaning with a suitable degreasant maintain absolute cleanliness throughout operation hold inner race of bearing between finger and thumb of one hand and spin outer race to check that it revolve absolutely smoothly repeat holding outer race and spinning inner race rotate outer ring gently with a reciprocating motion while holding inner ring feel for any check or obstruction to rotation reject bearing if action is not perfectly smooth lubricate bearing with generous amount of lubricant appropriate to installation inspect shaft and bearing housing for discoloration or other marking which indicate movement between bearing and housing ensure that shaft and housing are clean and free from burr before fitting bearing if one bearing of a pair show an imperfection it is advisable to replace both with new bearing an exception could be if the faulty bearing had covered a low mileage and it can be established that damage is confined to only one bearing never refit a ball or roller bearing without first ensuring that it is in a fully serviceable condition when hub bearing are removed or displaced new bearing must be fitted do not attempt to refit the old hub bearing when fitting a bearing to a shaft only apply force to the inner ring of the bearing when fitting a bearing into a housing only apply force to the outer ring of the bearing in the case of grease lubricated bearing eg hub bearing fill the space between bearing and outer seal with the recommended grade of grease before fitting seal alway mark component of separable bearing eg taper roller bearing when dismantling to ensure correct reassembly never fit new roller in a used outer ring alway fit a complete new bearing assembly 1 0 6 general information oil seal general alway renew oil seal which have been removed from their working location whether as an individual component or as part of an assembly never use a seal which has been improperly stored or handled such as hung on a hook or nail carefully examine seal before fitting to ensure that it is clean and undamaged ensure the surface on which the new seal is to run is free of burr or scratch renew the component if the original sealing surface cannot be completely restored protect the seal from any surface which it has to pass when being fitted use a protective sleeve or tape to cover the relevant surface lubricate the sealing lip with a recommended lubricant before use to prevent damage during initial use on dual lipped seal smear the area between the lip with grease ifasealspringisprovided ensurethatitisfittedcorrectly place lip of seal toward fluid to be sealed and slide into position on shaft use fitting sleeve where possible to protect sealing lip from damage by sharp corner thread or spline if a fitting sleeve is not available use plastic tube or tape to prevent damage to the sealing lip grease outside diameter of seal place square to housing recess and press into position using great care and if possible a bell piece to ensure that seal is not tilted in some case it may be preferable to fit seal to housing before fitting to shaft never let weight of unsupported shaft rest in seal use the recommended service tool to fit an oil seal if the correct service tool is not available use a suitable tube approximately 0 4 mm 0 015 in smaller than the outside diameter of the seal use a hammer very gently on drift if a suitable press is not available use the recommended service tool to fit an oil seal if the correct service tool is not available use a suitable tube approximately 0 4 mm 0 015 in smaller than the outside diameter of the seal use a hammer very gently on drift if a suitable press is not available 1 0 7 general information joint and joint face general fit joint dry unless specified otherwise alway use the correct gasket as specified when sealing compound is used apply in a thin uniform film to metal surface take care to prevent sealing compound from entering oilway pipe or blind tapped hole if gasket and or sealing compound is recommended for use remove all trace of old sealing compound prior to reassembly do not use a tool which will damage the joint face and smooth out any scratch or burr using an oil stone prior to reassembly blow through any pipe channel or crevice with compressed air locking device general alway replace locking device with one of the same design tab washer alway release locking tab and fit new locking washer do not re use locking tab locking nut alway use a backing spanner when loosening or tightening locking nut brake and fuel pipe union roll pin alway fit new roll pin of an interference fit in the hole circlip alway fit new circlip of the correct size for the groove key and keyway remove burr from edge of keyway with a fine file and clean thoroughly before attempting to refit key cleanandinspectkeyclosely keysaresuitableforrefittingonly if indistinguishable from new as any indentation may indicate the onset of wear split pin alway fit new split pin of the correct size for the hole in the bolt or stud 1 0 8 general information screw thread general metric thread to iso standard are used damaged nut bolt and screw must alway be discarded cleaning damaged thread with a die or tap impair the strength and fit of the thread and is not recommended alway ensure that replacement bolt are at least equal in strength to those replaced castellated nut must not be loosened to accept a split pin except in recommended case when this form part of an adjustment do not allow oil or grease to enter blind threaded hole the hydraulic action on screwing in the bolt or stud could split the housing alway tighten a nut or bolt to the recommended torque figure damaged or corroded thread can affect the torque reading to check or re tighten a bolt or screw to a specified torque figure first loosen a quarter of a turn then re tighten to the correct torque figure oil thread lightly before tightening to ensure a free running thread except in the case of thread treated with sealant lubricant and selflocking nut fastener identification bolt identification an iso metric bolt or screw made of steel and larger than 6 mm in diameter can be identified by either of the symbol iso m or m embossed or indented on top of the bolt head inadditiontomarksidentifyingthemanufacturer thetopofthe bolt head is also marked with symbol indicating the strength grade e g 8 8 10 9 12 9 14 9 as an alternative some bolt and screw have the m and strength grade symbol stamped on the flat of the hexagon encapsulated bolt and screw encapsulated bolt and screw have a microencapsulated locking agent pre applied to the thread they are identified by a coloured section which extend 360 around the thread the locking agent is released and activated by the assembly process and is then chemically cured to provide the locking action unless a specific repair procedure state otherwise encapsulated bolt may be reused providing the thread are undamaged and the following procedure is adopted remove loose adhesive from the bolt and housing thread ensure thread are clean and free of oil and grease apply an approved locking agent 1 0 9 general information an encapsulated bolt may be replaced with a bolt of equivalent specification provided it is treated with an approved locking agent self locking bolt and screw self locking bolt and screw i e nylon patched or trilobular thread can be reused providing resistance can be felt when the locking portion enter the female thread nylon patched bolt and screw have a locking agent pre applied to the thread they are identified by the presence of a coloured section of thread which extend for up to 180 around the thread trilobular bolt i e powerlok have a special thread form which create a slight interference with the thread of the hole or nut into which it is screwed do not reuse self locking fastener in critical location e g enginebearing flywheel alwaysusethecorrectreplacement self locking nut bolt or screw do not fit non self locking fastener in application where a self locking nut bolt or screw is specified nut identification a a nut with an iso metric thread is marked on one face or on one of the flat of the hexagon with the strength grade symbol 8 12 or 14 some nut with a strength grade 4 5 or 6 are also marked and some have the metric symbol m on the flat opposite the strength grade marking when tightening a slotted or castellated nut never loosen it to insert a split pin except where recommended as part of an adjustment if difficulty is experienced alternative washer or nut should be selected or the washer thickness reduced where bearing preload is involved nut should be tightened in accordance with special instruction self locking nut self locking nut e g nylon insert or deferred thread nut can be reused providing resistance can be felt when the locking portion of the nut pass over the thread of the bolt or stud where self locking nut have been removed it is advisable to replace them with new one of the same type don t use non self lock nut in the area where self lock nut must be used 1 0 10 general information flexible pipe and hose general when removing and installing flexible hydraulic pipe and hose ensure that the following practice are observed to ensure component serviceability clean end fitting and the area surrounding them as thoroughly as possible obtain appropriate plug or cap before detaching hose end fitting so that the port can be immediately covered to prevent the ingress of dirt clean hose externally and blow through with airline examine carefully for crack separation of ply security of end fitting and external damage reject any faulty hose when refitting a hose ensure that no unnecessary bend are introduced and that hose is not twisted before or during tightening of union nut fit a cap to seal a hydraulic union and a plug to its socket after removal to prevent ingress of dirt absolute cleanliness must be observed with hydraulic component at all time after any work on hydraulic system carefully inspect for leak underneath the vehicle while a second operator apply maximum brake pressure to the brake engine running and operate the steering fuel system hose all fuel hose are made up of two lamination an armoured rubber outer sleeve and an inner viton core if any of the fuel system hose have been disconnected it is imperative that the internal bore is inspected to ensure that the viton lining has not become separated from the armoured outer sleeve a new hose must be fitted if separation is evident cooling system hose the following precaution must be followed to ensure that integrity of cooling hose and their connection to system component are maintained hose orientation and connection correct orientation of cooling hose is important in ensuring that the hose doe not become fatigued or damaged through contact with adjacent component where timing mark 1 are provided on the hose and corresponding connection these must be used to ensure correct orientation hose must be pushed fully onto their connection point usually a moulded form 2 on the stub pipe provide a positive indicator hose clip marking 1 are usually provided on the hose to indicate the correct clip position if no marking are provided position the clip directly behind the retaining lip at the end of the stub as shown worm drive clip should be oriented with the crimped side of the drive housing 2 facing toward the end of the hose or the hose may become pinched between the clip and the stub pipe retaining lip worm drive clip should be tightened to 3 nm unless otherwise stated ensure that hose clip do not foul adjacent component heat protection alway ensure that heatshield and protective sheathing are in good condition replace if damage is evident particular care must be taken when routing hose close to hot engine component such as the exhaust manifold and the exhaust gas recirculation egr pipe hose will relax and deflect slightly 1 0 11 general information when hot ensure this movement is taken into account when routing and securing hose fuel handling precaution general fuel vapour is highly flammable and in confined space is also explosive and toxic the vapour is heavier than air and will alway fall to the lowest level the vapour can be easily distributed throughout a workshop by air current consequently even a small spillage of fuel is potentially very dangerous the following information provide basic precaution which must be observed if fuel is to be handled safely it also outline other area of risk which must not be ignored this information is issued for basic guidance only if in doubt consult your local fire officer alway have a fire extinguisher containing foam co2 gas or powder close at hand when handling or draining fuel or when dismantling fuel system fire extinguisher should also be located in area where fuel container are stored alway disconnect the vehicle battery before carrying out dismantling or draining work on a fuel system whenever fuel is being handled drained or stored or when fuel system are being dismantled all form of ignition must be extinguished or removed any lead lamp must be flameproof and kept clear of spillage warning no one should be permitted to repair component associated with fuel without first having specialist training warning do not remove fuel system component while the vehicle is over a pit fuel tank draining fuel tank draining should be carried out in accordance with the procedure outlined in the fuel delivery section of this manual and observing the following precaution warning do not remove fuel system component while the vehicle is over a pit extraction or draining of fuel must be carried out in a well ventilated area the capacity of container must be more than adequate for the amount of fuel to be extracted or drained the container should be clearly marked with its content and placed in a safe storage area which meet the requirement of local authority regulation fuel tank removal when the fuel line is secured to the fuel tank outlet by a spring steel clip the clip must be released before the fuel line is disconnected or the fuel tank is removed this procedure will avoid the possibility of fume in the fuel tank being ignited when the clip is released 1 0 12 general information as an added precaution fuel tank should have a fuel vapour warning label attached to them as soon as they are removed from the vehicle fuel tank repair no attempt should be made to repair a plastic fuel tank if the structure of the tank is damaged a new tank must be fitted body repair plastic fuel pipe are particularly susceptible to heat even at relatively low temperature and can be melted by heat conducted from some distance away when body repair involve the use of heat all fuel pipe which run in the vicinity of the repair area must be removed and the tank outlet plugged warning if welding is to be carried out in the vicinity of thefueltank thefuelsystemmustbedrainedandthetank removed before welding commence electrical precaution general the following guideline are intended to ensure the safety of the operator while preventing damage to the electrical and electronic component fitted to the vehicle where necessary specific precaution are detailed in the individual procedure of this manual equipment prior to commencing any test procedure on the vehicle ensure that the relevant test equipment is working correctly and any harness or connector are in good condition it is particularly important to check the condition of the lead and plug of main operated equipment polarity never reverse connect the vehicle battery and alway ensure the correct polarity when connecting test equipment high voltage circuit whenever disconnecting live ht circuit alway use insulated plier and never allow the open end of the ht lead to contact other component particularly ecus exercise caution when measuring the voltage on the coil terminal while the engine is running high voltage spike can occur on these terminal connector and harness the engine compartment of a vehicle is a particularly hostile environment for electrical component and connector alway ensure electrically related item are dry and oil freebeforedisconnectingandconnectingtestequipment ensure disconnected multiplug and sensor are protected from being contaminated with oil coolant or other solution contamination could impair performance or result in catastrophic failure never force connector apart using tool to prise apart or by pulling on the wiring harness alway ensure locking tab are disengaged before disconnection and match orientation to enable correct reconnection ensure that any protection cover insulation etc is replaced if disturbed having confirmed a component to be faulty switch off the ignition and disconnect the battery remove the component and support the disconnected harness when replacing the component keep oily hand away from electrical connection area and push connector home until any locking tab fully engage 1 0 13 general information battery disconnection before disconnecting the battery disable the alarm system and switch off all electrical equipment caution to prevent damage to electrical component alway disconnect the battery when working on the vehicle s electrical system the ground lead must be disconnected first and reconnected last caution alway ensure that battery lead are routed correctly and are not close to any potential chafing point battery charging alway ensure any battery charging area is well ventilated and thateveryprecautionistakentoavoidnakedflamesandspark discipline remove the handset prior to making any connection or disconnection in the system to prevent electrical surge caused by disconnecting live connection damaging electronic component ensure hand and work surface are clean and free of grease swarf etc grease collect dirt which can cause electrical tracking shortcircuit or high resistance contact when handling printed circuit board treat with care and hold by the edge only note that some electronic component are susceptible to body static connector should never be subjected to forced removal or refit especially inter board connector damaged contact can cause short circuit and open circuit fault condition prior to commencing test and periodically during a test touch a good vehicle body earth to discharge static charge some electronic component are vulnerable to the static electricity that may be generated by the operator electrical multiplug lubricate in order to prevent corrosion some multiplug under bonnet and carpet is wiped special lubricate in factory if these is destroyed in maintaining repair and replacing process special lubricate should be newly wiped supplementary restraint system precaution general precaution the srs system contain component which could be potentially hazardous to the service engineer if not serviced and handled correctly the following guideline are intended to alert the service engineer to potential source of danger and emphasise the importance of ensuring the integrity of srs component fitted to the vehicle warning it is imperative that before any work is undertaken on the srs system the appropriate information is read thoroughly warning it is imperative that before any work is undertaken on the srs system the appropriate information is read thoroughly warning the airbag module contain sodium azide which is poisonous and extremely flammable contact with water acid or heavy metal may produce harmful or explosive compound do not dismantle incinerate or bring into contact with electricity before the unit has been deployed warning alway replace a seat belt assembly that has withstood the strain of a severe vehicle impact or if the webbing show sign of fraying warning alway disconnect the vehicle battery before carrying out any electric welding on a vehicle fitted with an srs system caution do not expose an airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner to heat exceeding 85 it should be noted that these precaution are not restricted to operation performed when servicing the srs system the same care must be exercised when working on ancillary system and component located in the vicinity of the srs component these include but are not limited to steering system steering wheel airbag rotary coupler precaution warning the srs component are sensitive and potentially hazardous if not handled correctly alway comply with the following handling precaution ecu and airbag must be stored in a cabinet in a dry room at normal room temperature not exceeding 85 c ensure that heating fire water and other chemical corrosive substance cannot contaminate the stored srs component for safety reason do not store inflammable good close to the area where the srs component are stored keep new airbag module in the original packaging until just before fitting the storage area must comply with all legal requirement it must have suitable fire extinguisher or other fire extinguishing equipment never drop an srs component the airbag diagnostic control unit is a particularly shock sensitive device and must be handled with extreme care never wrap your arm around an airbag module if an airbag module has to be carried hold it by the cover with the cover uppermost and the base away from your body never uppermost if it is stored deployment side down accidental deployment will propel the airbag module with enough force to cause serious injury warning airbag module and seat belt pre tensioner are classed as explosive device for overnight and longer term storage they must be stored in a secure steel cabinet which has been approved as suitable for the purpose and has been registered by the local authority warning when recovering the seat belt pretensioner do not aim the piston pipe at yourself or other keep the piston facing the ground hold the case instead of the seat belt warning store the airbag module or seat belt pre tensioner in a designated storage area caution improper handling or storage can internally damage the airbag module making it inoperative if you suspect the airbag module has been damaged install a new module and refer to thedeployment disposal procedure for disposal of the damaged module srs harness and connector alway observe the following precaution srs with
