conditioning system precaution general the air conditioning system contain fluid and component which could be potentially hazardous to the service engineer or the environment if not serviced and handled correctly the following guideline are intended to alert the service engineer to potential source of danger and emphasise the importance of ensuring the integrity of the air conditioning operating condition and component fitted to the vehicle where necessary additional specific precaution are detailed in the relevant section of this manual which should be referred to prior to commencing repair operation therefrigerantusedintheairconditioningsystemishfc 134a hydrofluorocarbon r134a warning servicing must only be carried out by personnel familiar with both the vehicle system and the charging and testing equipment all operation must be carried out in a well ventilated area away from open flame and heat source warning r134a is a hazardous liquid and when handled incorrectly can cause serious injury suitable protective clothing consisting of face protection heat proof glove rubber boot and rubber apron or waterproof overall must be worn when carrying out operation on the air conditioning system remedial action if an accident involving r134a should occur conduct the following remedial action if liquid r134a enter the eye do not rub it gently run large quantity of eye wash over affected eye to raise the temperature if an eye wash is not available cool clean water may be used to flush the eye after rinsing cover the eye with a clean pad and seek immediate medical attention if liquid r134a is splashed onto the skin run large quantity of water over the affected area to raise the temperature implement the same action if the skin come in contact with discharging cylinder wrap the contaminated body part in blanket or similar material and seek immediate medical attention if the debilitating effect of inhalation of r134a vapour is suspected seek fresh air if the affected person is unconscious move them away from the contaminated area to fresh air and apply artificial respiration and or oxygen and seek immediate medical attention 1 0 21 general information warning due to its low evaporating temperature r134a must be handled with care r134a splashed on any part of the body will cause immediate freezing of that area also refrigerant cylinder and replenishment trolley when discharging will freeze skin to them if contact is made service precaution observe the following precaution when handling component used in the air conditioning system air conditioning unit must not be lifted by their hose pipe or capillary line hose and line must not be subjected to any twist or stress the efficiency of the system will be impaired by kink or restriction ensure that hose are correctly positioned before tightening coupling and ensure that all clip and support are utilised flexible hose should not be positioned close to the exhaust manifold less than 100 mm unless protected by heat shielding completed assembly must be checked for refrigeration line touching metal panel any direct contact of component and panel may transmit noise and so must be eliminated the appropriate torque wrench must be used when tightening refrigerant connection to the stipulated value an additional spanner must be used to hold the union to prevent twisting of the pipe when tightening connection before connecting any hose or pipe ensure that refrigerant oil is applied to the seat of the new o ring but not to the thread of the connection all protective plug must remain in place to seal the component until immediately prior to connection ensure component are at room temperature before uncapping to prevent condensation of moisture from the air that enter it component must not remain uncapped for longer than 15 minute in the event of a delay the cap must be fitted when disconnecting immediately cap all air conditioning pipe to prevent ingress of dirt and moisture into the system the receiver drier contain desiccant which absorb moisture it must be positively sealed at all time a receiver drier that has been left uncapped must not be used fit a new unit the receiver drier should be the last component connected to the system to ensure optimum dehydration and maximum moisture protection of the system whenever the refrigerant system is opened the receiver drier must be renewed immediately before evacuating and recharging the system use alcohol and a clean lint free cloth to clean dirty connection ensure that all new part fitted are marked for use with r134a refrigerant oil refrigerant oil easily absorb water and must not be stored for long period do not pour unused refrigerant oil back into the container alway use an approved refrigerant oil when replacing component in the a c system drain the refrigerant oil from the component being replaced into a graduated container on assembly add the quantity of refrigerant oil drained to the new component compressor anewcompressorissealedandpressurisedwithnitrogenga when fitting a new compressor slowly release the sealing cap gas pressure should be heard to vent as the seal is broken caution do not remove the cap s until immediately prior to connecting the air conditioning pipe to the compressor rapid refrigerant discharge iftheairconditioningsystemisinvolvedinaccidentdamageand the system is punctured the refrigerant will discharge rapidly the rapid discharge of refrigerant will also result in the loss of most of the oil from the system the compressor must be removed and all the remaining oil in the compressor drained and refilled as instructed in the air conditioning section of this manual precaution for refrigerant recovery recycling and recharging when the air conditioning system is recharged any existing refrigerant is first recovered from the system and recycled the system is then charged with the required weight of refrigerant and volume of refrigerant oil warning refrigerant must alway be recycled before re use to ensure that the purity of the refrigerant is high enough for safe use in the air conditioning system warning recycling should alway be carried out with equipment which is design certified by underwriter laboratory inc for compliance with sae j1991 other equipment may not recycle refrigerant to the required level of purity warning a r134a refrigerant recovery recycling recharging station must not be used with any other type of refrigerant 1 0 22 general information warning refrigerant r134a from domestic and commercial source must not be used in motor vehicle air conditioning system caution the system must be evacuated immediately beforerechargingcommence delaybetweenevacuation and recharging is not permitted air conditioning compressor replacement replacement instruction a new compressor is supplied filled with a full charge of refrigerant oil the new compressor must be drained and a calculated quantity of oil added before fitting to calculate the quantity of oil to be added carry out the following procedure 1 remove the filler drain plug from the old compressor 2 invert the compressor and gravity drain the oil into a calibrated measuring cylinder rotate the compressor clutch to ensure the compressor is completely drained 3 record the quantity of oil drained discard the oil 4 remove the filler drain plug from the new compressor 5 invert the compressor and gravity drain the oil into a calibrated measuring cylinder rotate the compressor clutch to ensure the compressor is completely drained 6 add the same amount of oil drained from the old compressor to the new compressor 7 discard the remaining oil drained from the new compressor 8 fit and tighten the compressor filler drain plug 1 0 23 general roadside assistance instruction for roadside assistance it is recommended that a rescue trailer or a two wheel rescue vehicle shall be used in emergency use a towing vehicle with front towing point front towing eye in the front of the vehicle removable towing eye can be used for rescue operation the front towing eye is usually kept in the vehicle toolbox and should be put back after using when fitting the towing eye remove the towing eye cover at the right side of the front bumper first then screw the towing eye into the mounting position of the front bumper assembly bumper beam manual transmission for four wheel towing operate from the front via the towing eye before towing release the hand brake put the shift lever at the neutral gear and the ignition switch at on position the towing speed shall not exceed 50km h 30mph the following precaution must be taken in order to ensure that the vehicle still has steering capability when it is towed it is necessary to insert the ignition key and 1 0 27 general information lifting towing put it to on position this state must be maintained when the vehicle is moving when the transmission or drive shaft fail the vehicle could not be towed ensure that the shift lever is at the neutral gear and parking brake is disabled if the engine is not in operation during towing more care must be taken in brake and steering operation caution if the transmission case occur malfunction and the transmission fluid level is too low the four wheel of the vehicle cannot be towed absolutely automatic transmission generally vehicle with automatic transmission should be towed with the front wheel off the road with front wheel hanged up caution a rear suspended tow must not be used for vehicle with an automatic transmission as serious damage will be caused to the transmission in order to ensure that the vehicle still has steering capability when it is towed it is necessary to insert the ignition key and put it to on position this state must be maintained when the vehicle is moving for vehicle with automatic transmission when towing the four wheel from the front attention must be paid to the following warning when the vehicle is being towed the selector must be at n thevehiclemustnotbetowedatspeedsgreaterthan 30 km h or for a distance exceeding 50 km do not tow if the gearbox or a drive shaft is faulty ensure the gear lever is in neutral and the parking brake is released remember that greater effort than normal will be necessary to operate the brake and steering if the vehicle is being towed without the engine running caution do not tow on four wheel if the gearbox or drive shaft are touching the body or frame note a vehicle with an automatic transmission cannot be started by towing or pushing vehicle shipping if your vehicle need to be shipped we recommend you to use special transporter to ship fix the vehicle on the transporter as follow apply the parking brake and place the transmission shift lever in p or n position position the brake pad on the front and rear wheel fit the tether around the tyre and secure it to the trailer tighten the tether to fix the vehicle firmly 1 0 28 lubrication system only the antifreeze with oat antiseptic can be used do not use other type of antifreeze to supplement or refill the cooling system in emergency if the antifreeze of this type is unavailable the clean water can be used to fill the cooling system however this may lower the frost protection capability the correct antifreeze concentration must be recovered as soon as possible at a prominent position of the vehicle to show the type of antifreezeusedinthecoolingsystem sothatcorrectantifreeze can be used for next refill concentration of antifreeze fluid the overall antifreeze concentration shall not be less than 50 of the volume meanwhile make sure the antisepsis property of the coolant antifreeze concentration more than 60 is not recommended as it will weaken the cooling efficiency the antifreeze capacity recommended in the following table shall provide frost protection at 37 40 engine concentration 50 capacity of antifreeze fluid 2 8l brake fluid use brake fluid dot 4 only pas fluid use the liquid that conform to the dexron iii specification 1 0 29 general information lubrication maintenance lubricating fluid overview the engine and other lubrication system are filled with high performance lubricating fluid to extend the service life caution alway use a high quality oil of the correct viscosity range in the engine the use of oil of the incorrect specification can lead to high oil and fuel consumption and ultimately to damaged component the use of the engine oil of recommended specification containing additive may decrease the corrosive acid caused by combustion and prevent formation of residue these residue may block the oil passage other engine oil additive should not be used repair and maintenance must be carried out within the specified interval engine oil 1 5l engine use 5w 30 engine oil in compliance with acea a1 b1 specification 5w 30 engine oil in compliance with acea a5 b5 specification and 0w 20 engine oil in thermostatthermostatthermostat 1 water pump 2 thermostat 3 water pipe 1 0 52 engine mechanical engine fuel rail assembly 1 fuel rail assembly 2 fuel injector 3 bolt fuel rail 4 o ring fuel injector 5 clip fuel injector 1 0 53 engine engine mechanical description overview the nse engine feature in line 4 cylinder 16 valve dohc timing chain drive and electronically controlled multi point fuel injection system it is mainly composed of cylinder block cylinder head crankshaft connecting rod and camshaft the cylinder head is made of cast aluminum and connected with the cast iron cylinder block through the multi layer cylinder gasket by using 10 high strength bolt and there are 3 additional high strength bolt directly connected with the cast iron cylinder block providing high overall strength and reliable structure cylinder head component the cylinder head adopt 4 valve per cylinder structure with a spark plug mounted at the centre of the combustion chamber due to adoption of overhead valve structure powerful whirlpool will be formed in the combustion chamber to make the mixed gas evenly distributed which is good for transmission of flame to improve combustion condition so as to enhance the fuel economy power performance and emission performance of the engine the mechanical tappet are fitted at the top of each valve and directly driven by the camshaft the bottom boss of the mechanical tappet are grouped by size and the valve clearance is optionally fitted with appropriate grouped mechanical tappet the valve stem oil seal is press fitted on the cylinder head oil seal seat and can be used as the valve spring seat to support the valve spring the exhaust valve is of a carbon deposit removal type and the valve stem with a machined side can clean the carbon buildup at the end of valve duct and in combustion chamber so as to prevent valve from seizing the metal cylinder gasket is of a multi layer structure the cylinder opening adopt a pressing ring structure which enhance sealing at cylinder opening local rubber coat technology is adopted to ensure the sealing performance there are four oil duct at the front bearing cap of the camshaft one oil inlet duct one oil return duct and two operating oil duct oil enter the oil control solenoid valve via the oil duct the tail end of the intake exhaust camshaft is equipped with a camshaft signal wheel to provide signal for the camshaft position sensor there is a timing pin hole on the camshaft for controlling camshaft timing phase during assembly a phase modulator is fitted at the drive end of the intake and exhaust camshaft which is fixed by bolt main part of the phase modulator are made of aluminum the housing stator driven by timing chain is connected with inner rotor through five blade fixed at camshaft end in a hydraulic way when the engine is at rest pin of an elastic load lock both halve of the modulator when the engine operate they are loosened in a hydraulic way to ensure stable starting two oil control valve are fitted on the front camshaft bearing cap and fixed with an o ring and a bolt respectively the control valve is a four way proportioning valve and consist of an oil inlet port an oil return port and two oil duct connected with the phase modulator working chamber cylinder block component the cylinder block is constructed from cast iron material ht250 this provide high overall strength reducing the likelihood of deformation the cylinder liner is formed by cylinder hole honing y shaped water passage are processed between two adjacent cylinder to ensure heat dissipation effect of the cylinder block the aluminum alloy thermal expansion piston is provided with a full floating piston pin which can rotate freely to reduce the friction resistance and the generation and delivery of heat so that the reliability and smoothness of high speed of engine rotation can be enhanced the forging steel connecting rod has the structure of the h section the big end of connecting rod move on the bearing shell of crankshaft journal the connecting rod adopt forging cracking process the crankshaft has four balance weight and is supported by five bearing shell the end float is controlled by the thrust washer on the top of the central main bearing shell the radial clearance of the bearing shell is optional which is controlled by the bearing shell of two different grade the oil groove offered in the upper half part of the main bearing shell supply oil to the connecting rod big end bearing shell through the oil hole in the crankshaft lower crankcase component the lower crankcase assembly consist of lower crankcase oil pump assembly oil strainer and oil filter the aluminum lower crankcase is designed into a shape that is suitable for accumulating oil around the strainer the oil pump and oil strainer are fitted to bottom of crankcase after being combined and oil pass through the oil strainer with screen and then the oil filter by pressurization of the oil pump clean oil is distributed to the main bearing and cylinder head via the main oil passage of the engine block to lubricate the crankshaft connecting rod and camshaft etc the mating surface of the lower crankcase and engine block is sealed with sealant and secured with bolt and the oil drain plug is located attheleftsideofthelowercrankcase inthedirectionofengine front end surface 1 0 54 engine mechanical engine engine accessory the accessory are a single 5 ribbed belt drive system driven by crankshaft pulley the system drive the water pump alternator and a c compressor and tension the belt via a damping tensioner crankcase ventilation the ventilation system redirect the exhaust gas escaped to the crankcase back into the intake system with the help of intake system vacuum level then send it to the cylinder for combustion so as to reduce the air pollution oil and gas in the crankcase are separated via an oil separating device in the camshaft cover and oil flow back to the crankcase 1 0 55 engine engine mechanical operation lubricating oil circuit 1 cylinder head oil duct 2 cylinder block main oil duct 3 crankshaft cross oil duct 4 engine oil inlet pipe 5 engine oil filter 6 engine oil pump 7 main oil duct 8 timing chain tensioner 9 oil control valve the lubrication system is a full flow filtration forced oil supply system oil is drawn from the oil pan through the oil strainer by the oil pump and the oil strainer is equipped with a screen which can prevent impurity from entering the oil pump the oil pump with a pressure limiting valve is driven by the crankshaft via a belt when the oil pressure reach a certain value the pressure relief valve will open the excess oil return to the inlet side of oil pump and this help maintain the oil pressure within a certain operating pressure range and ensure the normal operation of engine oil flowing from the oil pump outlet enter into the full flow oil filter the full flow oil filter is directly fitted on the oil pan the oil filter is equipped with a by pass valve when the filter element is blocked this valve will open and ensure the free flow of oil but the oil filter shall be replaced as soon as possible then theoilentersintothemainoilpassage andlubricatesthe main bearing shell directly via the cross oil circuit the cross oil circuit on the crankshaft deliver the oil from the main bearing shell to the connecting rod big end bearing shell the cylinder head has two side oil passage connecting with the main oil passage of the engine block after entering the side oil passage the oil flow into 2 horizontal oil passage of the standard length and is supplied to each mechanical tappet and camshaft journal 1 0 56 engine mechanical engine the oil splashed by the crankshaft lubricate the cylinder wall the oil collected by the lower side of the piston crown lubricate the connecting rod small end and oil is supplied to the piston pin from the connecting rod small end through the oil torque 5 fit the bold fixing the exhaust manifold shell tighten to 7 10nm and check the torque 1 0 102 engine mechanical engine cylinder pressure test test 1 run the engine until it reach normal operating temperature the battery must be fully charged or almost fully charged 2 stop the engine 3 disable the fuel system e g pull out the harness connector of the fuel pump 4 start the engine again until it stop so as to consume the fuel remaining in the fuel rail 5 disable the ignition system e g disengage the ignition coil for each cylinder and engine harness connector 6 remove the ignition coil for each cylinder 7 remove the spark plug for all cylinder 8 measure the compression pressure of the engine with the following procedure a fit the adapter of the spark plug hole into the spark plug hole b fit the compression pressure gauge into the adapter of the spark plug hole c fully depress the accelerator pedal d with assistance turn the key to start the starter until no increased pressure is indicated by the compression pressure gauge note do not keep the starter motor running for 5s at a time and the interval for next start shall not be less than 10s e check and record the reading on the pressure gauge f remove the compression pressure gauge from the adapter of the spark plug hole g remove the adapter of the spark plug hole from the spark plug hole 9 repeat step 8 to test the pressure of other cylinder the lower limit value for the engine cylinder under normal pressure temperature is 7 bar 10 difference between each cylinder pressure and average pressure for each cylinder for gasoline engine it shall not be greater than 8 11 here are some example of measurement a when the compression pressure is normal the compression pressure of each cylinder will increase quickly and evenly to the specified value b if the low compression pressure is caused by piston ring the compression pressure will drop in the 1st stroke and gradually increase in the subsequent stroke however it will never reach the normal value after oil refilling the compression pressure will increase substantially refill engine oil about 15ml through the spark plug hole and retest the cylinder pressure c if the low compression pressure is caused by valve low compression pressure will be measured in the 1st stroke and it will not increase even when the oil is refilled d the same consequence can be resulted from leaking cylinder head gasket and worn valve but for the crankcase the problem may be due to engine coolant in addition a leaking cylinder head gasket will result in low compression pressure reading of the cylinder in pair 12 resume the ignition system 13 resume the fuel system 1 0 103 engine engine mechanical special tool tool number description picture t10007 pipeline 15 clamp cooling system pipeline 16 pipeline assembly heater to engine 17 clamp radiator expansion tank overflow pipe 18 pipeline assembly engine to heater 19 clamp heater to engine pipeline 20 clamp heater to engine pipeline 21 clip engine expansion tank overflow pipe 22 engine overflow pipe 1 0 109 engine engine cooling system description overview the cooling system circulate the engine coolant in the loop of engine and heater core the coolant is the mixture of 50 water and 50 organic acid technique oat which help to maintain the optimum operating temperature of the engine when the environment and engine working condition are different caution engine coolant will damage paint finished surface if spilled immediately rinse area with plenty of water the coolant shall be replaced regularly to keep its optimum efficiency and corrosion resistance performance component of cooling system coolant pump the coolant circulate through the rotor type pump on the rear upper part of the engine crankcase drive which is sealed to the cylinder block by an o ring and driven by the crankshaft timing belt coolant hose the rubber coolant hose deliver coolant among all component the coolant hose is fixed to each component by spring clamp the heater core complete the fluid inlet by connecting the outlet elbow of the engine to the inlet pipe of the inlet hard pipe of heater core passing through the dash panel then goe to oil cooler water inlet pipe through the water outlet pipe of heater core and flow back to the engine cylinder block through the oil cooler radiator pressure seal cap radiator pressure cap is mounted on the top of the radiator which is equipped with a relief valve and vacuum valve when the system pressure is within 0 93 1 23 bar relief valve open to release system pressure when the system pressure is within 0 0 098 bar vacuum valve open and fluid is replenished to the system from outside so that the cooling system can run within a certain pressure range expansion tank the coolant expansion tank is fitted on the left side of the cooling module cooling fan one overflow return pipe is connected to the pressure cover at the top of the radiator through the bottom part of the expansion tank when the coolant is expanded during warm up additional coolant in the cooling system will flow back to the expansion tank through the overflow return pipe when the coolant in the cooling system is insufficient coolant in the expansion tank will flow back to the cooling system through the overflow return pipe thermostat this thermostat is a wax type component with pressure relief characteristic it is located in the sealing housing on engine pipe when starting a cold engine the coolant temperature is low the wax type component of the thermostat is in solid state and the thermostat valve switch off under the action of the spring at this point the cooling system is in a small circulation status namely the coolant cannot enter into the radiator for circulation but it can circulate between the bypass circuit and heater core when the coolant temperature rise the wax type component start melting into liquid state gradually followed by the increase of volume then the thermostat valve will open gradually under the action of the push rod at this point the cooling system is in a big circulation status the coolant will flow into the cylinder block through the bottom hose of the radiator allow the hot coolant to flow through the radiator from the top hose to balance heat flow and keep the temperature at the optimal for engine operation when the thermostat is fully open the coolant flow through the radiator radiator and cooling fan the radiator is of aluminum longitudinal flow type with injection moulding water tank on both end for automatic transmission model automatic transmission oil cooler is locate in the water chamber below the radiator the cooling hose linked with transmission is connected to the bushing of the transmission oil cooler with nut the radiator support the cooling fan assembly air conditioning a c condenser the top of the radiator is located in the rubber bushing supported by the upper radiator beam bracket the bottom of the radiator is located in the rubber bushing supported by the bracket fixed on the front longitudinal beam sponge sealing strip fitted on both side of the radiator are used to guide the airflow through the radiator the condenser fixed by bolt is fitted on the front end of the radiator the cooling fan and motor assembly is fitted on the fan shroud at the rear of the radiator the suction type fan draw out air through the radiator beside the fan shroud is equipped with additional vent hole allowing more air flow through radiator when driving at high speed together with cooling fan low speed resistance of the cooling fan is fitted at the left side of the radiator engine coolant temperature ect sensor ectthe engine coolant temperature sensor is fitted on the engine cylinder block with a packaged negative temperature coefficient ntc thermistor contacting with the engine coolant which is part of the bleeder circuit the circuit is composed of a 5v rated power supply an engine control 1 0 110 engine cooling system engine module ecm internal resistance and a variable resistor related to temperature ect sensor 1 0 111 engine engine cooling system operation coolant flow 1 5 dvct engine the cooling system utiliz the heat conduction principle conducting heat from the engine component to the coolant when the engine is at low temperature the coolant pump will circulate the coolant among the engine block cylinder head and passenger compartment heater core the thermostat will be kept closed to prevent the coolant from flowing through the radiator however a small amount of coolant may pass through the bypass this help to prevent the accumulated pressure from getting too high when the engine reach the normal operating temperature the thermostat open the cool coolant enter into the cylinder block through the hose at the bottom of the radiator and the hot coolant will flow into the radiator through the hose at the top of the radiator so that the cool and hot coolant may be balanced to maintain the engine at the best temperature when the thermostat is fully open all coolant flow through the radiator the excess coolant produced by thermal expansion return to the expansion tank through the overflow pipe at the top of the radiator meanwhile the expansion tank will remove the air in the coolant the radiator pressure seal cap with relief valve and vacuum valve isolate the cooling system from the ambient atmosphere the coolant expand as the temperature rise and the pressure of the cooling system will increase accordingly the increased pressure raise the boiling point of the coolant allowing the engine to run at a higher more efficient operating temperature without the risk of coolant boiling the pressurization of the cooling system is limited so a pressure relief valve is provided on the expansion tank cap by this way when the max operating pressure is reached the excess pressure in the cooling system can be released cooling fan control policy the cooling fan will operate at high or low speed and the control is achieved by the ecm and relay unit ecm the ecm utiliz the information provided by the ect sensor bcm and a c pressure switch to control the fan speed and engine coolant temperature 1 0 112 engine cooling system engine during the normal operation the cooling fan will operate at the following coolant temperature cooling fan status low speed high speed on 100 112 off 94 106 when the a c system is working the cooling fan will operate under the following pressure condition cooling fan status high speed on 17 7 mpa off 13 7 mpa the cooling fan control policy include the engine idle speed adjustment whichcancompensatetheadditionalloadafterthe fan is on after the ignition key is removed cooling fan status low speed high speed on 104 110 off 100 106 the cooling fan will operate for 30s to provide additional cooling for the engine at high temperature in case of ect failure the fan will operate at low speed cooling fan relay unit the cooling fan relay unit is connected to the ecm by 2 wire and one wire provide the ground for the internal relay winding the cooling fan relay is powered by a 12 v power supply through fuse 13 of the engine compartment fuse box when the cooling fan run at low speed the low speed relay provide the power supply when the cooling fan run at high speed the high speed relay provide the power supply the cooling fan relay may have fault under the following condition high speed relay coil is open high speed relay coil is short circuited high speed relay contact has a high resistance high speed relay contact is open low speed relay coil is open low speed relay coil is short circuited low speed relay contact has a high resistance external wire is open external wire is short to the 12v power supply external wire is short to the ground external wire has a high resistance warning keep clear of the cooling fan the fan may start automatically without warning even after the engine is turned off 1 0 113 engine engine cooling system service procedure coolant drain and refill drain 1 remove the coolant expansion tank cap warning since injury such as scalding could be caused by escaping steam or coolant do not remove the filler cap from the coolant expansion tank while the system is hot 2 secure a proper container to collect the coolant 3 remove the bottom deflector bottombottombottom deflectordeflectordeflector removeremoveremove 4 open the drain valve at the bottom of the engine cooling system radiator 5 drain the engine cooling system refill 1 close the coolant drain valve at the bottom of the radiator 2 prepare the coolant of specified concentration fluidfluidfluid 3 open the expansion tank cap fill coolant to the expansion tank until the coolant level reach the neck of the expansion tank and keep still 4 when filling coolant to the vehicle with 1 5 dvct engine unscrew the bleeder bolt on the radiator when founding that the coolant is about to spill screw up the bolt 5 ensure that the a c is turned off 6 start the engine and allow it to operate at the speed of 1500 2500 rpm fill the expansion tank as required to keep the level just below the neck of the expansion tank when the coolant level reach the neck of the expansion tank due to thermal expansion fit the expansion tank cap 7 continuously run the engine at the speed of 1500 2500 rpm until the cooling fan operate and then run the engine at idle speed until the cooling fan stop 8 turn off the engine and cool it down for 30 minute 9 check the cooling system for leak 10 check whether the coolant level is between min and max if not open the expansion tank cap and top up the tank with coolant or drain it to be between min and max as required 1 0 114 engine cooling engine a can bus 1 0 133 engine engine fuel management description overview the engine management system ecm is applied to control each aspect of the engine operation the system receive input from various sensor and then utiliz them to determine output including the fuel volume delivered to the combustion chamber ignition time air supply control etc engine use bosch me 1 7 8 8 ecm which is a continuous multi point fuel injection system controlled by ecm and adopt the speed density principle combined with electronic throttle control the control intention of the engine management system are to enhance power performance reduce fuel consumption and exhaust pollution engine control module ecm the engine control module is the control centre of the fuel injection system it monitor continuously the information from various sensor control various system influencing the vehicle performance it also execute system diagnosis function it is able to identify the operation fault remind the driver with malfunction indicator lamp mil and store the dtc at indication fault position to facilitate the repair of the service personnel ecmend view and pin information of connector 1 5l 1 pin power supply 46 downstream oxygen sensor heater 47 ecm ground 4 48 ecm ground 3 the engine management system is composed of the following 6 component according to control principle external control engine air supply control system engine ignition control system engine fuel supply control system auxiliary 1 heat management auxiliary 2 external assistance external control the external control of the engine mainly include main relay ignition switch battery cancommunication immobiliser power supply the external control mainly refer to the power supply and communication one line of power supply of the engine control module ecm is directly connected to the power supply through fuse to ensure ecm is in powered state when the ignition switch is in on or ign position the engine control module can control the opening of the main relay or connect to ignition power supply the engine control can communicate with controller of other node on the network through can to ensure each performance of the system it can also be connected with the data link connector dlc to facilitate the diagnostic operation of the service personnel main relay the main relay is in the engine compartment fuse box it is a 4 pin relay adopting the din pin layout the relay doesn t contain the reverse electrode protection diode this function is performed by the ecm when the engine control module ecm detect that the ignition switch is in ign position it will output a low level signal control the relay to engage and the ubr pin of ecm is connected to the power supply voltage so as to meet the start condition failure the main relay may have failure occurred under the following condition relay winding short circuited relay winding open circuit relay winding high resistance relay contact normally open relay contact normally closed relay contact high resistance relay harness open circuit relay harness high resistance relay harness connected to the 12v power supply is short circuited relay harness short to ground ecmfailure to provide ground access in order to determine whether the main relay work normally the following check may be carried out sound check place the ignition key in on position to check whether the fuel pump will work for 2s whether the fuel injector or ignition coil is supported by power apply multimeter to check the voltage of battery if the above are verified it indicate that the main relay work normally cancommunication the engine management system apply can communication network for the communication between ecm and abs bcm and tcm and diagnose output through diagnostic interface immobiliser power supply when the ignition switch is turned to gear 2 the engine control module ecm and body control module bcm communicate through can bus for mutual information verification after verification bcm will control the operation of starter motor ecm will control ignition fuel injection and the engine will be started engine air supply control system the engine air supply control system mainly include electronic throttle body accelerator pedal module intake air pressure temperature sensor camshaft position sensor variable camshaft timing valve 1 0 136 engine fuel management engine if the ratio of the air to fuel mass is 14 7 1 the fuel can be burnt sufficiently and the engine is at the best working state for this moment as for air supply portion the optimal fuel air ratio is achieved mainly by the control of air inflow and the master stroke of oil cylinder electronic throttle the electronic throttle is composed of throttle throttle actuator dc motor and throttle position sensor etc ecm give instruction to make the dc motor act and affect the throttle opening through the drive mechanism there are two potentiometer acting as position sensor which are fit in reverse phase when the position of the throttle change the two resistance vary linearly one increase and the other decrease when a 5v voltage is added the pedal position will be transformed into the voltage output corresponding to the change of the resistance value these two potentiometer along with two potentiometer on theacceleratorpedalmoduleusedformonitoringthestrokeof the accelerator pedal form a part of whole electronic throttle control system monitoring function which can provide the redundancy protection expected by the system control throttle position sensor the throttle position sensor is used to provide throttle angle information for the ecm ecm can obtain the engine load information and operating condition such as start idle speed motored condition part load full load acceleration and deceleration information according to this information actually the throttle sensor is an angle potentiometer characterized by linear output the potentiometer rotor arm and throttle are fitted coaxially when the throttle rotate the potentiometer rotor arm will be driven to slide into a certain position and the potentiometer output a voltage signal proportional to the throttle position accelerator pedal module there are two potentiometer in the accelerator pedal module acting as sensor their resistance value will vary with the position change of the electronic accelerator pedal while the ecm can response precisely to the movement of the accelerator pedal therefore the movement of the accelerator pedal may be monitored because both potentiometer are fitted in the same phase when the accelerator pedal position change their resistance will increase or decrease linearly when a 5v voltage is added the pedal position will be transformed into the voltage output corresponding to the change of the resistance value these two potentiometer and two potentiometer fitted on the electronic throttle used for monitoring the position of the throttle form a part of whole electronic throttle control system monitoring function which can provide the redundancy protection expected by system control intake air pressure temperature sensor tmap the intake air pressure temperature sensor measure intake air manifold absolute pressure and intake air temperature and provide engine load and intake air temperature information the intake air pressure portion is measured by a piezoelectric type sensor which can provide the load signal to the controller in accordance with the pressure difference between atmosphere and intake manifold the controller provide a 5v voltage and feedback a 0 5v voltage to the controller depending on the intake air pressure the intake air temperature portion is measured by a ntc negative temperature coefficient type sensor whose resistance change with the intake air temperature and which transfer a voltage showing the change of the intake air temperature to the controller the pressure p is proportional to output voltage and the temperature is inversely proportional to output resistance camshaft position cmp sensor the camshaft position sensor is applied to cooperate with the rotational speed sensor in the circumstance without electricity distributor to provide the ecm with the camshaft phase information namely identify the compression top dead centre and exhaust top dead centre of the cylinder 1 the camshaft position sensor is composed of a hall sensor and a rotor made of steel plate the hall sensor is fixed and the rotor with a 36 arc boss is fitted on the camshaft the inner magnetic field of the hall sensor will change when the boss pass it which lead to the change of output signal voltage in this way the two top dead centre are identified failure cmpthe sensor or magnetic ring may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition cmpsensor gap fail to comply with the specification cmpsensor is contaminated by metallic debris cmpsensor hall effect semiconductor is damaged cmpsensor magnetic field is too weak cmpsensor harness is open circuit cmpsensor harness is short circuited cmpsensor harness has a high resistance magnetic ring is contaminated by metallic debris the precision of magnetic ring is low due to mechanical damage exhaust camshaft timing is incorrect intake camshaft timing is incorrect 1 0 137 engine engine fuel management engine oil temperature sensor the engine oil temperature sensor eot is applied to monitor the engine oil temperature and control variable camshaft timing valve i e vct valve the variable camshaft timing valve is adjusted at different temperature to obtain optimal intake air failure the eot sensor may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition system lack of oil external harness open circuit external harness short to ground external harness connected to the 5v power supply is short circuited external harness high resistance ecmfailure to provide ground access eotsensor thermistor damaged variable camshaft timing vct valve the engine control module output pulse width modulation pwm signal to drive the solenoid change in duty ratio may adjust the opening of the solenoid the vct system can under the condition of low speed reduce the fuel consumption and improve the performance of engine in the process of exhaust emission especially torque performance ecmit receive signal from the ckp sensor and cmp sensor with these signal the valve timing can be determined by ecm by consulting control chart then ecm operate the vct engine oil control valve installed on one side of cylinder head these solenoid valve control the oil to flow into phase modulator and change the relative position to the camshaft of the phase modulator so as to adjust the valve timing engine ignition control system the engine ignition control system mainly include ignition coil 1 2 3 4 engine speed sensor knock sensor ignition sequence ignition coil the ignition coil turn the dc low voltage of battery to high voltage and ignite the air mixture in cylinder through the spark generated by the discharge of spark plug each ignition coil consist of a pair of coil winding around laminated iron core there are four ignition coil fitted on the camshaft cover of the engine and each cylinder has an independent ignition coil connected with the spark plug failure the ignition coil may have failure under the following condition primary winding open circuit primary winding short circuited resistance of primary winding out of tolerance secondary winding open circuit secondary winding short circuited the circuit connecting secondary winding with primary winding is short resistance of secondary winding out of tolerance coil top insulation mechanically damaged crankshaft position ckp sensor ckpthe crankshaft position sensor is applied to provide information of the engine speed and crankshaft top dead centre in the ignition system without electricity distributor it is a hall effect sensor matched with pulse wheel in application the pulse wheel is a tooth plate with 58 small tooth separated by 6 and a 2 tooth gap on magnetic ring the pulse wheel is fitted on the crankshaft and rotate with the crankshaft when the tooth tip pass by sensor end the pulse wheel made of steel magnetic material cut the magnetic force line of the permanent magnet in the sensor to produce the induced voltage in the coil which act as the rotational speed output signal failure ckpthe sensor or magnetic ring may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition ckpsensor gap fail to comply with the specification ckpsensor is contaminated by dust ckpsensor hall effect semiconductor is damaged ckpsensor magnetic field is too weak ckpsensor harness is open circuit ckpsensor harness is short circuited ckpsensor harness has a high resistance magnetic ring is contaminated by dust the precision of magnetic ring is low due to erosion or mechanical damage the ckp sensor signal is distorted due to out of round flywheel or drive disc the ckp sensor signal is changed due to crankshaft radial movement in order to check the ecm for receiving the signal from ckp sensor following operation may be conducted 1 0 138 engine fuel management engine check the operation of the fuel pump in the engine starting process if the fuel pump supply fuel when the ignition is on but not operating it indicate that ecm doe not receive ckp sensor signal knock sensor the knock sensor is used to provide electronic controller ecm with the engine knock information to carry out knock control the knock sensor is a vibration acceleration sensor it is fitted on the engine cylinder block the sensitive element of the sensor is a piezoelectric crystal the vibration of the engine cylinder block is transferred to the piezoelectric crystal through the mass block in the sensor due to the pressure generated from the vibration of mass block the piezoelectric crystal produce voltage on both pole surface to transform the vibration signal into voltage signal to output failure theknocksensormayhavefailuresorprovideincorrectsignal due to the following condition sensor open circuit sensor to ground short circuited sensor circuit intermittently open incorrect knock sensor refit in case of knock sensor signal failure the following symptom may occur store the failure code ecmfailure to inspect engine combustion knock ecmrestore default setting engine fuel supply control system the engine fuel supply control system mainly include upstream oxygen sensor downstream oxygen sensor fuel injector 1 4 canister valve fuel pump relay the engine fuel supply control system is mainly applied to control the fuel supply for the engine under different working condition when the engine start for the first time and engine speed is higher than the preset speed the system begin open loop operation the control module ignore the signal from the heated oxygen sensor ho2 the control module calculate the air fuel ratio according to the input signal from the ect tmap and tp sensor the system stay in open loop state until the following condition are met ho2swhen the output voltage change the increased temperature of ho2 is sufficient for normal operation ectthe temperature of the sensor is higher than the specified temperature the engine has started for a specified period of time with respect to above condition different engine have their specific value which are stored in an electronic erasable programmable read only memory eeprom after these value are met the system begin closed loop operation during the closed loop running the control module calculate the air fuel ratio and opening time of the injector according to the signal from various sensor especially the heated oxygen senor so that the air fuel ratio is alway close to 14 7 1 oxygen sensor the oxygen sensor measure the oxygen content in the engine exhaust to determine if the gasoline and air are burnt completely the electronic controller implement the closed loop control aiming at excessive air coefficient 1 according to the information to ensure that three way catalytic converter have the highest conversion efficiency in such three kind of pollutant as hc co and nox in the exhaust its sensing element is a ceramic tube the inner surface and outer surface of which are contacted with exhaust gas and ambient air respectively when the sensing ceramic tube temperature reach 350 it will have the property of solid electrolyte with this property the concentration difference of oxygen is turned to electric potential difference so as to form electric signal output if the mixture is rich the concentration difference of oxygen inside and outside the ceramic tube is high the electric potential difference is high a large amount of oxygen ion move from inside to outside and the output voltage is high approximate to 900mv if the mixture is thin the concentration difference of oxygen inside and outside the ceramic tube is low the electric potential difference is low only a few of oxygen ion move from inside to outside and the output voltage is low approximate to 100mv the oxygen sensor consist of the front oxygen sensor and rear oxygen sensor the former is used to measure the oxygen content in the engine exhaust to implement the closed loop control and the latter is used to monitor the conversion function of the three way catalyst failure ho2sit may have failure or provide incorrect signal due to the following condition contaminated by leaded fuel contaminated by carbon or silicon deposit impact damage mechanical impact 1 0 139 engine engine fuel management external harness open circuit external harness connected to the 12v power supply is short circuited external harness short to ground external harness high resistance heating device open circuit heating device short circuited ecmfailure to provide ground access for heating device harness shielding not connected to the ground fuel injector the fuel injector according to the command of the ecm inject fuel within the specified time to supply the engine with fuel and have it atomized ecmthe ecm energiz the injector coil to form magnetic force when the magnetic force is strong enough to overcome the resultant force of the return spring pressure needle valve gravity and friction the needle valve begin to rise and the fuel injection process start when fuel injection pulse end the pressure of return spring make the needle valve close again failure the fuel injector may fail under the following condition nozzle is contaminated by dust or wax flow is reduced nozzle offset injection positioned incorrectly fuel filter blockage winding open circuit winding short to driven vane pump which is fitted in the fuel tankandimmersedinthefuel itappliesfuelforheatdissipation and lubrication the battery supply power to the electric fuel pump via fuel pump relay which allow the electric fuel pump circuit to be closed only when the relay start and the engine operate when the engine stop running due to accident the fuel pump will stop operating automatically failure if the fuel pump relay fail the following symptom will occur at the time of start if the fuel system pressure fall to zero the engine will fail to ignite if the fuel system pressure is lower than normal operating pressure 0 38mpa the engine may try to ignite but fail to work normally if the fuel system pressure is normal the engine will ignite to operate but will flame out finally with decrease in fuel pressure and fail to restart during driving if the fuel pump relay fail during driving the engine will lose power and finally shut down due to shortage of fuel the fuel pump relay may have fitted between the air filter and the throttle body which is fixed by the worm drive clamp the air filter assembly is fixed to the upper part of the engine with 4 air filter mounting pin exhaust system the exhaust system consist of the front exhaust pipe and rear exhaust pipe which are connected by nut the muffler of the exhaust system is located on the front exhaust pipe the tail pipe is designed with an one piece pipe with single hole whichistreatedbythestainlesssteelpolishingtechnology the exhaust system is connected with the vehicle body through 4 rubber lug the rubber lug can decrease the vibration of the exhaust pipe during driving two thermal baffle are fitted respectively on the upper part of the exhaust muffler and on the outer surface of the fuel tank this can decrease the thermal radiation from the exhaust pipe to protect the shift device and fuel system 1 0 164 engine manifold exhaust system engine operation air filter air enter into the air filter through the air filter inlet pipe located at the front end cooling module the impurity in the air are filtered by the air filter element and kept in the air filter lower housing air from the air filter is transferred to the throttle body via the air filter outlet pipe exhaust manifold and exhaust system the high temperature pressure gas generated during the operation of the engine will enter into the exhaust manifold through each exhaust port the manifold branch integrally enter into a flange and transport the gas into the exhaust system via the three way catalytic converter and finally discharge it through the exhaust tail pipe with the exhaust system muffler flow noise of the gas can be greatly reduced to meet relevant noise regulation at last three way catalytic converter twc warning twc operate at very high temperature therefore extreme caution is necessary when handling this component caution for the twc to work efficiently and to prevent premature failure do not run on leaded fuel and do not allow unburnt fuel to pass through the exhaust system the primary combustion happen in the combustion chamber and the exhaust gas enter into the twc through the exhaust manifold twcit operate effectively only when its minimum working temperature is achieved the exhaust gas enter into an integral structure coated with pt and rh catalyst and twoprocedure reductionandoxidation areperformedhere the nitrogen oxide nox is restored to nitrogen from which the oxide is combined with carbon monoxide co to form carbon dioxide the hydrocarbon hc and co will continue to conduct combustion in the three way catalytic converter with the oxygen in the air which form water and co2 1 0 165 engine engine manifold exhaust system service procedure air filter assembly remove 1 operation system layout starting and charging system layout 1 starter motor 2 generator 1 0 172 engine electrical system engine description alternator the alternator is fitted on the right front of the engine the drive pulley is fitted on one end of the rotor which is driven by the multislot v shaped belt of the crankshaft the location of the alternator is fixed and the belt tension is maintained by the automatic tension pulley of the wheel train the alternator are similar in structure including a stator a rotor a rectifier and a regulator the one way output terminal is connected to the battery positive with a thick cable via 150a fuse the alternator is connected to the ground through its bracket a three pin connector is the load signal connecting terminal of charging warning lamp output ignition signal input and alternator output the rotor consist of an exciting winding which is wound on theironcoreandfittedontheshaft bothendsoftheironcore extend forming the north and south pole of the magnetic line in the coil the rotor is located in the stator which is fitted on the bearing so as to guarantee stable rotation and enable it to support the high load in the drive belt tension application the stator include a stator core and a stator coil which has bracket supporting at both end the stator is made of the thin and soft iron sheet its slot fit with the stator coil and is fixed on the alternator housing the stator has three set of coil which are made of copper wire with insulating paint these 3 coil winding are connected in a triangle connection scheme thus the tail end of each winding is connected together the output current is provided from the other end of each winding the stator coil allow the magnetic flux from the rotor magnetic pole to flow through the stator coil rotation of the rotor make the coil generate ac the rectifier is located at the rear side of the alternator convert the ac generated by the stator coil to dc required for the vehicle electrical system the rectifier include 8 semiconductor diode which are mounted on a heat sink for heat dispersion three diode are mounted on the front three on the opposite side and the other two in the middle when the output voltage of the alternator is lower than the battery voltage the rectifier also prevent the current from flowing from the battery to the alternator the integrated circuit regulator is also located at the rear side of the alternator control the output voltage of the alternator to avoid battery overcharging and protect the vehicle electrical system the regulator optimiz the battery charging according to relevant temperature set a rated output voltage of 14 5v and change the output voltage according to the battery load and required load for the vehicle electrical system transistor of the regulator can adjust the output voltage through the quick switch of the internal induced voltage the regulator has an output terminal of the charging indicator which can control the operation of the indicator when the output voltage of the alternator is lower than the battery voltage the indicator illuminate the alternator load signal output is also provided by the regulator and the output data is provided to ecm and ecm adapt to a variety of electrical load by adjusting the idle speed the ignition system provide excitation current to the rotor through the collector ring on the alternator rotor shaft when the alternator is at low speed when the speed of the alternator increase the alternator become self excited starter motor the starter motor is located at the left rear side of the engine it is fixed to the mounting hole on the transmission housing with 2 flange bolt the starter motor are of the pre engaged type which include a solenoid a one way clutch and a motor body when the ignition switch is in start position and the engine is requested for start the body control module integrated with anti theft function will output signal to switch on the starter relay after passing anti theft verification battery battery instruction all vehicle have a 12v maintenance free lead acid battery equipped at the left side of the engine compartment which is fitted on the pressed steel bracket fixed on the transmission mount fastened by the battery retaining strap and covered by the battery heat shield the battery electrode are of chuck connected pole type warning battery fluid electrolyte contain sulphuric acid it may cause severe burn if it get on your skin or in your eye wear protective clothing and a face shield if electrolyte get on your skin or clothe immediately rinse it off with water if electrolyte get in your eye immediately flush eye with water for at least 15 minute and seek medical help the battery has three main function firstly it provide power for starting the engine secondly it is used as a voltage regulator of the electrical system lastly it can provide energy in a certain amount of time when the electrical demand exceed the engine output compared with the conventional battery the packaging battery have following advantage there is no need to add water during the entire life cycle of the battery 1 0 173 engine engine electrical system it adopt the overcharge protective measure if the voltage applied to the battery is too high it will not receive the excess current as the conventional battery will for the conventional battery if the battery continue to be charged when the voltage is too high air leakage will occur causing fluid loss compared with the conventional battery self discharge doe not tend to occur for this kind of battery this is especially important when the battery is not used for a long time for the cigar lighter and smaller electrical equipment the electric quantity supplied is more reliable battery charging when the engine is not running by the reason of electric equipment opening for long time vehicle creepage and longtime parking or some malfunction in the alternator the battery might not be charged and will be flat the vehicle even not can be started on this condition the battery need to be charged caution when charging or emergency starting the negative cable should be connected to the body earth well while not the negative pole battery appearance checking before charging if the shell is broken or there is a leak do not charge the battery and replace it after finding the reason if the pole is broken do not charge the battery and replace it after finding the reason the bulge battery for over discharging or over charging can not be charged the battery should be replaced before charging clean the pole rub off rust and smear some butter to avoid electricity corroding charging announcement please pull on the security glass keep ventilation and normal temperature when charging do not smoke when charging and avoid kindling in before charging connect the positive cable at first after charging disconnect the negative cable at first battery charging operation connecttwopositivepolesbetweenchargerandbattery and connect two negative pole between charger and battery as well if the battery is not removed from the vehicle pleaseconnectthenegativecableofthecharger negative pole to the body ground well ensure the pole are clean and the charging circuit loop is all right during charging process if the battery temperature is higher than 40 must stop charging and after he battery temperature falling to environment temperature the battery can be charged again do not charge the battery under 0 for the battery whose voltage value is under 11 0v at the first of charging it may appear the phenomena that the battery cannot be charged because badly discharging the inverse proportion of vitriol in battery is too small the liquid is close to pure water battery s inner resistance value is too high with the charging going on the inverse proportion of vitriol in battery will rise up the charging process could become breath again battery measure battery can be measured by familiar equipment on the after sale market such as midtronic sery instrument if the equipment is not type suited the measured data can not be used to judge the battery s status battery replacement caution when removing the battery ensure that the alarm is disarmed and that the ignition is switched off alway disconnect the negative terminal first and then the positive terminal when refitting the battery alway fit the positive terminal first followed by the negative terminal refer to battery remove refit when replacing the battery 1 0 174 engine electrical system engine operation alternator alternator use the self excited system where many diode supply part of the current generated by the alternator for the rotor alternator can not supply the initial current required for start up of the charging procedure initial current is supplied by the battery power supply through ignition switch to activate the exciting winding to start up the charging procedure when the ignition switch is turned on the battery voltage is also supplied through the charging warning lamp and ground via the field winding of the alternator so that the led warning lamp illuminate when starting the engine the rotor is rotating in the stator generating the 3 phase alternating current ac when the rotor speed increase the output voltage will rise quickly and the potential difference of the diode output end of the magnetic field will raise when the voltage increase the charging warning lamp will darken gradually when the alternator output voltage is equal to the battery voltage the lamp will go out at this moment the alternator can generate sufficient self excited current via the diode in the magnetic field and begin to charge the battery if the charging warning lamp remain on the charging system fail and a check must be performed otherwise the battery will be damaged starter motor when the ignition switch is in start position the coil will supply power to connect the pinion and the flywheel gear ring the pinion move spirally around the motor reel thus the motor reel can achieve the connection through the gear ring on the starter motor the motor drive the pinion through the transmission instead of the motor reel directly when starting the engine the one way clutch prevent motor armature overspeed the driver should not remove the key from the start position so that the motor can keep running after start up of the engine when the ignition switch is released the solenoid coil will release energy to pull out the pinion from the gear ring 1 0 175 engine engine electrical system service procedure alternator remove 1 raise the front of the vehicle warning do not work on or under a vehicle supported only by a jack alway support the transmission shift lever cable 1 0 186 shift lock control new 4at transmission description gear shift control mechanism the gear shift control mechanism mainly consist of a shift lever a base a gear shift lock up solenoid valve a gear shift cable etc the gear shift control mechanism base is fixed to the body central passage with 4 screw the inner latch of the shift lever is engaged with the inner groove of the base 1 shift lever 2 p gear unlock lever the shift lever position operation is as follow p lock the transmission to prevent the wheel glide r choose it only when the vehicle is still and the engine is idling n no torque pass to wheel d all 4 forward gear can be used while in this position this is the default choosing position of normal driving s sport mode tiptronic tiptronic shift if the shift lever move from d position to the right side the system will automatically enter into sport mode with different shifting parameter then move the shift lever to separate the tiptronic mode and sport mode offering completely manual shift controlling move the shift lever forward to finish upshift and move the shift lever backward to finish downshift to exit tiptronic mode return the shift lever to d position gear shift lock up solenoid valve the transmission is equipped with a gear shift lock up solenoid valve located at the bottom of the shift lever the operation of the solenoid valve is determined by the bcm controlled gear shift lock up relay in the passenger compartment fuse box when the shift lever is in p position with the ignition switch on and the brake pedal not applied the lock up solenoid valve is not activated at the time the lock tongue of p position lock block contact with the corresponding groove at the shift lever bottom so as to prevent the accidental movement of shift lever the lock up solenoid valve is activated only when the brake pedal is depressed enable the solenoid valve to make the solenoid valve push rod move toward front part of the vehicle so as to push the lock tongue of p position lock block away from the corresponding groove at the shift lever bottom simultaneously press the lock button on the shift lever knob to allow the shift lever freely change to other position from p position shift lever moving out of p position in emergency with the ignition on and brake pedal depressed when the shift lever cannot be moved out from p gear turn off the ignition switch pull out the ignition key and apply the parking cable depress the brake pedal pry up the upper trim panel of the centre console with a proper tool press the p gear emergency unlock button located at the left upper corner of the gear shift mechanism move the shift lever to n position simultaneously start the engine and shift to the required gear gear shift cable the gear shift cable connect the shift lever to the shift rocker arm on the transmission the outer cable is fixed to the shift lever assembly end with a c clip and fixed to the body and transmission end by two bolt respectively the inner cable at the shift control mechanism end is connected with the shift 1 0 187 transmission shift lock control new 4at lever and the inner cable at the transmission end is buckled to the transmission shift rocker arm via a cable ball joint 1 0 188 shift lock control new 4at transmission service procedure shift lever knob automatic remove 1 remove the gear panel geargeargear
