componentscomponentscomponent 1 front shock absorber column 2 front suspension coil spring 3 front shock absorber column dust cover 4 front shock absorber column auxiliary spring 5 front suspension shock absorber column insulating pad 6 front shock absorber lower spring cushion 7 front shock absorber bearing seat 8 nut 9 front shock absorber upper spring cushion 10 front shock absorber rebound gasket 11 nut 1 0 383 suspension system front suspension description front suspension comprise of front macpherson strut damper front lower arm anti roll bar anti roll bar link front subframe front wheel hub bearing and drive flange the front suspension design feature anti dive and anti squat geometry via the anti roll bar and 1 1 strut movement ratio the layout and design of the strut minimiz camber loss due to side force improving handling and steering response front subframe assembly the front subframe is a fabricated steel construction made up of precise component welded together to form a light weight high structural strength assembly geometric machining of all key position take place after construction to ensure the precise location of front suspension and steering system component the subframe is attached to the body via 6 solid mounting point this help to increase the stiffness of the front subframe the arrangement of the supporting point avoid the displacement of the front subframe under high lateral force which could affect handling characteristic macpherson strut two macpherson strut control the damping of the front suspension each strut assembly comprise a damper unit a coil spring and a top mount assembly the coil spring is retained in a compressed condition between the strut spring seat and the top mount assembly isolator at each end of the spring reduce noise transmitted from the suspension to the body a bearing is fitted to the underside of the top mount assembly and allow the spring to rotate as the steering is operated a dust cover is fitted between the bump stop and the damper and protect the damper rod from dirt and damage the coil spring axis is offset to the axis of the damper this arrangement provide side load compensation which reduce friction within the damper damper each damper is handed is gas charged and oil filled and is of twin tube construction the twin tube construction allow the damper piston to operate on an inner tube with an oil gas cavity between the inner and outer tube this minimise oil cavitation and eliminate the effect of damage to the outer tube affecting piston operation the damper is attached at its lower end to a location on the swivel hub and is secured with 2 nut bolt correctly orientation is achieved by a machined section of the front hub being inserted in between 2 steel tang welded to the base of the damper the machined hub and tang contain hole which allow the fitment of the retaining nut bolt the damper rod is located in steel insert in rubber bush whichareintegralwiththetopmountingplate andaresecured with a washer and nut a hexagon is provided in the outer end of the damper rod to restrain the rod from rotating when the nut is tightened the damper rod is sealed in the damper with a low friction seal an angled spring seat is integral with the damper body a welded bracket on the body of the damper provide the location point for the anti roll bar link front lower arm assembly the front lower arm connect the front wheel hub to the front subframe the fabricated steel front lower arm feature an outer ball joint a front bush and a rear bush all component are serviceable the outer ball joint feature a parallel pin with a circumferentially machined groove that locate into the hub assembly and is clamped secured using a nut and bolt anti roll bar and link a solid spring steel arb is used that operate via arb link secured to each damper dedicated bracket the arb is attached to the front of the subframe with two polytetrafluoroethylene ptfe bush that are secured with clamp plate and bolt the low friction bush ptfe require no additional greasing and allow the arb to rotate freely with quiet operation this allow the bar to respond quickly to roll input and allow stiffer bush to be used as there is relatively little compression or twisting washer are attached to the arb to locate the outside of each bush and prevent sideway movement of the bar 1 0 384 front suspension suspension system the end of the arb are attached to each strut spring seat via an arb link this arrangement allow the arb to act on a ratio of approximately 1 1 with the wheel travel providing maximum roll bar effectiveness eacharblinkhasaballjointfittingateachend whichimprove response and efficiency thetopballjointismountedontheaxisofthelinkandattach directly to the damper strut and is secured with a locknut the lower ball joint is mounted at 180 to the axis of the link and attach to the arb and is secured with a locknut the link must be attached to the arb with the ball joint on the outboard side of the bar and the locknut facing inward the ball joint on the arb link are not serviceable and if replacement of either is necessary a new link will be required 1 0 385 suspension system front suspension service procedure front hub remove 1 raise vehicle on lift 2 remove front wheel suspension system rear suspension description rear suspension include coil spring damper h frame member h frame member the h frame member consist of a couple of trailing arm a cross beam a lower coil spring seat a damper fixing point and a stub axle the trailing arm are fabricated structure which is attached to the body via two fabricate mounting bracket forward of the rear wheel a fabricated bracket inboard of each stub axle connected to the trailing arm and cross beam provide the lower coil spring seat the trailing arm rearward of the stub axle provide the lower damper fixing point a stub axle is bolted with four bolt to each side of the frame and provide location for the rear brake and road wheel the twist beam principle use angled pivot bush which have a special construction to decrease the vertical vibration of the body coil spring coil spring manufactured from chromium steel are fitted between the body and the lower arm each spring is located in upper and lower spring isolator which reduce noise transmitted to the body from the suspension both isolator are positively retained to the spring the lower isolator is positively retained by a location pip on the lower arm a hole in the lower isolator is provided for water drainage and must be aligned with corresponding hole in the lower arm when fitted rear shock absorber the damper are gas charged and oil filled and are of twin tube construction the twin tube construction allow the damper piston to operate on an inner tube with an oil gas cavity between the inner and outer tube this minimise oil cavitation and eliminate the effect of damage to the outer tube affecting piston operation the lower damper mounting is fitted with a non serviceable bush that is attached to the trailing arm with a bolt and washer this mounting position give the damper an approximate ratio of 1 1 which is beneficial for damping control the damper rod is located in a bush in a top mounting and is secured with a rebound plate and a locknut the top mounting is attached to the body with two bolt the rebound plate fitted between the damper rod locknut and the top mounting cushion force as the damper rod reach full extension a spring aid plate and a spring aid are fitted over the rod and cushion the damper operation if maximum bump travel is reached 1 0 402 rear suspension suspension system service procedure rear damper 10nm 1 0 411 brake system brake description and operation system layout brake system 1 front brake caliper assembly 2 front ventilated brake hole disc 3 abs modulator 4 brake booster assembly 5 brake master cylinder 6 front wheel speed sensor 7 rear brake drum 8 rear wheel speed sensor 9 brake reservoir 10 air intake manifold 11 vacuum tube 1 0 412 brake brake system description vacuum servo assembly the vacuum servo booster assembly provide boost during braking to reduce the pedal force required for braking in a case of the vacuum booster assembly failure the hydraulic system maintain brake function but greater pedal braking force is required due to lack of vacuum assistance the vacuum booster assembly is connected to the bulkhead lhd pedal box rhd via 4 stud the brake master cylinder assembly is connected via two fixing bolt at the front end of the vacuum booster assembly the brake servo assembly consist of a circular housing which contain two diaphragm a central plate a control valve assembly input and output push rod and a filter the input push rod is connected to the brake pedal the output push rod locate in the primary piston of the master cylinder a protective gaiter is installed on the control valve assembly where it extend from the rear of the housing aportinthefrontfaceofthehousingisconnectedtoavacuum line from the engine a non return valve is incorporated into the vacuum line the control valve assembly consist of a valve body containing a valve a piston a valve spring and an input rod spring the valve control a vacuum port in the valve body the piston control an air inlet port between the valve and the piston a reaction disc and a ratio disc separate the piston from the output push rod a guide tube on the front of the valve body is attached to the front diaphragm and supported in a bush in the central plate a return spring locate in the open end of the guide tube brake master cylinder assembly a tandem brake master cylinder is installed on the brake servo assembly fluid is supplied via a remote reservoir connected to the master cylinder via two rubber hose the master cylinder is fixed to the front of the brake servo via 2 stud fixing when the brake pedal is pressed the brake master cylinder assembly produce hydraulic pressure so that the brake operate the tandem master cylinder contain two chamber and associated piston each piston chamber control a circuit the rear piston produce brake pressure for the primary circuit this cover lhf rhr the front piston produce brake pressure for the secondary circuit and this cover rhf lhr the remote brake fluid reservoir is installed higher than the master cylinder in order to maintain a gravitational feed the fluid reservoir is partitioned inside to feed fluid to each brake circuit independently thus avoiding the condition that both circuit could become devoid of fluid the brake fluid reservoir utiliz a fluid level switch a reed style switch featuring a permanent magnet and float is installed in the fluid reservoir which is linked to the fluid level handbrake and ebd warning lamp in the instrument pack front brake each front brake consist of a single piston sliding brake caliper assembly fitted to the hub and a ventilated brake disc rear brake each rear brake consist of a back plate a brake drum brake shoe ahydraulicwheelcylinder aselfadjustingrodandreturn spring the back plate is installed on the stub axle retained by the stub axle bolt and provide location for brake shoe and hydraulic wheel cylinder the brake drum is fitted to the flange of bearing and rotate with the wheel each rearward brake shoe is fitted with a handbrake cable lever brake pedal and brake lamp switch the brake pedal is installed on a pivot shaft in the pedal mounting bracket on the engine bulkhead a clevis pin and clip connect the input rod of the brake servo to the brake pedal a tang on the end of the brake pedal activate a plunger style brake switch in the pedal mounting bracket the brake switch is adjustable and secured in position using a lock nut the brake lamp switch provide two brake pedal state output one power the other ground this signal is supplied to the ecm and used to illuminate the stop lamp 1 0 413 brake system brake service procedure low brake fluid level indicator switch rhd remove 1 disconnect multiplug from brake fluid low level description value screw front speed wheel sensor 7 10nm screw rear speed wheel sensor 7 10nm bolt abs to bracket 7 10nm rear brake pipe to modulator 16 18nm 1 0 433 brake system brake module description and operation system layout brake system control component layout 1 brake fluid reservoir 2 brake fluid level sensor 3 vacuum booster 4 brake master cylinder 5 brake modulator 6 front wheel speed sensor assembly 7 rear wheel speed sensor assembly 1 0 434 brake module brake system system control diagram brake system control chart 1 0 435 brake system brake module description anti lock brake system abs during braking the function of the abs prevent wheel lock and thus the vehicle can stay stable even in emergency warning abs is an aid to retaining steering control and stability while braking abs cannot defy the natural law of physic acting on the vehicle abs will not prevent accident resulting from excessive cornering speed following another vehicle too closely aquaplaning etc the additional control provided by abs must never beexploitedinadangerousorrecklessmannerwhich couldjeopardisethesafetyofthedriverorotherroad user the fitting of abs doe not imply that the vehicle will alway stop in a shorter distance tip during normal braking the feel of the brake pedal on vehicle equipped with abs will be the same as that on non abs vehicle during anti lock braking operation the driver will experience feedback in the form of a pulsating brake pedal and solenoid pump motor noise from the abs modulator in conventional brake the abs doe not work brake fluid directly flow into each wheel cylinder from the reservoir through hydraulic control unit hcu channel to apply brake to each wheel once abs ecu detect that a wheel locking occur through the wheel speed sensor ecu will give an order to close the inlet valve and the brake fluid will no longer flow into the wheel cylinder meanwhile the outlet valve is opened to discharge the hydraulic oil from the wheel cylinder to allow the wheel to rotate if it is detected that a wheel slip normally the outlet valve will be closed due to low brake pressure and the brake fluid will flow into the wheel cylinder from the inlet valve to increase the brake force if the wheel lock in emergency the inlet outlet valve will be closed at the same time to keep the brake pressure in the wheel cylinder so as to exert the best brake performance electronic brake distribution ebd ebdthe function of the ebd is to optimize the distribution of the hydraulic pressure between the front and rear axle to keep the vehicle at a stable state in brake process according to the vehicle load condition and road condition with the abs ecu in the normal brake mode the vehicle deceleration of 0 3g and above i e moderate to high brake pedal load the ebd function can be automatically implemented in forward or reverse mode ebditsoperationissimilartotheab buttheebdoperate at lower wheel slip limit and the ebd is only applicable to the rear wheel brake during braking if the abs ecu detect that the rear axle wheel is slower than the front axle wheel that is the possibility of wheel slip exist the abs ecu will send a signal to the hydraulic pressure regulator to turn off the fluid inlet solenoid valve of the rear wheel brake this prevent further increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear wheel brake but allow the hydraulic pressure of the front wheel brake to increase the whole brake force is thus maximized if the rear wheel speed return to the calibration wheel slip limit range the abs ecu will send a signal to turn on the fluid inlet valve gradually which allow progressive increase of the hydraulic pressure of the rear axle brake ebdit operation can be felt through the following way the movement of the brake pedal is more violent by turning off the fluid inlet solenoid valve and the brake pedal has a slight pulse as a result of the fluid inlet solenoid valve s turning on when the brake pedal is released the ebd will stop immediately ebdthe wheel slip limit that enable the ebd vary with the vehicle speed 1 0 436 brake module brake system service procedure abs sensor front remove warning do not work on or with inner and outer constant velocity joint the inner joint is of the tripode type with spherical bushing to reduce sliding resistance the shaft and joint are one assembly theouterjointisaconstantvelocityballandsockettypewhich is splined to the shaft and secured with a snap ring all joint are packed with grease and protected by gaiter both drive shaft are fitted with dynamic damper the drive shaft transmit engine torque via the gearbox to the front wheel the dynamic damper reduce any vibration produced by the drive shaft s rotation 1 0 442 driveline axle driveline axle diagnostic diagnosis mechanical check when rotating a rattle sound can be heard when the worn or damaged constant velocity universal joint of the front wheel drive shaft rotate a rattle sound may be heard if there s a rattle sound check the constant velocity universal joint shield of the front wheel drive shaft for crack or damage a clunk sound can be heard during accelerating and sliding if a click sound can be heard during rotation check the following check the external universal joint for wear or damage check the seal for breakage or damage shake or vibration when accelerating start shake start shake refer to the vibration felt on the steering wheel and or the front of the vehicle when the vehicle is shifted from static starting to rapid accelerating during acceleration start shake may also refer to the swing occur in the front and the rear of the vehicle the start shake of the front wheel drive vehicle may caused by the following internal tee universal joint is worn or damaged internal universal joint is severely worn or ceratoid the universal joint show severe ceratoid angle which is normally caused by the excessive height of the front cocked head or the spring when the power assembly suspension is damaged or mismatched following situation may occur angle of the universal joint is oversize start shake during rapid acceleration high torque of the vehicle power assembly cause increase of the front suspension when the suspension raise the angle of the internal tee universal joint willincreaseaccordingly startshakemayoccurwhenfollowing situation appear operation system layout seat layout 1 driver seat 2 front passenger seat 3 rear seat 1 0 476 seat interior fitting description front seat assembly overview 1 seat headrest 2 seat back assembly 3 seat back cover and foam 4 seat back frame 5 seat cushion assembly 6 seat cushion cover and foam 7 seat cushion frame the common seat mainly consist of headrest seat back seat cushion and accessory among them both seat back assembly and seat cushion assembly consist of cover foam and frame the seat assembly and the main component are repaired as fitting according to the vulnerability and maintainability of the component the front seat assembly may have different configuration according to vehicle configuration and seat exterior for example 4 way manual seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination 6 way power seat forward backward adjustment seat back inclination and seat height adjustment fabric of high quality or pvc and fabric optional seat side airbag rear seat assembly the rear seat of this model is a 4 6 type seat with reclinable seat back if you want to expand the luggage compartment space first fully lower or remove all the rear seat headrest then pull up the unlock strap 1 on both side respectively and fold the seat back forward when unfolding the rear seat back pull up the seat back unlock strap push the seat back until it reach an appropriate position and the seat back is locked when it click 1 0 477 interior fitting seat operation manual seat driver seat 1 seat longitudinal position adjusting handle 2 seat height adjusting handle 3 seat back adjusting handle the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position the 2nd adjusting handle is located in the front of the trim panel outside the seat cushion which can adjust the seat height lift or press the handle repeatedly to raise or lower the seat the 3rd adjusting handle is located in the rear of the height adjusting handle which can adjust the seat back inclination lift the handle and pull it to the desired position release the handle to lock the seat to the selected position front passenger seat the passenger seat is adjusted in the same way as the driver seat the front rear position of the seat can be adjusted by the handle in the front of the seat the position of the sear back can be adjusted by the side handle 1 0 478 seat interior fitting service procedure front seat remove 1 if the seat is adjustable raise the exterior fitting mirror mirror specification torque description value screw exterior mirror 9 10nm 1 0 496 mirror exterior fitting description and operation system layout exterior rearview mirror layout 1 left exterior rearview mirror assembly 2 right exterior rearview mirror assembly 1 0 497 exterior fitting mirror system control diagram a hard wire 1 0 498 mirror exterior fitting description overview the power exterior rearview mirror assembly is fitted on all model as a standard configuration each exterior rearview mirror assembly is controlled by the circular control switch of the headlamp leveling switch exterior rearview mirror assembly the left and right exterior rearview mirror assembly shall be distinguished but the basic structure are the same the housing of the exterior rearview mirror assembly is a plastic mould which include the mirror and side direction indicator lamp the exterior rearview mirror assembly is fixed on the front door by 2 bolt which are visible only when the front trim panel of the exterior rearview mirror cover is removed the housing of the exterior rearview mirror assembly is unrepairable and shall be replaced as an assembly the exterior rearview mirror the exterior rearview mirror cover side direction indicator lamp bulb and the seal gasket are repairable headlamp leveling switch theheadlamplevelingswitchislocatedonthelowertrimpanel at the driver side the headlamp leveling switch has a circular control knob which can operate the vertical and horizontal motor in the exterior rearview mirror assembly the circular control knob can be adjustedto4directionsandrotate turnthetwoled l r abovethecircularcontrolknobcounterclockwise clockwiseto select one of the exterior rearview mirror when the width lamp or the headlamp illuminate the led flash 1 0 499 exterior fitting mirror operation exterior rearview mirror the steering gear of the exterior rearview mirror include 2 bidirectional motor vertical motor and horizontal motor which provide horizontal and vertical motion for the rearview mirror the vertical motor is responsible for the up and down motion while the horizontal motor is for the left and right motion the exterior rearview mirror can provide the following function exterior rearview mirror manual folding side direction indicator lamp exterior rearview mirror manual folding fold manually to achieve the folding expansion of the exterior rearview mirror interior rearview mirror manual anti dazzle function during driving the strong light from the interior rearview mirror may affect the driver to avoid such visual shock pull theleveratthebaseoftheinteriorrearviewmirrorandchange the view to dip the mirror push back the lever to return the interior rearview mirror to the normal position 1 0 500 mirror resistor the resistor reduce the power so the wiper work at low speed auto wipe variable intermittent wipe the function of variable intermittent wipe the vehicle is provided with a wiper delay potentiometer when receiving valid wiper interval request the bcm will control the wiper at slow speed the time of intermittent wipe vary with the vehicle speed level and the setting of wiper delay bracket remove 1 open the tail gate and when removing the tail gate make sure it is securely supported warning as the large weight of the tailgate operation should be performed after the safety feature is set and take extra care to prevent the vehicle from damaging and personal injury 2 carefully pry off the steel spring retaining clip on both side of the left gas spring forcibly unplug the ball cup at piston end from the ball head of the tail gate 3 then pull the ball head socket of gas spring sleeve from the ball head of left body bracket then forcibly unplug the ball cup at socket end of the gas spring from the ball head of the left body side bracket 4 remove 2 bolt fixing the mounting bracket of the left gas spring to the body and remove the gas spring mounting bracket 5 repeat step 2 4 to remove the right gas spring and its mounting bracket refit 1 align the mounting bracket of the left gas spring with the hole on the luggage compartment 2 fit 2 bolt fixing the mounting bracket of the tail gate gas spring to the body tighten to 7 10nm and check the torque 3 check the steel spring piece on both ball cup of the air spring ensure it is properly located in the spring piece slot 4 align the ball cup at socket end of the gas spring with the ball head on the left mounting bracket and make sure the gas spring piston is downward 5 hold the sleeve push the ball cup into the ball head with thumb if the two part are fitted well a crack sound will be heard caution to avoid damaging the airtightness of the air spring never hold the centre of the air spring and laterally apply force to it by hand 6 align the ball cup at piston end of the left gas spring with the ball head on the tail gate inner panel hold the end of the piston push the ball cup into the ball head with switch is enabled the bcm will wake up the security system lighting system and diagnostic system when the ignition switch is in acc position the bcm allow the washer wiper and power window system to operate when the ignition switch is in on position the fuel system start to work and the bcm communicate and transmit information with other ecus through the can and lin bus load management bcmthe bcm can manage the load of some electric appliance by configuration and ensure low battery power consumption during storage and transportation or idle time production mode it is configured for the bcm during vehicle assembly transportation mode after vehicle assembly programme the bcm so that the transportation mode can be used during transportation and low battery problem can be avoided when the factory deliver the vehicle to the authorized local after sale service centre this mode may restrict the function of some electric appliance the rear fog lamp doe not work when the engine is off the high beam headlamp doe not work when the engine is off the low beam headlamp doe not work when the engine is off the reverse lamp doe not work when the engine is off the direction indicator lamp doe not work when the engine is off the daytime running lamp if equipped doe not work when the engine is off follow me home function cannot be realized if the engine is disabled the hazard warning lamp backlight doe not work but the function of this lamp is not affected when the engine is disabled the window can be ascended but cannot be descended with the aftermarket diagnostic tool the local mg authorised repairer can shift the vehicle load management configuration from transportation mode to normal mode normal mode this mode apply the default setting after completion of the pdi the vehicle can enter into normal operation sleep mode with the ignition switch off and the can and lin buse disabled if the battery is still connected the bcm will stay in sleep mode and be prepared for receiving the signal from the can and lin buse wake up mode in sleep mode when one of the following operation is done the bcm will be waked up activation signal of hazard warning lamp switch is received activation signal of direction indicator lamp switch is received lock signal of internal lock is received unlock signal of internal lock is received activation signal of driver door switch is received activation signal of passenger door switch is received receive the activation signal of the luggage compartment lid switch receive the activation signal of the luggage compartment lid release switch activation signal of driver door lock switch is received activation signal of driver door unlock switch is received ignition switch is in acc position ignition switch is in on position activation signal of internal lamp switch linwake up signal from lin bus canwake up signal from can bus wake up signal from local hard wire valid radio frequency signal load management to meet the demand of the starter motor for high current during engine start some electric appliance shall be powered off for example disconnect the power of the hrw intelligent stop start energy saving system disable switch high beam headlamp reverse lamp horn and interior lamp etc after the engine is started reactivate each electric appliance to limit the current consumption of the battery diagnosis bcmthe bcm monitor information input and output if any fault is detected the corresponding dtc will be stored in the fault record bcmthe bcm can detect short and open circuit as well as wrong signal of the can and lin buse when any fault is detected the bcm will close the corresponding function when the fault is eliminated the corresponding function will be activated next time when the activation matter should be noted the paint and zinc coating should be completely ground off before grinding be careful when grinding apply the spot welding paint between the weld contact surface before the spot welding with a hairbrush or a spray particle on the surface will be absorbed by the electrode so it is necessary to clean the electrode tip frequently 1 0 585 body repair collision repair damage diagnosis damage detection during damage detection visually inspect the location collided to determine the direction and size of the impact and check for potential damage for vehicle damaged in an accident query the vehicle speed impact location or rollover location directionandanglewhentheaccidenthappen andunderstand the impact form location and angle of the impacted vehicle etc useanintuitivemethodtodeterminethelocationdamaged by the impact and area that may be affected by the impact test drive and measuring instrument can also be used for the thorough check of the vehicle confirm if the underbody is deformed and if the whole body is damaged and twisted check if the door can function properly etc to determine the level of damage and the method of repair 1 vehicle visual inspection 2 detail visual inspection damage determination deformation occur not only at the impact location but also at the whole body such as beam suspension and engine etc along the course of the impact systematically check the related part for damage until there is no any sign of damage and the damage in the surrounding area is found characteristic of body damage characteristic of damage caused by front impact characteristic of damage caused by middle impact characteristic of damage caused by rear impact damage measurement accurate measurement is one of the necessary process for completion of impact repair for integral body measurement is more important for a successful damage repair because the steering system and suspension are on the body and some suspension are designed according to the requirement for assembly to ensure correct steering and driveability the fit tolerance of the key machining size shall be within the permissible range measurement shall combine with remove operation or it is impossible to determine the damage to facilitate body repair and thorough examination of damage and avoid damage of the part removed due to repair remove the related part the principle for remove is to avoid any component damage measuring point selection screw hole process hole of the component overlap of the body steel sheet machining gap or raised imprint on the steel sheet 1 0 586 collision repair body repair method of measurement distance measurement the simplest and most practical method is to measure the distance between the reference point of the component with a steel tape centreing measure by locating the centre three dimensional coordinate measurement measure the data of the three dimensional coordinate on the measuring point selected and compare the data with those of the vehicle to determine body damage electronic measurement more accurate and more effective reason for error in body size measurement the error is due to the accuracy of the measuring tool or equipment itself error due to human factor failure to locate the measuring point wrong method is used for measurement failure to use the measuring tool and equipment in a proper way precaution for measurement level the body before measurement mark measuring point on the body to prevent confusion make accurate measurement carry out multiple measurement recheck the result of the measurement 1 0 587 body repair collision repair repair plan determination underbody correction when the vehicle is severely damaged the underbody is deformed no need to replace the whole body correct the underbody first underbody correction ensure the three dimensional position of the underbody and the general position of the body determine the position of the engine assembly and front suspension and recover the angle for wheel alignment and the position of other assembly after underbody correction repair the body panel body and covering component correction after an impact if the body and covering component only have repairable depression or deformation no serious damage occur no need to replace the component recover its shape by correction sheet metal replacement when the body part has a damage at one location or more than one location and the damage or corrosion is beyond the specified degree it is worthless to repair it or a repair cannot help it to achieve the specified technical standard and function requirement then replace the sheet metal body replacement when the vehicle is severely damaged the whole body is smashed by the impact the underbody is seriously deformed the two side roof cover engine hood and trunk lid are almost destroyed and the whole body is unrepairable then replace the body according to user s demand remove usable assembly and component from the damaged vehicle thoroughly check and repair the major assembly such as engine replace the body assembly and part that shall be replaced and reassemble them according to the assembly process of the vehicle partial replacement when impact occur and only local damage appear for example damage to the front rear fender door engine hood or trunk lid partial replacement can be performed for the body this save trouble time and reduce the cost body pillar replacement when the body pillar windshield pillar front or rear pillar is badly damaged and cannot be corrected by a simple repair replace it recover the large scale deformation before replacing the pillar 1 0 588 collision repair body repair body correction the purpose of body correction is to recover the correct position and size of the sheet metal by eliminating the deformation plastic deformation and residue stress caused by collision introduction of correcting equipment the correcting equipment shall provide the following feature the equipment shall have high strength body positioning device and fixture it shall have a service puller with various shape and different function that can meet the repair requirement for different location be able to perform multipoint and comprehensive correction drawing be able to accurately measure and detect the deviation and repair error for each reference point operation process for correcting equipment regardless of the type of the equipment used its operation is broadly divided into the following 4 step 1 get on the vehicle 2 position clamping 3 drawing 4 measurement auxiliary fixture prevent motion of the vehicle force caused by even and disperse drawing auxiliary support prevent secondary damage to the body due to drawing focus the drawing force on the damaged location and improve the efficiency of the drawing operation prevent moment resulted from drawing correction drawing method drawing sequence note the method and procedure for drawing during drawing draw for a little while then release the chain unload and make measurement operate from inside to outside 1 take length as the priority draw the vehicle in the longitudinal direction along the centreline of the vehicle 2 then correct the width correct the vehicle in the horizontal direction 3 finally correct the height stress elimination eliminate the stress during drawing knock the raised location beside the deformed region and the back knock the welding location of the deformed region knock the ridge of the deformed region knock the reinforcement of the deformed region the high strength integral body is susceptible to heat so do not attempt to complete the correction drawing just by operation of one step generally follow the cyclic process draw keep balance draw keep balance if some body are tightly folded after an impact and there is a risk of metal damage then heat it when heating only heat the corner or the location where the two panel are tightly connected heating is merely a mean to eliminate metal stress not a method to be used to soften some location note do not service the body by heating oxygen acetylene apply tensile force to the predetermined location recover the size and shape of the damaged steel sheet carefully and slowly 1 0 589 body repair collision repair completely eliminate the stress of the curved steel sheet to achieve accurate repair of the body note overstretch shall be avoided during body correction the only solution to overstretch is to replace the damaged sheet metal 1 0 590 collision repair body repair panel repair panel repair method puller method for sheet metal repair meson machine thepullertractionmethodforsheetmetalrepairisintroduced in recent year for this method a number of flat gasket puller is welded according to the size of the damaged sheet metal the flat gasket puller is a traction medium insert the wire into the medium and draw the wire with a manual or mechanical manner apply force to the damaged sheet metal through the medium so that it can be drawn outward and resume its position and shape especially for larger deformation bilayer structure inaccessible location corner transition and door pillar etc the puller traction method provide a more convenient method for repair heat shrinkage method local heat shrinkage is a commonly used method for sheet metal repair deformation of the sheet metal refer to tensile deformationoftherelatedposition useheatshrinkagemethod to eliminate tensile stress and resume the shape hammer and jacking block method the hammer and jacking block method is a conventional method for sheet metal repair the metaler hold the hammer with one hand and the jacking block with the other hand or use other tool to strike the sheet metal for repair the manual work of the sheet metal require the metaler to have the appropriate skill and experience good repair skill cannot be obtained overnight repair of the sheet metal include repair of the deformed surface and correction of the shape line technical requirement for panel repair no matter which method is used to repair the body surface the specified technical requirement shall be met and the performance and appearance quality of the vehicle shall be resumed shape recovery for repair the shape of the large smooth structure and the wedge structure of the local transition shall be recovered when it is hard to recover the shape change the original shape in an appropriate way the shape shall be symmetrical beautiful firm and durable integrity and fineness of continuous surface most of the car body are produced by mould stamping which have the integrity and fineness of the surface for repair the streamline surface shall have a continuous transition the transition point of the surface shall have a smooth transition theexternalsurfacerepairedshallbeasbrightasanewsurface ripple wrinkle dent knock mark scratch and visible defect or obvious defect that can be sensed by a touch are not allowed especially for large scale repair ensure the integrity streamline feature continuity and fineness of the continuous surface sufficient strength and rigidity the body of the original design has sufficient strength and rigidity the body repaired shall ensure sufficient strength and rigidity thevibrationnoiseofthebodyrepairedshallbewithin thepermissiblerange abnormalvibrationnoiseshallnotoccur ensure the body has no any fatigue damage within the certain distance the entire body shall have the specified rigidity make sure the body panel is able to maintain its shape in use 1 0 591 body repair collision repair sheet metal replacement the process for sheet metal replacement is as follow 1 preparation before cutting new old sheet metal 2 remove damaged sheet metal 3 position adjustment and cutting of new part 4 treatment and corrosion prevention before welding 5 final position and welding of the panel 6 treatment after welding 7 accessory installation and inspection after operation principle for sheet metal replacement thin panel replacement thin panel replacement is made for aesthetic purpose and shall follow the requirement below 1 cutting is performed on the shortest position 15mm from the foam damage to the foam by welding shall be avoided positionpositionposition ofofof thethethe foamfoamfoam 2 when replacing the a pillar and rear fender please remove the windshield 3 do not cut location including any reinforcement thick panel replacement thick panel replacement is made for strength purpose and shall follow the requirement below 1 do not cut the energy absorption area 2 no cutting is allowed within 50mm from the front rear of the position for laser welding 3 cutting is performed on the shortest position 15mm from the foam damage to the foam by welding shall be avoided positionpositionposition ofofof thethethe foamfoamfoam 4 do not cut location including any reinforcement 5 to ensure welding penetration the size of the cutting seam shall be equal to the thickness of a panel preparation before cutting new old sheet metal 1 remove all sealer residue with hot air gun to reduce the toxic fume generated due to heat during welding 2 straighten the existing panel side seam with form block and hammer 3 indicate the actual cutting position on the old body part reserve 15mm paper adhesive tape and indicate the location for welding spot elimination weldingweldingwelding informationinformationinformation providedprovidedprovided bybyby manufacturermanufacturermanufacturer 4 indicate the actual cutting position on the new body part and paste the paper adhesive tape damaged sheet metal remove 1 position the welding spot 2 grind the welding spot 3 carry out rough cut and remove the welding spot 1 0 592 collision repair body repair 4 remove the damaged sheet metal position adjustment and cutting of new part 1 position the new panel on the body and adjust its position and size compare the hole adjust the clearance adjustthedifferencebetweenthehighsurfaceandthelow surface 2 cut the new old panel by overlap take care not to cut the reinforcement inside the body remove the new panel and cut the excess old panel section by section 3 mark the punch position for welding the plug hole treatment and corrosion prevention before welding 1 new body part grind the location for welding and sealant application to remove the paint film 2 grind the location for welding and sealant application to remove the old paint film burr and rust 20mm from the welding joint but do not grind the cross section of the joint 3 clean the location for welding and sealant application on the new part and body and wait for 5 10min to volatilise the detergent 4 paint zinc spray to the welding junction of the new part and body that doe not need sealant and control the thickness of the zinc spray 5 apply structure sealant before application remove and discard the sealant 10cm to ensure uniform bond of the panel welding shall be finished within the time specified by the structure sealant or bond of the panel will be affected final position and welding of the panel adjusting panel refit 1 confirm the position of the ridge 2 compare the hole 1 0 593 body repair collision repair 3 adjust surrounding clearance 4 adjust the difference between the high surface and the low surface position of gas shielded welding 1 select a gas ratio of 80 argon 20 co2 2 to ensure the accuracy of the position positioned welding shall be performed on both side of the ridge instead of on the ridge and the distance of the positioned welding shall be above 30mm trial welding before spot welding 1 select an electric welding arm for resistance spot welding and shape the electrode head with nozzle finisher finish the nozzle making its diameter equal to 2 time the welding thickness and plus another 3 0mm caution use arm not exceeding 300 mm in length 2 fit the resistance spot welding arm and test equipment with test silicon slice so as to meet the operation requirement if there is no monitoring device the weld strength can be tested by checking welding pool pull wire around the metal under tensile force during the stretch welding sequence welding sequence to decrease the possibility of strength reduction due to welding shunt it is recommended that you begin with the direct welding and follow the sequence shown below the distance between the welding spot shall be 25mm 1 0 594 collision repair body repair welding requirement if the resistance spot welding has been used in production a new resistance spot welding must be done at any other feasible position during spot welding to ensure the quality of the welding test coupon of the same metal gauge and material are recommended for peeling test if condition permit the welding quality monitor can be used to ensure welding quality if the satisfactory spot welding can not be achieved plug weld welding must be used length of the welding rod holder of hand spot welding gun must be no more than 300mm single sided spot welding is not allowed neither braze welding nor gas welding is allowed unless it is specified in production gas shielded welding plug welding when welding the region with 3 layer or more than 3 layer of panel or the welding nozzle is close to the restricted area and resistance spot welding is impossible use the hole made by the cutter for spot welding or punch or drill a hole to perform the mag plug welding the diameter of the hole for plug welding of the structural component is 8mm and that for plug welding of the covering component is 5mm the ratio of the welding spot for plug welding to the manufacturer s welding spot is 1 1 gas shielded welding butt welding treatment after welding process for corrosion prevention 1 use p36 disc polisher or belt polisher to grind or finish all welding location and clean the location 2 paint anti corrosion primer which can prevent corrosion and improve the adhesion of the sealant 3 apply sealant fill the seam between the panel to realise the leak prevention purpose positionpositionposition ofofof sealantsealantsealant 4 deliver the vehicle to the painter for painting 5 apply wax to the cavity before fitting the accessory informationinformationinformation ofofof cavitycavitycavity waxwaxwax applicationapplicationapplication 1 0 595 body repair painting painting overview the purpose of automotive painting is to provide an excellent appearance decoration gloss protection colour protection corrosion resistance and a longer service life so it has distinctive feature automotive painting is a high level protective painting the coating shall have a superb corrosion resistance weather resistance acid alkali resistance inclusion resistance and an extensive adaptability etc automotive painting mainly refer to body painting is a high level decorative painting and careful design good working environment and condition are required for providing the coating with good decoration vehicle decoration mainly rely on painting the quality of the painting has a direct influence on the commercial value of the vehicle automotive painting is the most typical industrial painting it require reasonable and effective pretreatment method drying method and process preparation automotive painting is a multi coating painting system and the painting quality and process control of each coating is of great importance coating introduction coating composition duringmanufacture paintapplicationconsistsofthecorrosion resistant coating zinc coating of the raw material zinc phosphate coating electrocoating intermediate coating and top coat aftermarket paint repair is composed of the primer coat putty coat intermediatecoating pigmentedpaintcoatingandvarnish coating function of each coating 1 zinc coating with a good function of anti corrosion anti rust good paint adhesion decorative performance and forming property 2 zinc phosphate coating the first paint that is directly applied to the treated workpiece surface and is the base for the whole coating its characteristic are as follow it shall have a good adhesion to the treated surface of the workpiece and the primer film formed shall have an excellent mechanical strength it shall serve as a corrosion inhibitor and the primer coating shall have an excellent corrosion resistance water resistance and chemical resistance it shall well fit the intermediate coating or top coat it shall have a good workability 3 electrocoating enhance cavity corrosion resistance improve the uniformity and leveling of the paint film thickness raise the quality of the paint film painting efficiency and paint utilization efficiency 4 putty coat used for the object surface with primer precoat so as to fill the pit weld seam scratch rust hole etc on the object surface until the surface become smooth 5 intermediate coating it is the last intermediate coat before applying the top coat its binder content is between the primer and the top coat and the paint film is bright and semi bright it provide the following function enhance the adhesion between the primer and the top coat increase the total thickness of the coating and improve the film build improve the corrosion resistance and weather resistance of the primer top coat repair minor defect on the substrate surface raise the decoration performance of the top coat 1 0 596 painting body repair 6 top coat top coat is used for the final coating of the multi coating it directly affect the decoration performance weather resistance gloss protection colour protection chemical resistance contaminant resistance and appearance of the vehicle classification of top coat the classification of the top coat by colour effect is as follow pure pigmented paint containing resin pigment and additive only metallic paint containing metal powder with better gloss and saturation pear paint comparing with the metallic paint instead of using metallic paint it use mica powder coated with titanium dioxide and ferrum dioxide to improve the texture and gloss of the paint film by application procedure it can be classified into the following process single process a system that use one type of paint to form a complete surface coating single process pure pigmented paint and metallic paint are included double process asystemthatusestwodifferenttypesof paint to form a complete surface coating generally the pigmented paint is applied before the finishing varnish is painted double process pure pigmented paint metallic paint and pearl paint are included three process usually a layer of primer is required for thissystem thenalayerofpearlpaint andfinallyalayerof finishing varnish the combination of these three coating form a complete surface coating three process pearl paint and coloured varnish are included principle of paint repair the following principle must be followed when repairing the painted surface 1 use the certified jointing sealant to seal the inner and outer jointing 2 repair all damaged part of the sealant under the vehicle body 3 use two pack paint to repair the system and it shall be consistent with the original production part 4 apply the inner wax to the entire inner surface without paint finishing 5 steam cleaner or high pressure cleaner must be used to clean the vehicle thoroughly before paint repair 6 clean the local part needing requirement with warm and water soluble cleaning solution then thoroughly clean it with solvent painting must be done immediately after this operation 7 in order to thoroughly clean the area of exposed metal due to damage of the painted surface the cleaning area shall be larger than the original damaged area 8 use phosphate to eliminate the dust contaminant etc on the exposed metal and offer a good repair environment for the new coating 1 0 597 body repair painting painting process generally automobilepaintingcanbedividedintotwopart 1 metal treatment before painting also known as pretreatment 2 application process surface pretreatment surface pretreatment consist of the following work 1 surface cleaning before painting thoroughly remove the mud dirt other foreign material on the vehicle especially the dirt on the door frame luggage compartment engine cover seam and wheel house failure to do so may cause the new paint film to be contaminated treatment of dissolved substance apply degreaser to the surface with the special cloth then remove the degreaser and grease residue etc with a clean and dry cloth treatment of moisture and dust use a clean and dry cloth toremovemoistureanddust anddrywithahigh pressure air gun 2 coating identification and damage assessment coating identification identification of the coating on the body panel is crucial for the reapplication process improper identification may lead to serious problem for top coat application if no respray is performed the type of the coating can be determined by the vehicle manual but the type of the coating for the resprayed body shall be determined for repair damage assessment correct assessment of the damage is one of the key factor for determining the service cost and ensuring the quality of painting range of the repair treatment process transitional region location to be covered for protection and part to be removed etc are determined by correct assessment of the damage this lay the foundation for correct implementation of the subsequent process and guarantee of a satisfactory repair quality assessment of the damage to the paint film include visual inspection touch and ruler evaluation 3 removing old paint film clean the body and check the body paint carefully to see if there is any sign of damage such as bubble crack peeling rust and partial damage caused by baking repair gas welding etc for the above damage the old paint film shall be removed selection of partial elimination or full elimination of the old paint film is made according to the degree of the damage and the quality requirement after recoating sandpaper p80 p180 is recommended for grinding feather edge not less than 3cm precaution for mechanical grinding a do not use an electric grinding machine to grind surface with a big radian highly raised surface or concave surface in a cross way b do not use an electric grinding machine to grind the corner wrinkle seam weld junction or location with plastic sealant c for grinding the grinding machine can be started only when it contact the surface of the sheet metal in this process donotapplytoomuchforce ordeepgroove may occur and the grinding machine shall be aligned with the edge line to be ground before initiation of the machine d to avoid thermal deformation of the sheet metal do not stay the grinding machine at one position for a prolonged time e do not allow coarse grinding material to contact with the intact paint surface of the accessory in the grinding area it s better to protect the intact coating with tape operation of sanding a fine processing of the surface treated by coarse grinding its purpose is to acquire a flatter surface align the front of the rotating grinding wheel the surface to be ground and slightly keep the rear away from the surface maintain this angle move the 1 0 598 painting body repair grinding machine up and down the proper coverage area of each grinding mark is 50 60 replacing sheet metal use sandpaper p280 p320 to grind the electrophoretic primer by dry grinding 4 degreasing and descaling of the metal surface remove surface rust descaling of the metal include manual descaling polishing grinding and high pressure water etc mechanical method chemical method and ultrasonic method different rust apply different method for descaling clean the metal surface again 1 0 599 body repair painting coating application for paint repair of the pretreated sheet metal of the finished surface please refer to the detailed process below 1 primer application cover the area around the region to be repaired use a soft cloth with detergent to wipe the surface to be painted and use another clean cloth to wipe off the detergent residue on the surface purge the surface with compressed air spray anti rust primer and dry it 2 putty application not required for new part mix the putty and curing agent according to the ratio specified by the instruction use a clean putty mixing plate only mix putty enough for current use fill sandpaper mark and pore first then apply putty in the full scale flash off for 5min after application and bake it with the infrared baking lamp according to the instruction of the putty wear dust mask select appropriate sandpaper and hard grinding block rail grinding machine can be used and take care not to grind the surrounding area when using a grinding block grind in the horizontal direction and begin with the top then move down gradually clean the repair surface with an air gun use a soft cloth with detergent to wipe and dry with another clean cloth 3 intermediate coat application cover the area around the repair region prepare to apply intermediate coat the surrounding of the covered area shall be larger than the area to be sprayed above 10cm use a soft cloth with detergent to wipe the surface to be painted and use another clean cloth to wipe off the detergent residue on the surface purge the surface with compressed air mix intermediate coat curing agent and diluent according to the ratio specified by the instruction apply a thin film of intermediate coat first flash off for 5min and make two wet application after a levelling of 10 15min cure the paint film with an infrared baking lamp coat a grinding indicator layer on the surface so that the uneven surface can be easily found during grinding 1 0 600 painting body repair perform coarse grinding with p240 p400 sandpaper first then perform fine grinding with sandpaper p600 p800 remove the covering paper from the surrounding of the repair area purge the inner outer surface of the repair area with compressed air and thoroughly remove the abrasive debris and moisture 4 top coat application check the colour code and check the colour formulation according to the code polish the body surface around the repair area to acquire correct colour check for the difference between the colour mixed and that of the original body in the stage fluorescent lamp or sunlight when it is consistent with the vehicle to be painted dilution ratio distance pressure paint output temperature flash off time etc paint the colour test panel flash off the test panel for 15min bake the test panel as per the specified temperature and time take care not to overheat the test panel during baking use a soft cloth with detergent to wipe the area to be repaired and dry with another clean cloth coverthesurroundingoftherepairarea pastepaperfrom the inside of the repair area and take care not to tear the covering paper purge the panel to be painted and the vehicle with compressed air take care not to damage the covering paper then wipe the surface with a cloth to prevent dust particle etc from entering into the paint use a strainer to pour the mixed paint into the cup of the spray gun carry out a trial application check the barometric pressure paint output sector and atomization spray two wet paste coating the leveling of each shall be 5 10min for silver paint virtual spraying can be carried out at the last to guarantee the silver powder is uniform 5 varnish application after the pigmented paint is applied flash off for about 15min when the paste coating lose its gloss completely apply varnish spray wet varnish first flash off for 5 10min spray the second wet varnish flash off for 15min before baking note do not turn off the ventilation system of the spray booth during drying use the temperature and duration specified by the product to bake the paint film infrared baking lamp can also be used to perform a forced drying for 15min afterbaking furthertreatmentcanbedoneonlywhenthe paint film is completely cool such as grinding polishing etc 6 polishing waxing when the paint film is completely cool use sandpaper p1500 or p2000 to grind the dust sag etc on the paint film use slack wax and wool wheel to grind the location to be polished at the beginning of polishing the speed of the polisher shall not be high the speed shall be increased gradually polish with polishing wax and sponge wheel use a clean sponge wheel use polishing wax to remove the swirl mark caused by polishing to restore the gloss of the paint film check if the colour of the painted location is correct in the lamplight then check it in the sunlight 7 hairdressing cleaning 1 0 601 body repair painting 8 process information for paint of different brand recommend paint brand information ppg sherwin william akzo nobel primer p565 895 p565 9850 p565 9085 e2g973
