paint awx water based paint sikken oily paint sikken autowave water based paint 1 0 602 painting body repair general film coating defect paint film defect caused by application and treatment particle the phenomenon that bump in the film coating are spread over the whole or local surface in granular form analysis of possible cause poor air cleanliness and impurity in the painting environment such as dust worker wear dirty clothe or the fiber contained in the material are easy to fall out substrate that are not properly handled have bump the vehicle body is not cleaned thoroughly etc the painting pressure is insufficient atomization is poor the spray gun is not cleaned up and an improper screen is selected the pigment or flashing material in the paint are badly dispersed the paint are unfiltered or filtered improperly the paint become deteriorated and show abnormal sign such as separate out flocculation the film is moved out of the baking booth before drying the surface to dust free preventive measure ensure that the spraying environment is clean ensure that the object to be painted and worker are cleaned thoroughly before entering the baking booth ensure the paint performance the deteriorated and poorly dispersed paint are not applicable set the optimal parameter for paint application and use the spray gun to spray after the paint being filtered defect treatment the tiny dirty spot can be polished after removed by abrasive paper if the impurity particle are large or the dirty spot on the surface of primer and even surfacer can only be ground to the extent that the spot disappear completely perform the spraying again sag upon the liquid paint is sprayed onto the workpiece surface some wet film surface run down creating unevenly stripe and sag which are thin on the top and thick on the bottom resulting in the local thicker film on vertical surface it can be divided into sinking sagging and running depending on the shape of sag analysis of possible cause the painted workpiece surface is too cold low application environment temperature slow wind speed and high solvent vapor content in the ambient air low product temperature low spraying pressure slow gunning speed short distance to spray gun thicker film large caliber of spray gun poor atomization of spray gun slow solvent evaporation rate and low paint viscosity short distance between spray gun and workpiece slower gunning speed thicker spraying coating insufficient flash off interval between layer preventive measure improve the painting environment control the appropriate ambient temperature humidity and wind speed choose the correct solvent and pay attention to select the dissolving capacity and evaporation rate of the solvent strictly control the viscosity and the temperature for coating application reducetheamountofpaintsfromspraygun andincrease the gun distance and gunning speed full flash off between layer defect treatment for the slightly local varnish sagging it can be sanded to eliminate the sagging defect before being polished if this defect exist or if the sagging defect is more severe or it appear on the surfacer layer and the color paint layer finally revealed on the layer of varnish the sagging must be sanded thoroughly and make the spraying again pin hole eye theblistersorsolventbubblesappearonthefilmandthereare small needle hole on the top of them which are referred to as pin hole the solvent contained in the film coating evaporate rapidly during the surface drying its left trace form hole these pin hole are known as defect that the hole are still visibleonthesurfaceaftersprayingthefinishduetosomehole on the substrate are not filled up they are easily generated at the corner of workpiece analysis of possible cause pin hole the paint are mixed with impurity for example water enter into the solvent based coating the painted object have dirt on their surface the flash off interval between layer is insufficient during application the continuous spraying is too thick the temperature rise up suddenly and the surface is dried rapidly when stoving pin eye the uneven location on the original workpiece surface are not filled up precaution and improvement set appropriate temperature and humidity for application wind speed and other condition 1 0 603 body repair painting mix the paint and solvent in proportion to ensure the proper fluidness it should be heated up slowly to avoid the rapid solvent evaporation full flash off interval between layer defect treatment the defect should be completely ground to remove the pin hole and pin eye then repaint it blistering part of the film coating float off the painted surface or prime coat and is filled with liquid or gas which is about 1 5mm in diameter or float off in large piece if there is some water under the film the vapor generated by evaporation of water will plump up the film to form blister when the temperature rise up analysis of possible cause the ambient humidity is high and the film is dried naturally after spraying resulting in moisture infiltrating into the film the substrate is not dried properly the drying time is short between coating the heating and drying are too fast grind the putty while adding water and the putty absorb water there is oil or water in the compressed air line preventive measure grind the dry putty ensure that the previous coating has met the requirement for cleanness and dryness before spraying reduce the humidity in the painting and spraying room use the curing agent and diluent suitable for environment temperature make it dry as soon as possible once the spraying is completed to prevent the moisture from infiltrating into the film defect treatment once blister appear on the film surface this surface should be ground to remove the blistering defect completely then respray it depending on the surface condition orange skin the orange skin is used to describe the leveling property of the film when you observe the film surface against the light you can see different ripple of 1 5mm presenting on the film surface and the brightness and plumpness vary in different ripple area such texture on the film surface which is similar to orange peel is known as orange skin analysis of possible cause the substrate is not flat or the finish coating is sprayed to the surfacer surface without having the orange skin ground smooth the ambient temperature is high the wind speed is fast and the substrate temperature is high the diluent volatiliz rapidly the paint has high viscosity and the film is too thin the distance from spray gun is too far the gunning speed is too fast the spraying pressure is low and the amount of paint out of spray gun is little preventive measure grind the intermediate coating completely smooth according to the process requirement control the ambient temperature and wind speed 0 2 0 6m s use the diluent suitable for that temperature and adjust the paint viscosity as per the paint manufacturer product instruction adjust the gun pressure then regulate the gun to proper distance and reduce the gunning speed as per the manufacturer instruction spray the appropriate layer to ensure that the film get the desired thickness defect treatment only perform the grinding and polishing if the defect of orange skin is slight the orange skin should be ground to completely removed and then be resprayed if the defect is serious sanding scratch the sanding scratch left by sanding substrate are visible on the film after spraying the paint sometime the sanding scratch on the substrate will extend due to the erosion caused by solvent contained in the film the upper film would be subsided gradually after a period resulting in the sanding scratch more and more obvious analysis of possible cause if the improper abrasive paper are used to sand the substrate the sanding scratch will be wide and unable to be covered and filled up by upper film if the lower film is sanded and sprayed with upper film over it before it isn t totally dry it is easy to make the sanding scratch to extend due to the erosion caused by solvent in the upper film and form the sanding scratch defect the diluent that has high solubility for upper paint or too slow to dry has been misused preventive measure 1 0 604 painting body repair when you select the abrasive paper number to make sanding you should observe the principle that the abrasive paper number should not be jumped by 2 level each replacement ensure that the lower film is totally dry before sanding select the correct upper film solvent as per the product instruction defect treatment for slight sanding scratch they can be removed or made less obvious by sanding and polishing as they have been filled up by paint for the sanding scratch that are more obvious they cannot be removed by sanding and polishing it should be resprayed after removing the scratch putty mark a putty mark mean the defect that trace is left along the edge of putty application area after spraying the surfacer or finish analysis of possible cause the feather edge are improperly treated before putty application the putty is mixed with curing agent in improper proportion the next action is taken before the putty is completely dry sanding spraying the surfacer etc the putty is not flat enough the feather edge do not meet the standard and it is higher or lower than the old surrounding coating or metal the curing agent and diluent mixed in the surfacer or finish are dried slowly or the flash off interval between layer are too short when spraying the surfacer or finish andthesolventpenetratesanderodestheputty resulting in the edge expanded and extended preventive measure the width of the feather edge must be 20 30mm and the surface is smooth without step before putty application adding the curing agent in proper proportion according to the product instruction make sure to perform the sanding and spray the surfacer when the putty is totally dry use the correct curing agent and diluent and ensure the flash off interval between layer is enough the periphery of putty must be sanded to be qualified not only the putty but also the extended old surrounding coating should be sanded when you sand the putty edge to allow the putty together with the surrounding to form flat surface defect treatment once the putty mark appear those area should be sanded and apply the putty again and respray the surfacer and finish lifting the lower layer paint is single component or even if it is two component it is not dry and cured completely including improper amount of curing agent is used when the paint is sprayed above it the solvent contained in upper film will erode the lower film causing the uplift and shrink present on the lower film surface analysis of possible cause the lower film is not totally dry the lower film become deteriorated the continuous spraying of surfacer or finish result in too thick surface or the used curing agent and diluent are too slow to dry or the diluent with high solubility is used or the flash off interval between layer are too short when spraying the surfacer or finish or the low air temperature during the spray cause the infiltration and erosion of solvent contained in the surfacer or finish resulting in the lifting occurred at the putty edge one component putty or surfacer edge preventive measure for the film not totally dry it must be sanded and sprayed with the next layer paint after it is completely dry if it cannot be totally dry due to the improper adding of curing agent it need to respray the paint mixed with proper curing agent after film removal for the deteriorated film shown as chalking cracking loss of gloss they must be removed totally and resprayed with two component surfacer or finish use the correct mating curing agent and diluent and ensure the flash off interval between layer is enough use the two component putty and surfacer defect treatment when the lifting defect appear it should be sanded to remove any defect at the lifting area if the lifting is severe the film that has lifting should also be sanded and removed completely then spray the two component surfacer and finish paint film defect caused by operating environment and treatment paint film defect caused by external corrosive material defect phenomenon the film defect occur when the film is affected by the acidic and alkaline material as well as industrial fall out such as asphalt oil battery electrolyte bird dropping sap acid rain pollen dust produced by steel plant and black carbon from boiler stack it generally appear 1 0 605 body repair painting as uplift discolor and even cracking and peeling caused by the contaminant infiltrating into the film defect treatment if the film defect is tiny it can be recovered only by polishing the surface damage if the film has been discolored the defective area should be sanded until eliminating the defect then respray it water spot defect phenomenon when the rainwater on the film surface or the tap water left by car washing is heated to evaporate a white ring drop mark will be left on the water drop area and cannot be removed by common cleaning method cause as the water contain mineral substance such as calcium when the water is heated to evaporate some warm water will infiltrate into the softened film with heat and the left mineral substance infiltrate into the coating defect prevention and treatment try not to wash the car under the sunlight and wipe the water drop off the vehicle surface after washing to avoid evaporation caused by heating it should only be polished when water spot appear stone crash damage defect phenomenon some small stone may hit the film and cause the film peeling during vehicle traveling the area where the film is peeled off usually form the surface with sharp and jagged edge sometime a small indentation will appear in the middle of this area the front edge of the vehicle engine hood or the roof is usually damaged by stone defect treatment when the stone crash damage occur the film on the defective area must be removed and sanded to feather edge and filled up with putty or surfacer then continue to apply the surfacer and finish if the area hit by stone is rusted the rust must be removed thoroughly when new original part are sprayed if the finish is directly sprayed onto the electrophoretic coating without spraying surfacer and when the plastic part are sprayed if the surfacer and finish are not added with softening agent depending on the plastic hardness the stone crash protection for the film will be reduced and the film is easy to hit off by stone and sand discolor chalking loss of gloss defect phenomenon as the vehicle expose to the ultraviolet ray the pigment deterioration of the film cause color fading the resin deterioration cause yellowing the film peel off as powder and lose gloss defect treatment color fading is a representation of film aging it is usually shown up together with chalking and full loss of gloss once any one of these defect occur the defective film must be sanded and removed thoroughly then respray the two component surfacer and finish 1 0 606 seal body repair seal overview process area of underbody seal underbody area and doorsill outer panel should be processed with plastisol pvc underbody sealer this sealer shall not be reused when repairing the sealer for the underbody area the underbody sealer upon delivery should be removed and returned to the proper breakpoint expose the clean metal surface and the current sealing edge shall also fit firmly to the panel use new chassis sealant between the primer and the intermediate primer if necessary use the seam sealant before applying the chassis sealant before coating the chassis sealant fit the plug and insulating ring on the disk type floor except the part for wax spray refit the heat fusible plug damaged in repair with heat blower or replace them with rubber insulating ring before using new chassis sealant cover the suspension wheel tyre power unit exhaust system and brake system including all mounting point seam sealant for factory assembly apply pvc sealant treated by baking to special joint weld and sheet metal edge this kind of material is not suitable for repair so approved special sealant shall be used for aftersale service seam sealant is used after the primer and before the intermediate primer and outer coat the spray of the seam sealant must be smooth and continuous and the spray line depend on the type of the seam if the sealant is coated by brush pay special attention to the coverage area required for the weld at the place needing sealant formation use cloth dipped in the solvent oil to complete the required operation remember to seal all the seam to be processed after each repair the area away from the joggled joint often distort when the vehicle is damaged thus sealant for these place will frequently occur in the following repair and correction check all the weld around the area to be repaired to see whether it is damaged then process the damage as required then coat new sealant specific step are as follow clean the affected seam re process the exposed metal area with proper etch primer coat proper sealant at the required place process the affected area with acid etch primer chassis sealant is also acceptable and apply proper colour paint film after reassembly or refit of part if the seam is plugged then apply paste seam sealant to such seam blockage of the seam may occur after panel repair in this case apply sealant and paint before final assembly if the inlet is of proper size apply seam sealant to the two side of the joint to be repaired if the spray can be only done to one side such as compartment then use cavity wax to process the affected compartment beam seam which require sealant when assembling in the plant is described in the figure below caution look through all the joint near the fixed area if necessary all the combined fixed or replaced area should be sealed these area can t be ruined sealing could protect from water and wind noise and so on before the final inspection it is necessary to look through all the sealed area 1 0 607 body repair seal application of pvc sealant process operation method implementation effect section come to conclusion fast only by naked eye test tool and instrument whencheckingandcorrectingtheleakingproblem thebelow tool and instrument are recommended to use 1 garden sprayer manual 2 wet dry vacuum cleaner 3 dry absorbent cloth 4 battery torch 5 mirror 6 weatherstrip locating tool 7 trim panel remover 8 small wooden or plastic wedge 9 dry compressed air supply 10 hot blower 11 sealer applicator 12 ultrasonic leak detector when checking for leak at least the following part should be checked front inner space rear seat space storage compartment or luggage compartment test method and measure the operator at the fitting center can judge from where to check the leakage by information offered by the customer the next step is to find the leaking spot after the leaking place is founded for the first case an easy and effective method is the common garden sprayer nozzle of this extruding garden sprayer ought to be adjusted so that water can be sucked into the nozzle directly and spray out proper spray at the dark corner a mirror or battery torch cab be used the test light using supply voltage is not available the test order is very important begin from the lowest point and go up gradually this can avoid blocking off the other leaking place when an area is being checked if the test is begun from the windscreen any water flowing into the pumping chamber may drop on the pedal by baffle insulating ring even though it may be judged wrongly that seal of the windscreen fail another part for leaking check is to visually observe whether the door hole sealer insulating ring or weather strip is damaged aged or displaced meanwhile align the door with the seal note check the colour of the water around the leak if the water is dirty the leak is from under the vehicle if the water is clean it is from above when a leaking spot is found the method below are necessary for resolution of the leak problem 1 replace all of the damaged displaced and aged door hole seal and weatherstrip 2 check all the body seal and ensure it is fixed on the fixing flange fixing face correctly if necessary the simitar can be used 3 blow the body weld seam to be processed with compressed air and or hot blower when necessary 4 if necessary apply sealant to the outside of the connector to prevent water 5 when correcting leakage of the windshield and weatherstrip or polish between the upper glass and the body directly try not to remove the glass use qualified material at proper place such as glass to the weatherstrip or glass to the body initial door seal replacement the initial seal for both front and rear door are stuck on the specified position alway comply with the regulation below when replacing the weatherstrip clean the position where the door weatherstrip is located do not clean the door with paint diluent agent the ideal temperature for sticking the weatherstrip is 25 77 for either the door or the weatherstrip when the temperature is less than 18 64 it is hard to stick the weatherstrip do not apply too much force to stick the weatherstrip this will make disassembly or re installation much easier if the weatherstrip shall be refitted do not touch the contact face or stain it 1 0 609 body repair seal sealant position information body seal identification 1 between panel connected with bolt 7 riveting joint type a 2 panel edge connected with bolt 8 riveting repair gasket size information 1 0 625 body repair corrosion protection corrosion protection overview treatment in production during production the vehicle body apply the following method for anti corrosion treatment use galvanized steel sheet phosphate anti corrosion primer dip coating electrophoresis chip resistant coating underbody coating spray on the underbody of the main floor and rear floor and on the rear wheel opening cavity wax spray on the sheet metal part such as doorsill and rear window sill side extension and the lower sheet metal of the door protective wax spray on the opening not covered by the wheel house whenever repairing the body make sure the anti corrosion material from the repair area is also repaired or updated ensure the material used are the approved anti corrosion material precaution for body repair and operation in the workshop it is necessary to exercise caution in the operation of the vehicle for example when lifting the vehicle any carelessness and negligence can cause damage to the underbody sealant seam sealing material underbody sealing wax and body panel etc when fitting the accessory ensure that the vehicle anti corrosion protection is not impaired by broken protective layer or moisture do not screw the self tapping screw directly into the body panel pre fix the matching plastic insert to the body panel alway brush the sheet metal chassis component and drill hole edge of other body part with proper zinc rich or acid etch primer and brush the surrounding area with wax never contact the painted metal surface of the part with the body without taking appropriate protection measure between the metal surface requiring bolt connection be sure to have appropriate media such as weldable zinc rich primer or zinc strip etc the heat from the steam cleaning equipment has the risk of damaging some decorative part softening or liquefying certain adhesive and anti corrosion material so the equipment shall be adjusted so that the nozzle temperature doe not exceed 90 194 do not have the steam nozzle stay in one place for a long period of time the distance between the nozzle and the sheet metal surface shall be at least above 300mm during service do not remove the protective wax or paint from the underbody or lower part of the engine cover if the above area shall be cleaned with steam then apply a new layer of protective wax or an underbody protection layer as soon as possible maintenance check when doing the anti corrosion check on the body observe the following step lift the vehicle visually inspect the underbody sealant for any damage lower the vehicle check the body exterior paint for damage and the body panel for corrosion note if the vehicle is dirty it will need to be washed prior to inspection of bodywork the above check refer only to visual check when checking for corrosion and paint damage do not remove the decorative panel or sound insulation material 1 lift the vehicle and use the test lamp or spotlight to perform a visual check for the following corrosion damage and paint damage and condition of the lower panel of the front rear vehicle doorsill and the underbody sealant at the wheel opening damage to underbody sealant erosion condition in surrounding area of the suspension mounting point and fuel tank mounting point note for unexposed metal the little bubble on the underbody sealing material can be neglected pay special attention to damage of the sheet metal or anti corrosion material due to incorrect lifting method warning it is essential to follow the correct jacking and lifting procedure 2 after lowering the vehicle visually check all the visible painted surface for sign of damage and corrosion and pay special attention to the following area leading edge of the engine cover visible flanging of the engine compartment underbody and door panel take feasible measure to correct or repair any body damage or corrosion found during the inspection as soon as possible these include diminishing breakage and ensuring the long term effectiveness of the anti corrosion treatment performed in the production if the corroded area is increasingly clear and diffuse or expand from the bottom of the replaceable part such as various trim panel window glass seat etc then replace the part to achieve the most effective repair 1 0 626 corrosion protection body repair the repair work on damaged area but also to the recovery work on the protective layer of certain area indirectly weakened due to accident or maintenance operation before straightening or doing panel repair you must remove the corrosion protection layer of the damaged area it s particularly important especially for the panel with a protective wax layer the underbody with polyvinyl chloride pvc step 1 remove the existing underbody coating 2 after the sheet metal are repaired use solvent to clean the affected area and use the etching phosphate material to process bare metal 3 apply new primer after repairing the sheet metal at the damaged wheel opening spray new wax which is also applicable for the painted surface and underbody sealant remove the old wax on the underbody and the range of the underbody wax is at least 200mm larger than the range of the new underbody sealant note where repair include the application of finish paint coat in the area requiring underbody wax carry out paint operation before applying wax cavity wax injection after repair be sure to use approved cavity wax for treatment in addition for all inner surface damaged by the repair whether these area have been treated in the production process they shall be subject to a cavity wax treatment this include all the cavity and the door interior if necessary allow additional hole to be made for wax injection but these hole can not be located in the load bearing part make sure that these hole are treated by using the proper zinc rich primer and brushed with protective wax and finally sealed with the rubber ring before injecting the wax make sure the cavity to be treated is free of contaminant and foreign material if necessary use compressed air to remove the debris in the cavity alway ensure cavity wax injection is performed after the final finish and before re assembling other component during the spray process ensure that the cavity wax cover all the flange and seam and be fully distributed to the repair area of all new and existing sheet metal it is noteworthy that there is no wax injection process when assembling new sheet metal and complete body shell such operation shall be performed after repair it is quite essential to take efficient cavity wax protective measure alway observe the following point before wax injection ensure the paint repair work is completed if necessary clean the body panel area and blow the cavity prior to the operation during wax injection and drying the temperature should be at 18 64 check the pattern of the injection equipment 1 0 627 body repair corrosion body repair corrosion protection chassis adhesive function of chassis adhesive improve the stone chipping resistance of the underbody improve the corrosion resistance of the underbody sheet metal improve the seam sealing performance of the underbody sheet metal improve the sound insulation performance of the underbody caution ensure that suspension unit wheel tyre power unit exhaust and brake including all mounting point are shielded prior to application of fresh underbody sealer operation procedure for chassis adhesive 1 cleaning to ensure the maximum viscosity of the product the underbody surface shall be clean and free from any dirt oil stain grease or wax residue 2 inspection before operation thoroughly inspect the underbody for any potential damage or rust key location wheel arch lower side seam and overlap joint 3 dust elimination eliminate the rust spot on the underbody with a shovel or wire brush pay particular attention to the inaccessible location for example wheel frame overlap or seam 4 check the type of the underbody coating check the type of the underbody coating and select a suitable product 5 read use instruction before operation please carefully read the instruction on the package of the product and view the technical data sheet read the safety instruction in the material safety data sheet carefully so that you can minimize some potential risk and hazard and achieve the optimum effect 6 use cross spray technique spray the rust prevention product at a distance of around 30cm spray in the horizontal direction first then spray the stiffener of the base plate from a proper angle cross spray technique to achieve an optimum thickness 1 5mm of the wet film at least three spray shall be done 7 drying time a specific drying time shall be defined the coating material shall adhere to the underbody firmly and permanently different solution and different coating thickness may require different drying time product with high volatile solution require 2hr drying time at least 8 clean spray gun use spray gun detergent to clean the spray gun immediately after use 1 0 634 welding process body repair welding process welding process overview characteristic of welding process it is applicable to any shape and can be used to connect the integral body and the vehicle body integrity can be maintained after welding mass reduction can be achieved due to lack of the connecting part good sealing performance for air and water high production efficiency the welding connector strength is affected by the skill level of the operator excessive heating may cause surrounding panel deformation for modern vehicle the body welding repair usually adopt mag shielded arc welding resistance spot welding and soldering for the body panel welding repair an optimum welding method that will not reduce the original strength and durability of the body shall be applied depending on the specific situation spot welding or inert gas welding is preferred mag shielded arc welding mag shielded arc welding is mainly used for welding high strength low alloy steel body and aluminum casting etc advantage of the mag shielded arc welding include easy operation this method can melt the base metal completely low current can be used to weld thin metal which can diminish damage to the adjacent area strength stable and controllable arc suitable for welding location with gap and misalignment temperature and duration of the welding is controllable short heating time of the welding zone reduce fatigue and deformation of the base metal typical welding location and method butt welding fit two adjacent metal edge together fit mutually along the two metal panel or weld the butted edge overlap welding weld two overlapping metal panel plug welding punch a hole on one or several exterior workpiece arc pass through the hole before entering into the interior workpiece and the hole is filled with the melting metal spot welding when the wire feed timing pulse is triggered current will be introduced into the two metal panel to be welded resistance spot welding the resistance spot welding is the most common method for welding the integral body this method can be used for the non deformable thin steel sheet on the body which require a high welding strength suitable for roof window doorsill and external component etc the quality of the resistance spot welding depend on the voltage current pressure time and thickness of the metal panel to be welded etc the detail are as follow material and cleanness of the weldment the location between the weldment or the contact point between the weldment and the electrode shall be clean electrode pressing force insufficient force can cause spatter on the contact point excessive force can generate large current but it will lead to small welding spot diameter and penetration due to increase of the heat distribution zone welding current the welding spot diameter and welding strength increase with the current and large current and small pressure may also lead to spatter between the panel the spatter will be otherwise minimized welding time long welding time will cause much heat large diameter and deep penetration of the welding spot however prolonged welding time will lead to electrode impress and thermal deformation rather than increase of the welding spot arrangement of the welding spot the gap and margin of the welding spot distance between the spot and the panel edge is also crucial for determining the welding spot strength welding spot gap decrease can improve the weldment connection strength but the effect is actually limited excessive gap may cause the welding current to transfer through the last welding spot or leak then the added welding spot will not strengthen the connection strength of the weldment any more and it will have an adverse impact therefore the gap of the welding spot shall be beyond the area of current leak correct welding sequence shall be applied for the resistance spot welding continuous welding in one direction is generally not allowed when the electrode tip is hot and discolour stop welding and allow it to cool down to avoid crack due to stress concentration do not weld the boundary of the corner 1 0 635 body repair welding process welding protection measure electronic control unit before welding repair is operated in consideration of the electronic control unit ecu refitted on the vehicle the following protection measure must be followed the plentiful heat and sharp vibration produced during these operation may damage the electronic control unit when disconnect or remove the airbag control module sdm pay special attention to the following proper protection measure protection measure for assistant protection system implement and remove or replace any item related to the assistant protection system srs be especially careful and comply with proper protection measure equipment when starting any test on the vehicle ensure that the related test equipment work normally and all harness and connector are in good state this is especially important to the electronic control unit seat belt rivet joint seat belt rivet joint is crucial for safety when repairing these area it is important to follow the following design specification note high strength low alloy steel hsla can be used at the installation position for seat belt try best to use the original production assembly including rivet joint of seat belt or the layout of cutting line should not interfere with the original rivet joint of seat belt carefully check the welding quality of the jointing for seat belt rivet joint within 250mm including the spot welding spacing 1 0 636 welding process body repair welding part signal c hard wire 1 0 670 vehicle access safety and restraint description security system the function of the security system is to prevent illegal start of the vehicle when the ignition switch is turned to acc position the anti theft system can be disabled by the transmitter on the remote key this transmitter can be identified by the reader coil around the ignition switch and the coil transmit electric power and data to the remote key in a frequency within a certain distance away from the signal reader coil the security system include door switch luggage compartment switch bonnet switch security warning lamp hazard warning lamp button remote key ignition switch body control module bcm door switch each door lock assembly contain a microswitch to notify the bcm of the door status when the door is open the microswitch contact will close to connect the ground circuit of the bcm when the door is closed the microswitch contact will disconnect and convert into an open circuit door switch bonnet and luggage compartment open switch are the main component of the alarm system when the alarm system work these switch will monitor the external intrusion into the vehicle the door open signal is also used by the bcm to indicate the door status information in the instrument pack information centre and control the interior lighting luggage compartment switch the luggage compartment lock motor contain a microswitch used to notify the bcm of the luggage compartment lid status when the luggage compartment lid is closed the microswitch contact disconnect and the circuit of the bcm become an open circuit when the luggage compartment lid is open the switch will be closed and the ground circuit of the bcm will be connected canwhen the bus detect that the ground circuit is connected it will trigger the alarm the luggage compartment open microswitch is also used by the bcm to indicate the luggage compartment status information in the instrument pack information centre and automatically control the interior lighting 1 0 671 safety and restraint vehicle access operation overview operation of vehicle access system the vehicle security system include the following three part 1 peripheral system bonnet alarm if fitted door and luggage compartment lid open switch ignition switch central lock lock unlock switch 2 acoustic alarm vehicle horn 3 visible alarm hazard warning lamp the security system is divided into three independent zone and each zone include many discrete input signal see the table below when the security system is working any intrusion into these zone can trigger the alarm safety zone discrete signal 1 front bonnet open switch 2 middle inside driver door open switch front passenger door open switch left rear door open switch right rear door open switch driver door key unlock timed for 15s wrong key is inserted into and placed in acc on position central lock lock unlock switch 3 rear luggage compartment lid release switch complete anti theft status when the three zone of the security system are closed if a lock request from the remote control or mechanical key is received the security system formally enter the complete anti theft status once in the complete anti theft status all activated safety zone will be monitored at all time when the switch of zone 2 or 3 is enabled if you attempt to lock the system from outside the vehicle will enter the partial lock status and give an error lock alarm bcmit will monitor the following outside lock request signal valid lock request from the remote key is received valid lock request from the driver door lock switch is received when the security system enter the complete anti theft status the bcm will allow the direction indicator lamp to give three flash complete anti theft status to partial anti theft status in the complete anti theft system if a valid unlock request of the luggage compartment lid is received the bcm open the luggage compartment lid and the security system enter the partial anti theft status partial anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone 1 only for example the bonnet is open zone 2 and 3 will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone 2 only expect driver door for example 1 door or more door are open zone 1 and 3 will enter the complete anti theft status if the error lock is caused by zone 3 only for example the luggage compartment lid is open zone 1 and 2 will enter the complete anti theft status partial anti theft status to complete anti theft status when each safety zone is in the security system the vehicle will enter the complete anti theft status for partial lock caused by error lock due to open door when the security system enter the anti theft status it can be disabled after being enabled within 5 second and the alarm will not be triggered if the partial anti theft is caused by several zone when the relevant door cover of these zone are closed these zone will enter the complete anti theft status the entire security system enter the complete anti theft status only when all the zone enter the status error lock alarm with the door bonnet luggage compartment lid open and the ignition switch in the acc gear on gear or start gear or the engine running when using the mechanical key or remote key to send the lock request outside the vehicle the bcm will activate the horn to send an acoustic alarm appr 30m to indicate error lock disable security system any of the following operation will cause the bcm to disable the security system valid unlock request from the remote key is received valid unlock request from the driver door switch is received a valid emergency key is inserted into the ignition switch and switched from the acc gear to the on gear 1 0 672 operation the function of the anti theft system is provided by the bcm ecm and module which store and report environment identifier lf anti theft coil when the ignition key is inserted into the ign lock cylinder and the ignition switch is in on position therftransceiverofthekeywillbeswitchedon by the lf anti theft coil of the ign lock cylinder then the rf transceiver transmit a signal which contain its characteristic value bcmthe bcm receive the signal and compare the value with that stored in the memory if the two value match the bcm send a pre cancellation password to the ecm through the serial data circuit if the characteristic value of the rf transceiver is wrong the bcm send fuel disable password to the ecm when ecm receive the pre cancellation password from bcm the ecm will verify the password ecmthe ecm return the password verified to the bcm through the serial data circuit ecmthe ecm and bcm calculate the password if the result calculated by the bcm and ecm is consistent the ecm will allow the vehicle to start 1 0 677 safety and restraint alarm system service procedure anti theft coil remove 1 disconnect the battery negative 2 remove the steering column belt pretensioner connector is a 2 pin connector connected with the body harness in case of collision the seat belt pretensioner is controlled by the signal from the srs ecu the retractor of the two pretensioner are positioned and work in the same way each pretensioner unit is provided with an initiator and a propulsion generator which act on the rotor of the seat belt retractor warning once the pre tensioner has been operated it cannot be reset the gas generator will be exhausted the unit must be replaced seat belt the seat belt provide the most basic protection for all occupant the restraint system also provide further protection for the front occupant through the airbag module and the pretensioning seat belt the front seat are all fitted with appropriate retractor and three point belt the vehicle retractor is a type of emergency locking retractor elr the retractor is provided with an additional lock mechanism which include a webbing sensor and an automotive sensor if the webbing is suddenly pulled the webbing sensor will immediately start the lock mechanism if the vehicle is suddenly slowed down or tilt at a large angle the automotive sensor will start the lock mechanism the retractor will lock in case of impact so the front seat belt retractor is equipped with load limiter if the load limit has exceeded the critical level the load limiter make the seat belt webbing loose so that the load acting on the occupant are reduced front seat belt the retractor of the front seat belt is fitted on the body in the cavity at the bottom of the b c pillar lower trim panel pull the belt webbing from the retractor and fix it to the mounting point under the b c pillar rear seat belt the retractor of the rear seat belt at both side is fixed to the reinforcement panel of the rear wheelhouse inner panel with bolt one end of the webbing is fixed to the upper end of the c pillar the other end is fixed to the mounting point of the body floor the retractor of the central seat belt is fixed to the seat frame with bolt and the other end is fixed to the lower part of the rear seat the right rear seat belt buckle and the central seat belt buckle form an assembly which is fixed by one bolt between the right rear seat and the central rear seat the left rear seat belt buckle is located at the occupant s right hip 1 0 682 seat belt safety and restraint seat belt warning the front seat belt buckle are integrated with the seat belt not tiedwarningswitch whichisconnectedtotheinstrumentpack through srs ecu the seat belt warning will be triggered if the seat belt is not tied firmly during driving the warning message comprise an acoustic alarm and a warning lamp on the instrument panel to prevent unnecessary warning some vehicle specification specify that a pressure sensor shall be fitted within the foam of the passenger seat when there is no passenger on the seat if there is any passenger on the seat but the pressure sensor is not activated then the seat belt warning will not be triggered even if the occupant doe not wear the seat belt 1 0 683 safety and restraint seat belt operation overview when the remote key is inserted the restraint system is activated with the ignition switch in on position the srs warning lamp illuminate for 6 second then goe out this indicate that the system is in normal condition side impact normal operation of the side seat airbag and head curtain airbag depend on whether the severity level of the side impact detected by the side impact sensor exceed the threshold for triggering the side impact sensor when the srs ecu receive a trigger signal from the side impact sensor it will activate the side seat airbag and curtain airbag module the triggered gas generator can produce a large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered theheadairbagwillejectfromtheinteriortrimpanel above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury warning lamp check when the ignition switch is turned on the srs warning lamp shall illuminate then check the lamp if no failure occur in the srs the warning lamp shall go out after about 6 second to monitor the subsequent ignition cycle system check include the state of srs ecu and srs harness system continuity fault with the ignition switch on if any of the following fault occur in the system the srs warning lamp will illuminate and remain on during subsequent ignition cycle ecuand srs failure harness failure ground connection failure open circuit airbag module failure seat belt pretensioner failure if the system detect any failure during driving the warning lamp will also illuminate to indicate failure of the srs system when the warning lamp illuminate the srs will not work when any impact occur withtheignitionswitchon thediagnosisfunctionofthesr ecu will monitor the srs if any fault is detected the srs ecu will store a related dtc in a nonvolatile memory and output a signal to illuminate the srs warning lamp the failure information in the memory can be read with the scan tool low voltage failure when the voltage supply is not within the specified range the malfunction indicator lamp will illuminate the dtc is stored in the memory intermittent failure the warning lamp will illuminate at the moment and remain on in subsequent driving cycle the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory permanent failure for a permanent failure the srs lamp will illuminate when the test is started and it will remain on in every subsequent ignition cycle until the cause of the failure is solved in addition the diagnosis system will also record the information of the failure occurred when any failure is detected the system will retain certain operating function if it is detected that the circuit of the srs outside the srs ecu is faulty srs ecu will still activate the airbag and seat belt pretensioner module when the srs ecu failure or power supply failure is detected the whole system will not work if the circuit of the srs warning lamp is faulty the warning lamp will not illuminate when the ignition switch is turned on for initial check of the warning lamp if there is no other fault in the system all function of the system are available in case of a crash additional information provided by the scan tool for dlc include srs ecudtc version level of hardware and software impact mode state vehicle identification number vin data srs ecuthe srs ecu record fault information in the memory and the information can be read by connecting the scan tool to the dlc system control diagram airbag control diagram a hs can bus b hard wire 1 0 692 supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint description overview the srs can provide passive protection for the passenger when severe impact occur this system is usually based on application of the standard protection system seat belt the srs consist of the following component supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu driver airbag module front passenger airbag module seat side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped side impact sensor if equipped rotary coupler srs warning lamp on the ipk seat belt not tied warning lamp on the ipk srs ecuthe srs ecu connect the vehicle dlc through the hs can bus supplementary restraint system ecu srs ecu srs ecuit determine the affected range of the front impact side impact or rear impact through the internal and external impact sensor if equipped so as to control the srs srs ecuit is fitted on the central passage beneath the centre console which is secured with three screw srs ecuit is connected to the body harness through two connector srs ecuit monitor the vehicle impact through the internal accelerometer and the external sensor srs ecuit can identify the type and severity level of the impact according to these input data and ensure normal operation of the airbag if the severity of the front impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send a signal to activate the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner when fitting the side airbag two side impact sensor shall be fitted to monitor the side impact if the severity of the side impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send a signal to activate the corresponding side airbag module and seat belt pretensioner if the severity of the rear impact exceed the preset trigger value the srs ecu will send a signal to activate the seat belt pretensioner caution it is important that the srs ecu is correctly mounted and is fitted in the designated location and orientation to ensure correct operation ecumonitoring after the vehicle is powered on the srs ecu monitor the preparation of the srs module after start it monitor the system continuously srs ecuthe srs ecu monitor the status of the following module srs ecuinternal accelerometer srs ecumicro processor side impact sensor if equipped driver side airbag module passenger side airbag module side airbag module if equipped seat belt pretensioner if equipped diagnosis count if any system failure is detected the srs warning lamp will illuminate to warn the driver the recommended diagnostic device can be used to check the srs malfunction the diagnostic information can be read include current malfunction frequency of malfunction and duration of each malfunction power supply and backup situation the power supply provide voltage for the srs ecu through a special system fuse in the passenger compartment fuse box in case of power failure check the connection status of the passenger compartment fuse box and the fuse and harness of the srs ecu when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be activated srs ecuthe srs ecu contain a capacitor which can save electricity and ensure the corresponding trigger device and ignitioncircuitcanstillworkwithinaspecifictimewhenregular power supply is cut off by an impact side impact sensor for vehicle with side airbag module the side impact sensor is besidethebodysillframe whichisfixedonthebodybyscrew the sensor is designed with a mounting position for correct refit each sensor include an electronic circuit and a sensing unit the side impact sensor send an acceleration signal to the 1 0 693 safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint ecu and the ecu determine whether to trigger it through calculation caution the side impact crash sensor must be fitted in the correct orientation ensure the fixing screw do not damage the sensor or sensor harness when refitting the front seat driver airbag module 1 airbag cover 2 housing 3 gas generator 4 airbag connector the driver airbag module is fitted on the steering wheel by three bulged pin and one airbag module connector when fitting the driver airbag module push the airbag module toward the steering wheel firmly to embed the wire spring around the airbag module into the opening of the 3 pin the electrical connection between the driver airbag module and the srs ecu is realized by the rotary coupler which is connected to the steering wheel by a connector the driver airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu a folding airbag and a gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly front passenger airbag module warning after replacing the vehicle windscreen the passenger restraint system may not be capable of normal operation for 24 hour warning if a new airbag module show any passenger seat the airbag module is fixed on the instrument panel with bolt the front passenger airbag module is triggered by the control signal from the srs ecu a folding airbag and a gas generator are mounted in the module when severe front impact is detected by the srs ecu it will trigger the gas generator and expand the nylon bag rapidly side airbag if equipped warning if a new airbag module show any sign of damage do not use 1 airbag housing 2 airbag connector 3 bracket the side airbag is outside the seat bracket below the seat upholstery it can protect the rib pelvis and upper visceral connector which is below the seat cushion and connected with the srs ecu through the body harness 1 0 694 supplemental in atable restraint safety and restraint caution do not try to remove the connector at the module end it is a permanent connection 1 0 695 safety and restraint supplemental in atable restraint operation overview when the ignition switch is in on position the srs will be is severe and exceed the threshold set in thesrsecu thefrontairbagmoduleandfrontpretensioning seat belt will work when the accelerometer and front impact sensor in the srs ecu detect any impact the ecu will produce current to trigger the front airbag module thedriversidefrontairbagmoduleisactivatedtoexpand the airbag the passenger side front airbag module is activated to expand the airbag when the front airbag is fully expanded it will release the gas immediately from the passage port and provide the passenger movingforwardwithgradualdeceleration thisreducestherisk level of injury to the occupant srs ecuthe srs ecu can determine the type and severity level of the impact if the front impact detected by the sensor is severe the srs ecu will send an ignition signal to the front airbag module and seat belt pretensioner the triggered gas generator can produce a large quantity of gas to expand the airbag the expanded airbag will eject from the seat upholstery similarly if the head curtain airbag module is triggered the airbag will eject from the interior trim panel above the door to protect the passenger from head injury the fully expanded airbag will release excess gas to reduce the risk of passenger injury front impact with angle when a front impact with angle happen whether the airbag and seat belt pretensioner work depend on the speed and angle of the impact there are several condition the impact is below the threshold of the impact sensor in the srs ecu and no airbag or seat belt pretensioner is activated the speed and angle of impact exceed the triggering threshold of the front impact sensor and the front driver and passenger airbag and seat belt pretensioner are activated side airbag may also be triggered warning lamp check when the ignition switch is and it will be disabled after the failure is eliminated the warning lamp will not illuminate in the next ignition unless the failure occur again but the dtc is still stored in the srs ecu memory diagram at a hs can bus b hard wire 1 0 702 park assistant system safety and restraint description rear obstacle detection if equipped during reversing if the vehicle has to go through obstruction on the road the pdc will send a warning to the driver the system consist of the ultrasonic sensor fitted on the rear bumper and the instrument pack when the vehicle is in reverse gear the ultrasonic sensor is used to monitor the area around the rear bumper if any object is detected within the monitoring area the acoustic alarm device in the instrument pack will send an acoustic alarm the system is able to detect relatively hard solid object and object like wire fence if the rear camera is fitted the image of the rear vehicle will be displayed on the entertainment mainframe display during reversing rear ultrasonic sensor if equipped the ultrasonic sensor are fixed on the rear bumper all the sensor share the same structure with the color matching the vehicle the detection range of the rear ultrasonic sensor involve the area 1200mm away from the back of the bumper the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the distance range from 300mm to 1200mm when the object is 300mm away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm acoustic alarm the acoustic alarm in the instrument pack can inform the driver of the system status by acoustic alarm rear camera if equipped the rear camera is between the two rear license plate lamp under the tail gate which can provide the driver with real time image around the rear of the vehicle and help the reverse operation end view and pin information of harness connector end view and pin information of rear camera connector by025 pin no description 1 signal ground 2 ground 3 video signal ground 2 ground 3 lin2 4 power supply 1 0 704 park assistant system safety and restraint operation overview the system start to run when the ignition switch is in on position after the ultrasonic sensor receive the reverse gear signal it output signal to the acoustic warning device then the acoustic warning device will give an acoustic alarm beep this indicate that the system is activated then it receive the distance information from the ultrasonic sensor and determine whether there is any object in the detection range if there is no object it will not send an acoustic alarm if any object is detected it output signal to the acoustic warning device and control the device to provide a repetitive acoustic alarm from the edge of the detection zone about 1200mm from the rear vehicle the alarm frequency increase with the shortened distance between the object and the vehicle when the object is 300mm away from the vehicle the alarm become consecutive alarm detection zone the table below show the relationship between the alarm level obstacle distance and the acoustic alarm sn alarm level distance mm alarm cycle msec 1 00 no obstacle 1200 2 01 zone 1 0 300 3 02 zone 2 300 450 4 03 zone 3 450 600 5 04 zone 4 600 750 6 05 zone 5 750 900 7 06 zone 6 900 1050 8 07 zone 7 1050 1200 caution blind spot area where the sensor cannot detect obstruction may occur if obstruction are present at distance of around 200 mm from the sensor 1 0 705 safety and restraint park assistant system service procedure ultrasonic sensor remove 1 disconnect the battery negative 2 remove the rear bumper selected for display long press more than 2s the button on the end of the steering lever switch the selected item can be reset total mileage it display the total mileage of the vehicle trip a and trip b it display the mileage since last resetting trip a and trip b are two independent mileage record the display range is within 0 9999km when the mileage exceed 9999km it will reset automatically trip a and trip b can be reset independently so that the vehicle can record the trip separately range to empty it display the approximate mileage without refueling range to empty will be recalculated after refueling the range to empty is calculated by the average fuel consumption and the remaining fuel in the fuel tank of the latest driving record instantaneous fuel consumption instantaneous fuel consumption is calculated by the vehicle automatically when the vehicle is stationary or the vehicle speed is very low the instantaneous fuel consumption display l 100km average fuel consumption connector end view 147 5 v0 1 foreword foreword about this document outline this document is intended to help you diagnose electrical malfunction this document is divided into the following part 1 foreword contain the following content about this document electrical protection abbreviation wire color code wire harness abbreviation wire harness configuration code chart power supply line function connector list malfunction diagnosis and how to use the wiring diagram 2 locator of connector and ground point show the location of each connector on the vehicle through the three dimensional space diagram in addition many connector will be shown in an illustration for convenience 3 fuse description contain detailed description for location rated current and the protection circuit 4 power distribution circuit contain engine bay power distribution and passenger compartment power distribution it is intended to understand the condition of power distribution 5 service circuit contain the repair electrical wiring diagram detailed connector list instruction and connector end view of the section for each system on the vehicle note ensure that the relative mechanical function operate normally before performing the electrical test reference reference to the lh or rh side given in this document is viewed from the rear of the vehicle the operation instruction in this document do not contain the test reference after the vehicle is repaired after all the operation finished it is necessary to perform the inspection test it is especially important while performing the operation related to the safety part caution please refer to the electrical protection before performing the operation related to the electrical equipment battery voltageopen circuit voltage test before starting the electrical malfunction diagnosis check if the battery is available with the open circuit voltage test 1 turn off all the electrical equipment on the vehicle 2 adjust the digital multimeter to the appropriate scale to read the dc voltage 3 try to contact the two pole of the battery with the probe to ensure the two pole are connected correctly and record the voltage displayed when the displayed voltage is 12 3 v or more it mean that the battery is available or the battery need to be charged caution if the vehicle has been used within 8 hour before testing the static on the surface of the battery must be removed by turning on the headlight for about 30 second wait for 60 second before checking the open circuit voltage the battery voltage can be used as a known reference value to determine whether the voltage in the circuit is enough high to operate each component normally this reference value can only be used as a reference because many circuit can operate in a large range of voltage in addition the change of the reading caused by the voltage drop and the voltage fluctuation caused by the length of the wire should be taken into consideration when the voltage passing through certain component foreword 6 v0 1 electrical protection outline the following instruction are intended to ensure the personal safety of the operator when installing the electrical equipment and electronic component equipment ensure that the relative test equipment operate normally and the wire harness and the connector are well connected before performing a vehicle test procedure pay more attention to the condition of the wire and the connector used to operate the equipment caution before starting any work on ignition system make sure the high voltage terminal adapter and diagnostic equipment for testing have been insulated or shielded absolutely to minimize the risk of electric shock in order to prevent personal accident the medicinal pacemaker carrier should be away from the ignition circuit and the diagnostic equipment polarity never connect the two pole of the battery reversely ensure that both pole are connected correctly when connecting them to the test equipment high voltage circuit please use the insulated plier to disconnect the live circuit to prohibit the bare conductor contacting other component especially various ecus pay attention to the ignition coil terminal as it may produce high voltage pulse when the engine is running connector and wire harness it is difficult to install the electronic part and the connector in the engine bay therefore care should be taken ensure that the electronic part and the connector are dry without any oil before disconnecting and connecting the test equipment never use tool or drag the wire harness to disconnect the connector forcibly before disconnecting be sure to release the connector locking tab and reconnect them in the correct direction be sure to replace any protective cover and insulating material immediately once they are damaged refer to the service guide of the repair manual before replacing the defective part be sure to turn the ignition switch off disconnect the battery refer to battery disconnection to avoid unnecessary terminal damage support any wire harness disconnected when replacing the part keep the oily hand away from the electronic connection area and ensure that the connector locking tab are engaged completely battery disconnection turn all the electrical equipment off before disconnecting the battery some data e g time of the clock will be lost when the battery is disconnected caution to prevent damage the electrical equipment alway disconnect the battery when repairing the electrical system on the vehicle when the battery is disconnected the ground terminal must be disconnected first and connected last be sure to connect the wire harness of the battery correctly locate it in place and keep it away from any potential hazard area all the time battery charging ensure good ventilation of the battery top when charging the battery outside the vehicle after charging or discharging the battery for approximately 15 minute the battery will release the hydrogen the gas is combustible 7 v0 1 foreword alway keep the charging area of the battery in good ventilation and prevent the creation of flame and spark rule turn off the ignition system before connecting or disconnecting any part in the system or the electrical component may be damaged by the electromagnetic excitation produced due to disconnection of the live circuit ensure your hand and the working surface have no oil or metal particle etc for example a short connection or high resistance may be caused by the oil when handling the printed circuit board pay attention to the electronic component sensitive to the body static do not remove or install the connector forcibly especially the connector in the board the damaged connection will cause a short or open circuit perform the ground protection periodically before or during the test for example cigar lighter doing so can release the static of the vehicle body as the static may cause some electronic component damaged 1 power supply line function meaning of power supply line function number according to the different state of the power supply on the vehicle the power source can be coded as 15 15a 15c 30 31 50 58 there are 15 stand for the battery power b with the ignition switch in on or st ign 1 15a stand for the battery power b with the ignition switch in on ign 2 15c stand for the battery power b with the ignition switch in on or acc acc 30 stand for connecting the battery power b directly bat 31 stand for ground gnd 58 stand for the battery power b with the headlight switch in 1 and 2 ill illumination the connector are instructed in detail connector information include the following content connector no pin color attribute connector no the number is distributed by the system with a prefix as wire harness id such as bd which stand for body wire harness refer to the wire harness abbreviation list for wire harness id pin stand for the total number of the connector pin caution not all the hole have lead connected color stand for the color of the connector housing attribute stand for the attribute of the connector include male connector female connector and eye connector the connector are usually inserted into the element directly and they commonly have convex pin connector function description usually derive from the connected element connector location usually determine the location of the connector by referring to the connector locator inline connector no optional item fit for the connector inlined with this one connector end view show the outline of the connector with pin no in transmission case wire harness dash panel lh gb003 fc004 1 9 8 18 foreword 14 v0 1 malfunction diagnosis outline when diagnosing the electrical malfunction observe the following step 1 study the wiring diagram carefully to make sure that you are familiar with the wiring diagram related to the malfunction 2 study the power distribution and the fuse and check the relative circuit connected to the fuse or ground check if the circuit are operating normally 3 use the connector layout to confirm the connection point in the circuit perhap between the power supply and the ground point and these place are damaged easily 4 check if the lead out line information and the terminal signal information of the connector are correct 5 check the next connector in the circuit with the above method 6 using the above method check until the malfunction is found and troubleshoot to make sure that the circuit operate normally caution do not contact the face of the connector with probe as it may damage the terminal and cause an incorrect inspection in general the inverse side of the terminal should be detected note do not damage the terminal or seal caution do not detect the insulation part of the wire otherwise the small diameter wire will be disconnected and cause the humidity to enter into the wire and corroded it 15 v0 1 foreword how to use the wiring diagram wire jump page it show that the information mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 introduction thank you for your trust in choosing an s04 with your new s04 you will enjoy a vehicle with the most advanced technology and high quality equipment we recommend that you carefully read this user manual to become familiar with your vehicle and make the most of all its feature in your day to day driving information on handing the vehicle is complemented with instruction for vehicle operation and maintenance this guarantee your safety and preservation of your vehicle s value we also provide you with valuable practical tip and suggestion to drive your vehicle efficiently and in an environmentally friendly way we hope you enjoy your vehicle and alway have a nice trip silence team introduction 3 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04
