5 6 safety safe driving safety first before driving both for your own safety and your passenger s we recommend that you consider the following aspect before you start driving make sure that the light and turn signal are in perfect condition check tyre pressure page 97 check that all window provide good visibility factor bearing on safety as driver you are responsible for your own safety and your passenger s alway remain attentive to traffic and do not become distracted by your passenger or telephone call never drive when your faculty are impaired e g under medication drug alcohol follow traffic rule and speed limit alway adapt your speed to the characteristic of the road as well as weather condition and traffic circumstance on long trip stop regularly to rest at least every two hour whenever possible avoid driving when tired or tense make sure that luggage is properly secured page 72 make sure that no object prevent pedal operation page 16 adjust mirror page 71 adjust the front seat page 72 protect child by using an appropriate child seat and correctly adjusting the seat page 21 sit correctly also advise your passenger to sit correctly adjust your seat belt correctly page 20 tell your passenger to properly buckle their seat belt page 19 warning this chapter contain information of interest on handling the vehicle both for the driver and the passenger in other chapter on on board documentation there is other important information of which the driver and passenger must also be aware of their safety 12 safety mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 correct vehicle occupant position correct seat position safety equipment do not put your or your passenger s safety at risk safety equipment can reduce the risk of injury in the event of an accident the following section include part of your s04 s safety equipment three point seat belt the aforementioned safety equipment s purpose is to protect you and your passenger the best way possible in the event of an accident these safety system will be of no use to you or your passenger if you sit in an incorrect position or use these system inappropriately safety affect all of us warning driving under the effect of alcohol drug medication and narcotic can cause serious accident that can even be lethal alcohol drug medication and narcotic can considerably alter your perception reaction time and safety while driving which could lead to loss of control over the vehicle fig 1 the correct distance between the driver and the wheel must be at least 25 cm 10 inch fig 2 correct seat belt adjustment safety 13 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 as follow we show correct seat position for you and your passenger people who cannot reach a correct seat position due to their physical constitution must ask about possible special device at a specialised shop optimum protection is only granted by the seat belt in a correct position silence recommend that you see technical service for your own safety and to avoid injury in the event of sharp movement or braking or of accident silence recommend the following position valid for all vehicle occupant keep the back of your neck as close as possible to the headrest fig 2 alway keep your foot in the footrest area while driving properly adjust and buckle your seat belt fig 2 the driver must also have their back completely resting against the seat adjust the seat so that the wheel is at least 25 cm 10 inch from the sternum fig 1 and you can hold it with both hand on both side from the outside with your arm slightly bent adjust the seat lengthwise so that you can press the pedal down all the way with your knee slightly bent with a distance between the knee zone and the instrument dashboard of at least 10 cm 4 inch fig 1 alway keep your foot in the footrest area to keep control over the vehicle at all time number of seat the vehicle has 2 seat in the front each seat is equipped with a seat belt warning sitting in an incorrect position in the vehicle can increase the risk of serious or lethal injury in the event of abrupt movement or braking collision or accident before driving all occupant must alway be seated in a correct position and remain in this position during the entire trip the same is true for seat belt placement only transport as many people as seat with seat belt that the vehicle has while driving alway keep your foot in the footrest area never place them for example on top of the seat or instrument dashboard and never put them out the window otherwise not only will the seat belt be unable to offer you any protection but it may also increase the risk of injury in the event of an accident 14 safety mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 risk of sitting in incorrect position if you are not wearing your seat belt or are wearing it improperly this increase the risk of serious or lethal injury seat belt only guarantee optimum protection when worn properly sitting in an incorrect position considerably reduce the protective function of the seat belt consequently you can suffer serious or even lethal injury the risk of serious or lethal injury especially increase if the occupant of the vehicle knock into each other because they are not seated correctly as driver you are responsible for every individual especially child that you transport in your vehicle as follow is an illustrative list of incorrect position that could prove dangerous to vehicle occupant when the vehicle is in movement never stand inside the vehicle never stand on the seat never kneel on the seat never lean on the instrument dashboard never sit just on the front of the seat never sit to the side never lean outside the window never put your foot outside the window never put your foot on the instrument dashboard never put your foot on the seat padding or on the back of the seat never travel in the footrest area never sit on top of the armrest never travel without being seated in the seat with your seat belt on never stay in the boot warning sitting in an incorrect position in the vehicle can increase the risk of serious or lethal injury in the event of accident abrupt movement or braking all occupant must alway be correctly seated while driving and be wearing their seat belt correctly vehicle occupant who are not seated correctly or are not wearing their seat belt run the risk of very serious or lethal injury safety 15 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 warning if you cannot activate the pedal freely critical situation may arise while driving never place rug or any other carpeting over the rug already installed since this reduce the pedal area and can prevent you from using them with the consequent risk of accident never place object in the driver s footrest area they could move into the pedal area and prevent the driver from activating them pedal make sure that you can alway press the brake and accelerator pedal without any problem make sure that the pedal can return to their original position without any problem make sure that the rug are entirely attached so that they do not move while driving or impede pedal operation you may only use rug that leave the pedal area unobstructed and that can be attached to keep them from moving you can purchase suitable rug at a specialised establishment element to attach rug have been placed in the footrest area when one of the brake circuit fail you have to press the brake pedal farther down to stop the vehicle wear suitable footwear wear shoe that properly adjust to your foot and do not impede sensitivity when activating the pedal the control light activate to remind you to buckle your seat belt before driving the vehicle adjust your seat belt correctly tell your passenger to correctly buckle their seat belt before beginning to drive if you exceed a certain speed when you begin driving without buckling your seat belt regardless of the passenger or if the passenger unbuckle while driving you will hear an acoustic signal for a few second the warning light will also blink the light will turn off when with the on button activated you buckle your seatbelt seat belt why wear seat belt control light the driver has not buckled their seat belt light up in red fig 3 pedal location 16 seat belt mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 the protective role of seat belt properly worn seat belt keep occupant in the correct position they also help to avoid uncontrolled movement that can cause serious injury and reduce the danger of flying out of the vehicle in the event of accident vehicle occupant who properly wear their seat belt will largely benefit from the fact that kinetic energy is absorbed by the seat belt the structure of the front part also guarantee absorption of the released kinetic energy this decrease the kinetic energy being released and at the same time the risk of injury this is why you must put your seat belt on before you begin driving the vehicle even for just a short trip also ensure that your passenger has buckled their belt statistic on traffic accident have shown that correctly wearing your seat belt considerably reduce the risk of serious injury and increase the likelihood of surviving in the event of an accident fig 4 driver who wear their seat belt properly will not be thrown from the vehicle in the event of abrupt braking important safety instruction to use seat belt alway wear your seat belt as described in this section ensure that it is possible to wear your seat belt at all time and that the seat belt are not damaged warning if you are not wearing your seat belt or are wearing it improperly this increase the risk of serious or lethal injury seat belt provide maximum protection only if used correctly one same seat belt should never be used by two people at the same time including child never remove your seat belt while the vehicle is in movement otherwise there is risk of death the seat belt should not slide over hard or fragile object glass pen etc since this could produce injury in the event of an accident the seat belt must never be caught damaged or rub against sharp edge never wear your seat belt under your arm or in any other incorrect position seat belt 17 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 frontal accident and law of physic it is easy to explain how the law of physic work in the event of a frontal accident as soon as the vehicle begin to move this create both in the vehicle and in its occupant energy called kinetic energy the magnitude of the kinetic energy mainly depend on the speed weight of the vehicle and weight of the occupant the greater the weight the greater the energy that must be absorbed in the event of an accident however the most important factor is the vehicle s speed for example if speed double from 25 km h 15 mph to 50 km h 30 mph the kinetic energy quadruple given that the vehicle s occupant in our example do not have their seat belt buckled in the event of collision all of the occupant kinetic energy will only be absorbed by this impact even driving at a speed between 30 km h 19 mph and 50 km h 30 mph the force on the body in an accident can easily exceed one tonne 1 000 kg the force on your body thick unbuttoned garment e g a coat over a sweater make it difficult to correctly adjust seat belt so they reduce their protective capacity avoid obstructing the fastener with paper or similar object since the buckle cannot click into place never modify the position of the seat belt with pin for the belt fastener ring or similar object seat belt with damage to the fabric joint automatic roller or fastener can cause serious injury in the event of accident for this reason regularly check the condition of the seat belt after an accident go to a specialised workshop to replace seat belt that have been excessively loosened due to the strain they underwent it may be necessary to replace them even if there is no visible damage moreover you must check the belt s point of attachment never try to repair seat belt on your own seat belt must not be disassembled or modified in any way the belt must be kept clean since if it is very dirty its automatic roller s operation may be jeopardised fig 5 driver not wearing their seat belt will be thrown forward 18 seat belt mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 increase along with the speed at which you drive occupant of the vehicle who have not buckled their seat belt are not united with the vehicle in the event of a frontal collision these individual will move at the same speed as the vehicle before the collision this example is valid not only for frontal collision but also for all kind of accident even when driving at a low speed in the event of collision there are force on the body that cannot be counteracted just by leaning against one s hand in the event of a frontal collision vehicle occupant who are not wearing their seat belt will be launched uncontrollably forward and will crash for example against the wheel the instrument dashboard or the windscreen proper seat belt adjustment buckle and unbuckle your seat belt fig 6 insert the seat belt buckle in the fastener fig 7 release the buckle from the belt fastener when properly placed seat belt keep occupant in the most protected position in the event of abrupt braking or accident buckle your seat belt before each trip fig 6 properly adjust the front seat page 13 pull the belt buckle and place the belt uniformly over your chest and pelvic region do not fold the belt when doing so buckle the belt into the fastener on its seat pull the belt to ensure that the buckle is properly inserted into the fastener unbuckle the seat belt fig 7 only unbuckle the seat belt when the vehicle is stopped press the red button on the fastener the buckle will be released from the fastener accompany the belt with your hand so that the belt roll more easily it seat belt 19 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 proper belt placement fig 8 correctly adjusted seat belt seen from the front and side fig 9 seat belt placement for pregnant woman doe not fold and the covering is not damaged and reduce the risk of serious or lethal injury when properly worn for this reason the seat belt must alway be buckled and you must ensure that the belt is in proper position an improper position in the seat can lead to serious or even lethal injury page 72 the shoulder belt must alway go over the middle of the shoulder never over the neck arm or under the arm or behind the back the lap seat belt must alway go over the pelvic region and never over the abdomen alway keep the belt flat and tight against the body if necessary pull on the seat belt a bit for pregnant woman the seat belt must be uniformly placed against the chest and as low as possible on the pelvic region and kept flat so it doe not press against the stomach it must also be used during the entire pregnancy fig 9 warning the seat belt can only guarantee optimum protection when properly worn according to the occupant s height removing the seat belt while driving can cause serious or lethal injury in the event of accident or abrupt braking the seat belt or a loose seat belt can cause serious injury if the belt move from rigid area of the body to soft area e g to the abdomen seat belt only offer optimum protection in the event of an accident 20 seat belt mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 adapt the belt s placement to the occupant s height warning if the seat belt is improperly placed this can lead to serious or lethal injury in the event of an accident the shoulder belt should go over the middle of the shoulder and never under the arm or over the neck the seat belt must be flat and tight against the upper part of the body the lap seat belt must go over the pelvic region and never over the abdomen the seat belt must be flat and tight against the pelvis if necessary pull on the seat belt a bit for pregnant woman the lap belt must go as low as possible over the pelvic region flat and surrounding the stomach fig 9 do not fold the belt while wearing it once the belt has been properly placed do not pull it away from your body with your hand do not put the belt over rigid or fragile object such as glass pen or key never modify the position of the seat belt with pin for the belt fastener ring or similar object when due to their physical constitution individual cannot reach the optimum seat belt position they must ask at a specialised shop about possible special device to achieve optimum belt protection silence recommend that you see technical service the s04 doe not have backseat for this reason you may carry a child in the passenger seat warning warning transporting child introduction if you need to mount and use a car seat for baby or child consider pertinent legal provision and the assembly instruction of the manufacturer of the car seat for baby or child information along with vehicle documentation we recommend that you alway carry the car seat manufacturer s user manual in the vehicle unlike adult baby and child do not have fully developed musculature or bone structure for this reason they run a greater risk of injury to reduce the risk of injury child may only be transported in chair especially designed for them seat belt 21 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 you must follow applicable law in each country and the manufacturer s regulation for using and mounting car seat classification of car seat in group you must only use approved car seat that are suitable for the child standard ece r 44 or ece r 129 govern these seat ece r mean regulation of the economic commission for europe car seat by weight group car seat are classified into 5 group car seat approved by regulation ece r 44 or ece r 129 have the ece r 44 or ece r 129 approval mark a capital e in a circle and underneath the control number car seat by approval category car seat may be categorised by universal approval semi universal approval specific approval for the vehicle all according to regulation ece r 44 or i size according to regulation ece r 129 universal car seat with universal approval may be mounted in all vehicle it is not necessary to review any list of vehicle make for universal approval for isofix the car seat is additionally provided with a top tether semi universal in addition to standard universal approval requirement semi universal approval require safety device to anchor the car seat that require additional test car seat with semi universal approval include a list of vehicle make in which they may be mounted vehicle specific specific approval for a vehicle require a separate dynamic test for the car seat for each vehicle make car seat with vehicle specific approval also group child s weight group 0 group 0 group 1 group 2 group 3 up to 10 kg up to 13 kg from 9 to 18 kg from 15 to 25 kg from 22 to 36 kg include a list of vehicle make in which they may be mounted i size car seat with i size approval must meet the requirement set forth in regulation ece r 129 in term of assembly and safety car seat manufacturer may indicate which seat have i size approval for this vehicle 22 seat belt mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 baby car seat placement read and follow the baby car seat manufacturer s user manual place the baby car seat in its correct position and take the seat belt from the back rest of the vehicle put the belt through the coloured mark you will see on the baby car seat and buckle the belt to the lower part of the seat ensure that the belt is properly fixated by pulling on it ensuring that the seat doe not move attaching the child car seat with the seat belt place the seat belt and put it through the child car seat following the car seat manufacturer s instruction ensure that the seat belt is not twisted insert the belt buckle into the seat s fastener until you hear it click child must ride with a restraint system that is appropriate for their age weight and height in any event read and consider the information and safety tip for using child car seat warning wheel wheel and tyre general observation if your vehicle has new tyre drive with extreme prudence for the first 500 km if you must drive on kerb or similar do so slowly and if possible with your wheel perpendicular to the obstacle occasionally check whether the tyre are damaged hole cut crack or dent remove all foreign object from the tyre s profile damaged wheel or tyre must be immediately replaced keep the tyre from being soiled by oil grease or fuel if you have lost one of the valve cap replace it as soon as possible mark wheel before dismounting them to keep the same rotation direction when you reassemble them wheel 23 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 keep dismounted wheel or tyre in a cool dry and if possible dark location invisible damage damage to tyre and wheel is often not visible to the naked eye if the vehicle is vibrating unusually or tend to veer to the side this may be a sign of tyre deterioration reduce speed as soon as possible if you suspect that has a wheel has been perforated check whether the tyre are damaged if the outside of the tyre bear no sign of damage keep driving slowly and prudently to the nearest specialised shop so they can check your vehicle foreign object inserted in the tyre do not remove foreign object if they have penetrated the inside of the tyre use the tyre repair kit page 29 to seal the damaged wheel go to a specialised shop for repair or replacement silence recommend going to a silence dealer to do this the tyre tread patching compound envelop the inserted foreign object and provisionally seal the tyre unidirectional tread tyre the side of unidirectional tyre are marked with arrow it is essential that you follow this mandatory rotation direction when mounting the wheel this guarantee best use of the tyre s property in term of aquaplaning adherence noise and wear later accessory assembly if you wish to replace or refit the wheel tyre or hub cap we recommend that you ask an official silence service to consult on existing technical possibility new tyre do not reach optimum adherence until after the first 500 km as such we recommend that you drive with caution to avoid possible accident never drive with damaged tyre there is a risk of having an accident if the vehicle vibrate unusually or tend to veer to the side while driving stop immediately and check the condition of the tyre do not use used tyre when you do not know the circumstance under which they were used before warning new wheel and tyre we recommend that you bring your vehicle to a specialised shop to conduct all work on wheel or tyre they have the necessary knowledge and special tool and appropriate spare part even snow tyre lose adherence with ice if you have put on new tyre drive carefully and moderate your speed during the first 500 km you must alway use the same type 24 wheel mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 only use combination of tyre and wheel as well as wheel bolt which are suitable and approved by silence otherwise the vehicle may be damaged and this can cause an accident for technical reason it is not possible to use wheel from other vehicle in some case not even wheel from the same vehicle make do not exclusively select spare tyre based on nominal size because although they have the same nominal size they can greatly vary depending on the manufacturer lack of clearance can deteriorate the tyre or the vehicle and this can jeopardise road safety risk of accident only use tyre older than 6 year in the event of an emergency and only when driving prudently it is forbidden to mount run flat tyre on your vehicle this forbidden use may cause damage to your vehicle or cause accident warning of tyre of the same size wheel perimeter and if possible tread for all 4 wheel when you replace tyre do not just replace one change at least the two tyre on the same axle if you wish to equip your vehicle with a combination of tyre and wheel other than the one assembled at the factor ask at your specialised shop before purchasing them the dimension of combination of wheel and tyre to use on your vehicle are specified in the vehicle s additional documentation e g eec certificate of conformity the additional documentation in the vehicle is specific to each country date of manufacture the date of manufacture is on the side of the tyre or on the inner side of the wheel dot 2218 mean e g that the tyre was manufactured in week 22 of the year 2018 information used tyre must be disposed of according to applicable regulation wheel 25 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 fig 10 pressure label you must ask at a silence assistance centre about the possibility of mounting wheel or tyre of a size other than the one originally mounted at silence do not use used tyre whose history you do not know warning tyre life cycle to lengthen the life cycle of the tyre they must alway be inflated to the proper pressure and you must drive with moderation check your tyre pressure at least once per month and also before each long trip you must alway check pressure when the tyre are cold do not reduce tyre pressure if they are hot adapt tyre pressure to the load the vehicle is carrying avoid driving too fast on curve and abrupt acceleration occasionally check the tyre for irregular wear tyre pressure inflation pressure is indicated on the adhesive label located on the back of the front left hand door frame fig 10 insufficient or excessive pressure greatly reduce the life cycle of tyre and has a negative influence on how the vehicle behave while driving tyre pressure is extremely important especially when driving at high speed tyre pressure must be adapted to the vehicle s current load if the vehicle is going to be completely loaded you must increase the inflation pressure to the top load number indicated on the adhesive label driving style fast driving on curve and abrupt acceleration and braking squeaking brake increase wheel wear wheel balance the wheel on a new vehicle are balanced however different circumstance during use create imbalance eccentricity which manifest as vibration through the wheel since imbalance also mean greater wear on steering suspension and tyre you must balance the wheel again wheel must also be balanced again after installing a new tyre and after each repair 26 wheel mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 failure in wheel alignment if the undercarriage is improperly adjusted this not only increase wear on tyre but also reduce safety while driving if you observe excessive tyre wear an official silence service shop must check wheel alignment inappropriate handling of tyre and wheel can lead to sudden pressure loss in tyre loss of tread or even exploding tyre the driver is responsible for inflating all the vehicle s tyre to the correct pressure the recommended inflation pressure is indicated on a sticker fig 10 regularly check tyre pressure and alway keep it at the recommended inflation pressure if the tyre s pressure is too low the tyre may overheat causing the tread to come off and even explosion when the tyre are cold they should alway match the pressure on the sticker fig 10 regularly check tyre pressure when tyre are cold if necessary adjust mounted tyre pressure when the tyre are cold regularly check that the tyre show no sign of wear or damage never exceed the speed and maximum load permitted for your vehicle s tyre type warning information if tyre pressure is insufficient it increase energy consumption wear indicator at regular interval and crosswise from the direction the vehicle drive there are wear indicator at the bottom of the original tyre tread that are 1 6 mm high fig 11 the minimum profile depth permitted is reached when the tyre are worn down to the wear indicator replace the tyre with new one fig 11 wear indicator tyre must be replaced at the latest when the wear indicator are worn down otherwise you run the risk of an accident especially when driving under adverse weather condition such as rain and ice it is important that the tread on the tyre be as deep as possible and that it be approximately the same on the tyre on the rear and front axle poor driving safety due to insufficient tread depth is clear especially when driving with the risk of aquaplaning when driving through deep puddle when driving on curve and in braking behaviour if you do not adapt your speed you can lose control of the vehicle warning snow chain after driving for a few metre check that they are properly in place correct their position if necessary by following manufacturer installation instruction remember the maximum speed of 50 km h maximum permitted speed is 10 km h tow with a recovery vehicle the vehicle can be towed both from the front and from the back silence recommend that the vehicle be transported without the wheel touching the road towing the s04 while towing a vehicle driving behaviour and braking capacity considerably vary towing the vehicle with a cable or a tow bar can cause damage to the vehicle if you tow the vehicle with a cable or tow caution caution warning when dismounting and mounting the cover and the tow ring damage can be caused to the vehicle to avoid damaging the vehicle dismount and mount the cover and tow ring with care warning if you push the vehicle with your hand this can damage rear light and large surface of the sheet metal the rear spoiler may also be removed if you must push the vehicle do not press on rear light large sheet metal surface or the rear spoiler bar you must take special care if possible have the vehicle towed with a recovery vehicle if you cannot guarantee that the wheel rotate without any problem or that steering work after an accident request aid from specialised personnel and if necessary transport the vehicle without the wheel touching the road if applicable inform the people involved in the towing process especially the towing company headquarter and driver that this is an electric vehicle tow cable and tow bar the tow bar is safer for towing and prevent damage to the vehicle the tow cable should only be used if a tow bar is unavailable the tow cable should be elastic to prevent damage to the vehicle use a synthetic fibre cable or a similar elastic material attach the tow cable or bar only to the anchor point provided to this end ensure that the tow cable is not twisted otherwise the tow ring may unscrew during towing turn both vehicle and their hazard light on if applicable consider other different provision that may be applicable in this regard to release the handbrake and allow the vehicle to move without driving it charge this is influenced by many factor such as driving style vehicle load kg and proper distribution tyre pressure and unfavourable weather condition such as wind which can reduce it the display provide an estimate of remaining kilometre or mile of autonomy this information is approximate and depend on the driving mode city eco sport and the current amount of power used battery charge indicator in soc in order to read the battery s state of charge soc in with utmost precision you must finish the charging process up to 100 at least after 3 or 4 partial charge good battery charging practice you must follow these recommendation when charging the battery when charging at home place the battery in isolated ventilated area like garage laundry room storage room or patio do not charge the battery in the rain when it is outside the vehicle do not charge in area with traffic or entrance to the home keep the battery far from hot element or that can cause heat such as radiator or stove keep the battery far from mobile element like vehicle or door avoiding all possible blow to the battery keep the battery at a prudent distance from all other element like table cabinet chair and shelve periodically check on the battery while charging good practice for proper battery maintenance the battery s life cycle can last 1 000 cycle with 80 of its capacity if you conduct maintenance as prescribed by the warranty and follow a sery of good practise avoid excessive use of sport mode and attempt to use city mode as much as possible reason avoiding aggressive driving with constant abrupt acceleration avoid completely draining the battery charge the battery when it near 25 of soc thereby avoiding complete cycle and limiting discharge depth keep the battery temperature between 20 30 c and limit time outside this range to the least amount possible when you exceed these temperature the battery deteriorate more easily and lose its property both with high and low temperature whenever possible use the internal charger to charge avoiding constant and stop the vehicle if necessary so it can cool down 80 use mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 battery safety alert never open the management system that control parameter like temperature and voltage it was developed by silence s r d team and play a very important role it balance the charge in the different cell providing top battery performance this system is also responsible for communicating the state of charge and setting charge and discharge current level it also has an action plan in the event of anomalous battery status tripping protection in preventive fashion in the event of excess or defective current voltage temperature etc in short the bms is the battery s brain which work to optimise battery performance can be harmful to your health alway keep maintenance product in a safe place and out of reach of child risk of intoxication warning high pressure cleaning device if you wash the vehicle with a high spray straight at joint inside window door or cover the same hold true for tyre flexible rubber tube soundproofing material sensor etc keep a minimum distance of 40 cm do not remove snow and ice with a high pressure cleaning device never use circular sprayer or mill to remove dirtiness water should be no hotter than 60 c automatic carwash rinse the vehicle before you begin washing ensure that the window are closed and the windscreen wiper are deactivated follow the carwash operator s instruction especially if there are removable part on your vehicle maintenance 91 mu s04 my23 en user manual s04 my23 v001 vehicle give preference to brush free carwash washing by hand clean the vehicle from the top down with a gentle sponge or brush for washing use cleaning product that are solvent free wash vehicle with matte paint by hand to keep from damaging the vehicle while washing it first remove the bulkiest dust and dirtiness to remove insect remain grease stain and fingerprint it is best to use a special cleaner for matte paint apply the product with a microfiber cloth avoid using excessive pressure to keep from damaging the paint surface rinse with plenty of water next clean with a neutral cleaner and a gentle microfiber cloth rinse the vehicle with plenty of water again then allow to air dry remove any remaining water with a leather cloth only wash the vehicle when turned off or according to carwash operator instruction risk of accident if you clean underneath the car or inside the wheel well protect yourself from sharp or cutting metal part risk of cutting yourself after cleaning the brake may take longer to brake due to moisture or in winter to ice on the disc or brake pad risk of accident in this case you must brake several time until the brake dry warning if you wash the vehicle in an automatic carwash you must retract the outside mirror to keep them from being harmed do not wash the vehicle if exposed to intense solar radiation risk of damaging the paint do not use sponge to clean insect remain or kitchen sponge with a rough surface or anything similar risk of damaging the surface vehicle part with matte paint do not use polish or hard wax risk of damaging the surface never select wash programme that include waxing this could ruin the matte paint s appearance do not put sticker or magnet on matte painted part since they can damage the paint your safety and the safety of other are very important we have provided many important safety message in this manual and on your appliance alway read and obey all safety message this is the safety alert symbol this symbol alert you to potential hazard that can kill or hurt you and other all safety message will follow the safety alert symbol and either the word danger or warning these word mean you can be killed or seriously injured if you don t immediately a dan g e r follow instruction you can be killed or seriously injured if you don t follow instruction all safety message will tell you what the potential hazard is tell you how to reduce the chance of injury and tell you what can happen if the instruction are not followed 1 2 mi maytag chopper dishwasher general theory of operation maytag chopper wash system maintenance free chopper system a 4 blade stainless steel chopper pulveriz food preventing particle from plugging the wash system and spray jet providing optimal cleaning without the need to prerinse no more scraping plate the 4 blade stainless steel chopper disintegrate the food particle that come off your dish so they don t end up recirculating and sticking to your dish and glassware m maytag dishwasher feature the most powerful motor on the market so you can be sure what you put in come out clean how chopper work see figure 1 1 2 water and food particle flow through the coarse filter then are disintegrated by the chopper blade a an impeller pull soiled water into the wash pump through the screen b filtering soil particle too large to pass through and sending them back to the chopper blade the wash water is then cycled back through the spray arm c during the drain phase a secondary pump flush out used wash water figure 1 chopper wash system maytag diagnostic fault code and troubleshooting information for the maytag chopper dishwasher diagnostic troubleshooting safety service diagnostic cycle service diagnostic cycle note customer cycle operation service diagnostic with error code service error code troubleshooting guide maytag chopper dishwasher hi 2 1 diagnostic troubleshooting for service technician use only a danger electrical shock hazard electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform disconnect power before servicing diagnostic voltage measurement replace all part and panel before o ting after performing voltage measurement in death disconnect power before servicing vps aga resulting on failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock voltage measurement safety information when performing live voltage measurement you must do the following verify the control are in the off position so that the appliance doe not start when energized allow enough space to perform the voltage measurement without obstruction keep other people a safe distance away from the appliance to prevent potential injury alway use the proper testing equipment after voltage measurement alway disconnect power before servicing important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem are present everywhere most people begin to feel an esd discharge at approximately 3000v it take as little as 10v to destroy damage or weaken the main control assembly the new main control assembly may appear to work well after repair is finished but a malfunction may occur at a later date due to esd stress m use an anti static wrist strap connect wrist strap to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance or touch your finger repeatedly to a green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance before removing the part from its package touch the anti static bag to a green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance avoid touching electronic part or terminal contact handle electronic control assembly by edge only when repackaging main control assembly in anti static bag observe above instruction acceptable in the appliance repair trade any attempt to repair a major appliance may result in personal injury and property damage the manufacturer or seller cannot be responsible nor assume any liability for injury or damage of any kind arising from the use of this data sheet 2 2 hi maytag chopper dishwasher diagnostic troubleshooting for service technician use only aded suimo o uo sajon a perform the following while in standby hm press start resume to wake up the control panel press any 3 key in the sequence 1 2 3 1 2 3 1 2 3 with no more than 1 second between key press m the service diagnostic cycle will start when the door is closed m torapid advance 1 interval at a time press start resume rapid advancing may skip sensor check as some check require 2 complete interval note while in the diagnostic cycle the start resume feature is turned off for example auto resume after door interrupt and the start resume key become an interval advance key h invoking service diagnostic clear all status and last run information from memory and restore default drain and wash motor will pulsate on and off last run cycle and option are returned to default reset owi calibration value to the default value force owi optical water indicator calibration cycle on next customer cycle note calibration cycle may add additional rinse prior to the final rinse to assure clear water and then calibrate the owi during the fill at the beginning of the final rinse mh operating state return to standby upon completing or terminating the service diagnostic cycle m reference service diagnostic with error code section for detail 2 turn on all led immediately upon receiving the entry sequence even if the door is open for 5 second as a display test turn off all led for 1 second prior to reporting customer error history 3 press hi temp in this interval to clear customer error history if the hi temp key doe not respond the control panel is in sleep mode open and close the door to wake up the control panel and then press hi temp to clear error history 4 owi optical soil sensor check hm check owi sensor for the presence of water during the interval 14 and turn on the clean led in interval 13 if water detected hm check owi for the presence of air during drain interval 5 and turn on the clean led in interval 4 if air detected m check owi sense for the presence of bulk soil during pause interval 9 execute apf and turn on clean led in interval 8 if bulk soil detected 2 4 hi maytag chopper dishwasher 5 thermistor temperature sensor check turn clean led on if thermistor is in its normal temperature range 32 f to 167 f 0 c to 75 c turn sanitized led on if fill temperature is above 65 f 18 3 c 6 turn on sanitized led in this interval to indicate that the salt level reed switch is closed 7 turn on clean led in this interval to indicate that vent current is detected customer cycle operation to quickly advance through customer cycle invoke the rapid advance mode by pressing high temp heated dry high temp heated dry after starting the cycle then press start resume to advance through cycle interval note rapid advance mode is automatically enabled in the service diagnostic cycle but must be manually invoked in customer cycle diagnostic troubleshooting for service technician use only service diagnostic with error code entry sequence press start resume to wake maytag chopper dishwasher hi 3 1 testing for service technician use only a danger ef electrical shock hazard electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform disconnect power before servicing diagnostic voltage measurement replace all part and panel before o ting after performing voltage measurement failure to do so can result in death or disconnect power before servicing electrical shock failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock voltage measurement safety information when performing live voltage measurement you must do the following hm verify the control are in the off position so that the appliance doe not start when energized allow enough space to perform the voltage measurement without obstruction keep other people a safe distance away from the appliance to prevent potential injury alway use the proper testing equipment after voltage measurement alway disconnect power before servicing important electrostatic discharge esd sensitive electronic esd problem are present everywhere most people begin to feel an esd discharge at approximately 3000v it take as little as 10v to destroy damage or weaken the main control assembly the new main control assembly may appear to work well after repair is finished but a malfunction may occur at a later date due to esd stress m use an anti static wrist strap connect wrist strap to green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance or touch your finger repeatedly to a green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance before removing the part from its package touch the anti static bag to a green ground connection point or unpainted metal in the appliance avoid touching electronic part or terminal contact handle electronic control assembly by edge only when repackaging main control assembly in anti static bag observe above instruction important safety notice for technician only this service data sheet is intended for use by person having electrical electronic and mechanical experience and knowledge at a level generally considered acceptable in the appliance repair trade any attempt to repair a major appliance may result in personal injury and property damage the manufacturer or seller cannot be responsible nor assume any liability for injury or damage of any kind arising from the use of this data sheet 3 2 hi maytag rotation 12 to 30 rpm 3 4 hi maytag chopper dishwasher fuse service resistance check f9 small triac load fuse check operation of load during the service diagnostic cycle if any of the triac load work f9 fuse is ok if all triac load fail to work f9 fuse could be open see fuse resistance check fuse resistance check 1 unplug the dishwasher or disconnect power 2 measure resistance of f9 fuse note fuse are on the bottom of the control board but can be checked from the top side see control pinout diagram if resistance is 3 q then fuse is ok if resistance is 3 q then fuse is open if the fuse is open inspect and check resistance of all load on fuse if any load are open shorted or have evidence of overheating or pinched wire replace load and or repair wire component testing testing dishwasher component from the control before testing any of the component perform the following check e the most common cause for misdiagnosed control failure is poor connection therefore disconnecting inspecting and reconnecting wire will be necessary throughout test procedure e all test check should be made with a vom or dvm having a sensitivity of 20 000 ohm per volt dc or greater e check all connection before replacing component looking for broken or loose wire failed terminal or wire not pressed into connector far enough e voltage check must be made with all connector attached to the board e resistance check must be made with power cord unplugged or power disconnected and with wiring harness or connector disconnected from the control e the testing procedure in this section may require the use of needle probe to measure voltage failure to use needle probe will damage the connector testing for service technician use only electronic control board control assembly pinch to release rotate control panel snap lock control in place connector brace to remove pinch arm and rotate ds gix button on bottom of control housing slide into keyhole slot on control panel to support the control meter check of load supply important load must be connected for triac to operate correctly meter check best made at the control q of f9 triac fuse ore aos ooo a tttogy oooooogonyo 0 hiore 1 6 1 6 1 5 6 1 3 5 1 2 4 6 1 3 p ps p6 p7 p8 p9 p10 p11 p12 br bu rd vv br rd yl l1 neutral 13v 5v dc gnd maytag chopper dishwasher hf 3 5 testing for service technician use only a danger electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform diagnostic voltage measurement after performing voltage measurement disconnect power before servicing failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock general theory of operation refer to wiring diagram on page 6 3 neutral and l1 ac voltage enter the control board at p4 pin 1 2 respectively ac is converted to dc voltage at the low volt power supply lvp supply include 13 and 5 vdc and dc gnd ref these low voltage supply are used to provide power to the microprocessor and board component control the triac power the sensor buzzer fan motor and energize the ac relay the 13 vdc is vital to the operation of the dishwasher this supply is necessary to operate all 120 vac load in the dishwasher whether they are connected to a relay or controlled by triac 13 vdc is generated by the power supply and flow through the door switch when closed to be available to the heater relay l1 n wash motor relay and the pilot relay a relay coil become energized when the control close the lv drive circuit for a specific relay completing 13v pathway which in turn close the relay switch providing ac to the load the pilot relay provide l1 to the remainder of the component that are controlled by the neutral sensed triac in the chopper dishwasher there is one fuse on the control board f9 small triac load fuse if the triac fuse is open all load controlled by triac will not operate note refer to fuse service and diagnostic check on page 3 4 3 6 hi maytag chopper dishwasher power check this test check for incoming and outgoing power to and from the control board this test assume that proper voltage is present at the outlet or direct connect cable test procedure 1 unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 2 remove access panel 3 remove terminal box cover 4 with a voltmeter set to ac insert black probe inside white wire screw nut n and insert red probe inside black wire screw nut l1 5 plug in dishwasher or reconnect power if 120 vac is present unplug dishwasher or disconnect power and proceed to step 6 if 120 vac is not present have customer correct power problem at outlet or breaker 6 remove outer door panel 7 remove cover from control board and locate connector p4 8 with a voltmeter set to ac connect black probe to p4 pin 1 n and red probe to p4 pin 2 l1 9 plug in dishwasher or reconnect power if 120 vac is present go to step 10 if 120 vac is not present check for open connection between terminal block and control repair as needed 10 verify dc supply e 5 vdc is used to power ic s and micro processor on the circuit board as well as provide power to the sensor and fan motor if5 vdc were missing the fan motor and owi optical water indicator would not function to verify 5v 5 with a voltmeter set to dc connect the black lead to p13 4 dc gnd and the red lead to p11 2 5v e 13 vdc is used to actuate the 120 vac relay and triac on the control if 13 vdc was missing the heater motor and all other load would not turn on to verify 13v 5 witha voltmeter set to dc connect the black lead to p13 4 dc gnd and the red lead to p9 6 13v troubleshooting missing dc supply refer to the wiring diagram on page 3 3 when troubleshooting the dc supply if 5 vdc or 13 vdc is missing on the control unplug dishwasher or disconnect power and then disconnect all component load from the control relying on the missing or loaded supply plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and check if the dc supply has returned if not replace the control ifit has turn off dishwasher and reconnect one connector at a time until the component loading down that supply has been identified 11 unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 12 reassemble all part and panel testing for service technician use only a danger electrical shock hazard only authorized technician should perform diagnostic voltage measurement after performing voltage measurement disconnect power before servicing failure to follow these instruction can result in death or electrical shock door switch circuit perform the following check if the dishwasher doe not detect the door open or closed this test will check the wiring to the door switch and the door switch itself the following item are part of the door switch circuit hm harness connection h door switch latch assembly hb control board test procedure 1 check for improper installation of the dishwasher or leveling check door latch mechanism for obstruction or binding verify door seal is seated properly check for interference between dish rack and door repair as necessary 2 unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 3 remove outer door panel to access door latch and control board strip circuit door switch to heater to wash motor vent and triac load 4 check door switch contact and all connection in the door switch circuit visually check that the p9 connector on the control and the door latch connector are securely installed if visual check pass go to step 5 if any of the connector are not inserted properly reconnect and retest door latch switch 5 disconnect connector p9 from the control board 6 using an ohmmeter measure across p9 pin 5 and 6 with the door closed strike completely in latch mechanism switch closed if 3 ohm or less is measured proceed to step 7 if high resistance is measured when door is closed check for loose connection and repair as needed 7 using an ohmmeter measure across p9 pin 5 and 6 with the door open strike removed from latch mechanism switch open if reading is infinite go to step 8 if reading show continuity or door switch is damaged replace door switch and retest 8 set voltmeter to dc and connect red lead to test pad p9 6 13v and black lead to p13 4 dc gnd on the control board 9 plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and with door open verify that 13 vdc is present across p9 6 and p13 4 if 13 vdcis not present replace the control and retest if 13 vdcis present proceed to step 10 10 reconnect p9 to control board and perform diagnostic cycle to verify operation 11 unplug dishwasher or disconnect power 12 reassemble all part and panel 13 plug in dishwasher or reconnect power to heater to wash motor a sprocket to g1 to decrease tension move spring end left to next groove replace assembly draw take up spring so that it rest on regulator stop b1 fig 14 and recheck tension tighten stud set screw e1 fig 5 to adjust needle thread tension this machine is equipped with a one cycle dial tension which when set correctly offer a barely perceptible tension at o to a maximum at the end of one complete turn of thumb nut at o there should be a barely perceptible amount of tension as otherwise difficulty may be experienced when sewing satin stitching and other form of closed ornamental stitching adjustment pull thread through tension disc to test amount of tension on thread at o position at this point there should be a slight pull on the thread to indicate there is minimum tension which gradually increase with the turning of thumb nut h1 to the right providing a full range of tension with one revolution of the thumb nut if the pull is too strong for a minimum tension press in dial ji to disen gage pin k1 on nut from dial and reset pin in next hole to left of previous setting repeat this adjust ment until a point of no tension is reached then advance one hole to right to give minimum tension at zero position to set needle bar at correct height fig 18 setting needle bar at correct height preparation 1 remove throat plate 2 remove face plate check 1 turn handwheel over toward you until needle bar is at its lowest point the upper timing mark on needle bar should now be in line with the bottom of the needle bar frame upper needle bar bearing 2 continue turning handwheel over toward you until lower timing mark on needle bar is in line with bottom of needle bar frame upper needle bar bearing the shuttle point should now be directly in line with the needle and a short 1 8 above the top of the needle eye setting 1 with needle bar at its lowest point loosen set screw l1 fig 18 and raise or lower needle bar as required 2 wile maintaining correct needle bar height make certain needle bar stay correctly turned i e needle clamp and thumb screw be parallel with the front edge of the bed and securely tighten set screw l1 3 replace throat plate 4 replace face nh hint for adjuster and mechanic check these point when a machine bind sprung or cracked bed or arm incurred during transit bent arm shaft bent needle bar bent take up lever thread take up crank set too tightly misalignment of thread take up lever link hinge stud insufficient thread clearance between heel of shuttle and shuttle driver shuttle jammed with thread or fluff 9 binding or end play in oscillating shaft 10 11 12 13 14 15 feed dog striking end or rubbing on side of throat plate slot feed lifting rock shaft feed rock shaft and oscillating rock shaft center too tight feed bar center too tight bent feed fork insufficient clearance between arm and clamp stop motion flanged bushing burr or damage to bearing surface to run in the machine when a machine is completely assembled and adjusted it should be checked for binding lubricate the machine as instructed on page 3 and 4 then run in tho machine with an electric motor for from 5 to 10 minute at a medium speed or until all moving part run smoothly when machine is turned over by hand running in a machine should be done after every installation of an oscillating shaft and after every general repair index machine 237 page unit is turned on 1 do not look directly into the pickup len wy do not use optical instrument to look at the pickup len do not adjust the preset variable resistor on the optical pickup do not disassemble the optical pickup unit if the optical pickup is replaced use the manufacture specified replacement pickup only use of control or adjustment or performance of procedure other than those specified herin may result in hazardous radiation exposure pap wn hi safety precaution this safety precaution is applied only in u s a before servicing unplug the power cord to prevent an electric shock wy when replacing part use only manufacturer s recommended component for safety check the condition of the power cord replace if wear or damage is evident after servicing be sure to restore the lead dress insulation barrier insulation paper shield etc before returning the serviced equipment to the customer be sure to make the following insulation resistance test to prevent the customer from being exposed to a shock hazard gue gon sf insulation resistance test e 1 unplug the power cord and short the two prong of the plug with a jumper wire 2 turn on the power switch 3 measure the resistance value with ohmmeter between the jumpered ac plug and each exposed metal cabinet ry part such as screwhead antenna control shaft handle bracket etc equipment with antenna terminal should read between 3mq and 5 2mq to all exposed part fig a equipment without antenna terminal should read approximately infinity to all exposed part fig b note some exposed part may be isolated from the chassis by design these will read infinity antenna terminal exposed exposed metal metal part part ohmmeter ohmmeter fig a fig b resistance 3mq2 5 2mq resistance approx oo 4 if the measurement is outside the specified limit there is a possibilty of a shock hazard the equipment should be repaired and rechecked before it is returned to the customer li accessory ac power supply cord polarized 1 concerning the remote control battery installation ww use of battery operation note 1 open cover xe ne 1 open the cover press gently and slide the cover out 2 insert the battery included with this unit when you need to replace these battery use two um 4 aaa iec ro3 1 5 v or equivalent battery do not mix old and new battery or battery of different type carbon and alkaline etc the average battery life is about one year although the battery life vary depending on how often the device is used you should gener ally replace the battery about once a year replace the battery if command from the remote control transmit ter do not operate the unit even when the transmitter is held close to the front panel never subject battery to excessive heat or flame do not attempt to disassemble them and be sure they are not short circuited if you will not be using the remote control for a long period of time remove the battery and store in a cool dark place aim the remote control s transmission window toward the cd player s sensor avoid any obstacle pie saale aay 7 cd player cre 7 meter 23 ft use the remote control with a 60 degree angle of the cd player maximum distance is within 7 meter 23 ft directly facing toward the cd player q insert battery included 3 close cover be sure to insert battery so that their positive and negative polarity match the indication in the compartment batter ies installed incorrectly may leak and cause damage refer to use of battery below for additional information 3 close the cover if a battery leak remove all battery and dispose of them properly thoroughly clean the battery compartment before inserting new bat tery if the leaking electrolyte come in contact with skin or clothe flush with water immediately keep battery out of reach of child do not use rechargeable type nickel cadmium battery do not attempt to recharge alkaline or carbon battery alway remove old weak or worn out battery and dispose of them properly do not dispose of used battery in household rubbish avoid dust be sure the transmission window and the cd player s sensor are free from dust excessive dust might prevent reception if direct sunlight or another strong light source strike the remote control signal sensor part of the cd player it may impair the remote control s operation if there is a problem place the cd player away from the light source if this unit is installed in a rack with glass door the glass door thickness or color might make it necessary to use the remote control transmitter a shorter distance from the unit sl pg440 li basic operation in the traverse deck optical pickup may break down due to potential difference caused by static electricity of clothe or human body so be careful of electrostatic breakdown during repair of the traverse deck optical pickup 1 handling of traverse deck optical pickup do not subject the traverse deck optical pickup to static electricity as it is extremely sensitive to electrical shock variable resistor do not turn optical pickup sl pg440 2 to prevent the breakdown of the laser diode an antistatic shorting pin is inserted into the flexible board fpc board when removing or connecting the short pin finish the job in as short time as possible 3 take care not to apply excessive stress to the flexible board fpc board 4 do not turn the variable resistor laser power adjustment it has already been adjusted len do not touch fpc board handle it carefully be sure to short this position clip shorting pin use the shorting pin or clip e grounding for electrostatic breakdown prevention 1 human body grounding use the anti static wrist strap to discharge the static electricity from your body 2 work table grounding put a conductive material sheet or steel sheet on the area where the optical pickup is placed and wrist strap anti static bracelet ground the sheet caution 1mq the static electricity of your clothe will not be grounded through the wrist strap so take care not to let your clothe touch the traverse deck optical cr sk pickup iron plate or some metal to conduct electricity ell sl pg440 li disassembly instruction 1 remove the 5 screw mea removal of the power switch p and e8 a sl pg440 digital servo system the newly developed digital servo system is adopted in the servo circuit of the unit s cd player instead of the ordinary analog servo system y 1 the diagram shown below represent difference between the analog servo and digital servo system the head amp output signal i e focus error and tracksing error output signal are analog these analog signal are converted to the 8 bit digital signal through the mn66271 the mn66271 perform the following adjustment automatically focus offset tracking offset focus gain tracking gain and tracking balance adjustment the output from the mn66271 such as the focus coil driving signal tracking coil driving signal and traverse motor driving signal are converted to analog signal again and sent to the coil and motor to perform proper servo control for a disc analog servo system nsisesesist 6 vrs require adjustment an8373 to emf demodulation digital audio rf signal d a conv playback f focus coil focus error output signal tracking coil tracking error output signal digital servo system only 1 vrrequire adjustment fe 5 best eye adjustment an8802 teem o 3 f demodulation t digital audio playback focus error output signal focus coil len i aarti traverse coil ania y 7 7 gana microcomputer soft microprogram processing tracking error output signal renewed digital servo circuit the sl pg440 employ a renewed digital servo circuit improved vibration resistance this new circuit has the following improvement over the double floating head mechanism use rubber and the former circuit spring mounting e reduced operation noise fo 50 hz former 20hz new a double stage noise reduction system is employed in e reduced component count the loading mechanism three ics used in the former model were integrated into shortened access time from 2 9sec to 1 9sec a single chip the traverse gear have been improved former model mn6626 mn6650 an8800 new model mn66271 od sl pg440 rt 2 the servo processor ic mn66271 offset 3 focus gain 4 tracking gain and 5 tracking balance therefore you do not have to perform the above mentioned electrical adjustment the unit optimiz the servo for each loaded disc you must
